entry.title: Tool | Claude Code | Cost Optimization: How to Cut Claude Code Spending in Half
entry.content:
Cost Structure Anatomy 任何优化策略的前提是理解成本的构成。
Claude Code 的费用来自 Anthropic API 调用，按 token 计价。截至 2025 年的公开定价：
模型输入（每百万 token）输出（每百万 token）Claude Opus 4$15$75Claude Sonnet 4$3$15Claude Haiku 3.5$0.80$4值得注意的不对称性：输出单价是输入的 5 倍，而 Opus 的输出单价达到了 Sonnet 的 5 倍。这意味着一次让 Opus 生成长篇分析的操作，成本可能相当于 25 次 Sonnet 的等长输入。
单次交互（分析一个文件并修改它）的 token 消耗通常在 10K-50K 之间，费用从几美分到几十美分不等。这个数字本身不吓人。真正的成本陷阱在别处。
上下文膨胀是隐形的费用黑洞。Claude Code 的每一轮对话都将完整的对话历史发送给 API。到第 20 轮时，你发送的一条简短指令实际上附带了前面 19 轮的全部内容。对话越长，每一轮的边际成本越高——这是一条向上弯曲的成本曲线。
养成使用 /cost 命令的习惯。它提供当前会话的累计 token 消耗和费用。持续的费用感知是一切优化行为的起点。
The prerequisite for any optimization strategy is understanding cost composition.
Claude Code costs come from Anthropic API calls, billed per token. Public pricing as of 2025:
ModelInput (per million tokens)Output (per million tokens)Claude Opus 4$15$75Claude Sonnet 4$3$15Claude Haiku 3.5$0.80$4Note the asymmetry: output unit price is 5x input, and Opus output is 5x Sonnet output. This means a single Opus long-analysis generation can cost the equivalent of 25 Sonnet equal-length inputs.
A typical interaction (analyzing and modifying a file) consumes 10K-50K tokens, costing a few cents to a few dozen cents. This figure alone is not alarming. The real cost trap lies elsewhere.
Context inflation is the invisible cost black hole. Each conversation round in Claude Code sends the complete conversation history to the API. By round 20, your brief instruction actually carries all 19 previous rounds of content. The longer the conversation, the higher the marginal cost per round — an upward-curving cost function.
Build the habit of using /cost. It provides cumulative token consumption and cost for the current session. Continuous cost awareness is the starting point for all optimization behavior.
Strategy 1: Context Compression /compact 是 Claude Code 最重要的成本控制工具。它将当前对话历史压缩为一段简短摘要，用摘要替换完整历史。
触发时机的经验判断：
对话超过 10 轮 刚完成一个子任务，即将进入下一个 /cost 显示费用增速明显抬头
一次 /compact 通常能将上下文从数万 token 压缩至数千 token。此后每一轮对话都因此受益——这是一项具有持续复利效应的操作。
Claude Code 也支持自动压缩。可以在设置中配置当上下文达到一定阈值时自动触发 compact（具体配置方式请参考官方文档，因版本更新可能有所调整）。
/compact is Claude Code's most important cost control tool. It compresses current conversation history into a brief summary, replacing the full history with that summary.
Heuristics for when to trigger:
After 10+ conversation rounds Just completed a subtask, about to start the next /cost shows noticeably accelerating costs
A single /compact typically compresses context from tens of thousands of tokens to a few thousand. Every subsequent round benefits — an operation with sustained compound returns.
Claude Code also supports automatic compression. You can configure it to auto-trigger compact when context reaches a certain threshold (refer to official documentation for specific configuration, as it may change across versions).
Strategy 2: Model-Tiered Scheduling 并非所有任务都需要动用最强模型。这个认知偏差是大多数用户超支的首要原因。
通过 /model 命令可以随时切换模型。基于任务复杂度的分级调度策略：
Sonnet 即可胜任的任务： 单文件 bug 修复 代码解释与文档生成 测试代码编写 格式化、重命名等机械操作 翻译任务
Opus 不可替代的任务： 跨多文件的复杂重构 需要全局架构理解的功能开发 深层调用链的调试 需要创造性方案的设计问题
Sonnet 的单价是 Opus 的五分之一。如果 60% 的任务可以分流给 Sonnet，仅此一项策略即可节省近 50% 的总开销。这不是估算——这是简单的算术。
Not every task requires the most powerful model. This cognitive bias is the primary cause of most users' overspending.
Use /model to switch models at any time. A tiered scheduling strategy based on task complexity:
Tasks Sonnet handles well: Single-file bug fixes Code explanations and documentation Test code generation Mechanical operations (formatting, renaming) Translation tasks
Tasks requiring Opus: Complex refactoring across multiple files Feature development requiring global architectural understanding Debugging deep call chains Design problems requiring creative solutions
Sonnet's unit price is one-fifth of Opus. If 60% of tasks can be routed to Sonnet, this strategy alone saves nearly 50% of total spending. This is not an estimate — it is simple arithmetic.
Strategy 3: Conversation Granularity Control 一个大任务不应该在一个连续对话中从头执行到尾。原因已在"上下文膨胀"一节中阐明：对话越长，每一轮的边际成本越高。
更优的策略是将任务分解为独立子任务，每个子任务使用一个新对话（或 /clear 重置后的干净上下文）。
以"为项目增加用户管理模块"为例，可分解为：
对话 1：设计数据库 schema 和 API 接口（Opus，需要架构判断） 对话 2：实现数据库 migration 和 service 层（Sonnet，执行性任务） 对话 3：实现 API 路由（Sonnet） 对话 4：编写测试（Sonnet） 对话 5：集成测试和 bug 修复（Opus，需要跨模块理解）
每个对话从干净上下文启动，规避了膨胀成本。前序对话的设计产出可以直接粘贴到后续对话作为输入——信息传递的成本远低于历史膨胀的成本。
A large task should not execute from start to finish in a single continuous conversation. The reason was articulated in the "context inflation" section: the longer the conversation, the higher the marginal cost per round.
The superior strategy is decomposing tasks into independent subtasks, each using a new conversation (or a clean context after /clear).
Taking "add a user management module" as an example:
Conversation 1: Design database schema and API interfaces (Opus — requires architectural judgment) Conversation 2: Implement database migration and service layer (Sonnet — execution task) Conversation 3: Implement API routes (Sonnet) Conversation 4: Write tests (Sonnet) Conversation 5: Integration testing and bug fixes (Opus — requires cross-module understanding)
Each conversation starts from clean context, avoiding inflation costs. Design outputs from earlier conversations can be pasted directly into later ones — information transfer costs are far lower than history inflation costs.
Strategy 4: Input Precision 模糊指令是 token 的黑洞。一条"帮我优化一下这个项目的性能"会触发 Claude 扫描整个代码库——读取大量不相关的文件、生成冗长的分析报告，消耗数万 token 却可能命中率极低。
对比以下两种输入：
Vague instructions are token black holes. A "help me optimize this project's performance" triggers Claude to scan the entire codebase — reading volumes of irrelevant files, generating verbose analysis reports, consuming tens of thousands of tokens with potentially minimal hit rate.
Compare these two inputs:
# 模糊 / Vague> 帮我优化一下这个项目的性能# 精准 / Precise> src/api/users.ts 里的 getUserList 函数，每次调用都查数据库，> 加一个 Redis 缓存，过期时间 5 分钟
前者的 token 消耗可能是后者的 10 倍以上，而产出的可用性可能更低。
一条实用原则：当你已知目标文件的路径时，在指令中直接声明它。 这一条简单的习惯能消除 Claude 搜索和定位环节的全部开销。
The former's token consumption can be 10x or more than the latter, while its output usability may be lower.
A practical principle: when you know the target file path, declare it directly in your instruction. This simple habit eliminates all overhead from Claude's search and navigation phase.
Strategy 5: Subagent Isolation Claude Code 的 Agent 工具可以派出子代理执行独立任务。子代理拥有自己独立的上下文窗口，其 token 消耗不会回流至主对话的上下文。
这提供了一种精妙的成本控制手段：将需要大量读取操作的调研性工作（如"检查这个代码库的测试框架配置"）委派给子代理，主对话仅接收最终的简洁摘要。调研过程中消耗的数万 token 被隔离在子代理的上下文中，不会导致主对话的上下文膨胀。
在 CLAUDE.md 中可以引导 Claude 在合适的场景下使用子代理：
Claude Code's Agent tool can dispatch subagents for independent tasks. Subagents have their own context windows, and their token consumption does not flow back into the main conversation's context.
This provides an elegant cost control mechanism: delegate read-heavy investigative work (e.g., "examine this codebase's test framework configuration") to subagents, while the main conversation receives only the final concise summary. The tens of thousands of tokens consumed during investigation are isolated in the subagent's context, preventing main conversation inflation.
CLAUDE.md can guide Claude to use subagents in appropriate scenarios:
## Workflow- For codebase exploration tasks, use subagents to avoid context bloat- When modifying 3+ files, analyze each file's current state via subagent first Cost Estimates for Reference 以下数据基于个人使用中的观察，作为量级参考而非精确基准（实际费用因任务复杂度和对话长度有较大波动）：
任务类型未优化（估算）优化后（估算）节省幅度单文件 bug 修复~$0.15 (Opus)~$0.03 (Sonnet)~80%功能开发（中等复杂度）~$2.50~$0.80~68%大型重构（10+ 文件）~$8.00~$3.50~56%代码审查 + 修改~$1.20~$0.40~67%优化手段的组合：模型分流 + 及时 compact + 任务分解 + 精准输入。
The following data is based on personal usage observations, provided as order-of-magnitude references rather than precise benchmarks (actual costs vary significantly with task complexity and conversation length):
Task TypeUnoptimized (est.)Optimized (est.)SavingsSingle-file bug fix~$0.15 (Opus)~$0.03 (Sonnet)~80%Feature development (medium)~$2.50~$0.80~68%Large refactor (10+ files)~$8.00~$3.50~56%Code review + fixes~$1.20~$0.40~67%Optimization mix: model routing + timely compact + task decomposition + precise input.
Core Logic of Cost Control 归结起来就是两条原理：
减少不必要的 token 消耗——compact、精准输入、子代理隔离 用更低单价的模型完成可胜任的任务——模型分级调度
五个策略按投入产出比排序： 模型分级调度（即时生效，效果最显著） 上下文压缩（养成习惯即可） 输入精度（需要刻意训练） 对话粒度控制（大型任务必备） 子代理隔离（进阶技巧）
不必一次全部部署。从模型切换和 compact 起步，这两项即可覆盖绝大部分节省空间。
It reduces to two principles:
Eliminate unnecessary token consumption — compact, precise input, subagent isolation Route tasks to lower-priced models that can handle them — model-tiered scheduling
Five strategies ranked by ROI: Model-tiered scheduling (immediate effect, most significant) Context compression (just build the habit) Input precision (requires deliberate practice) Conversation granularity control (essential for large tasks) Subagent isolation (advanced technique)
No need to deploy all at once. Starting with model switching and compact covers the majority of savings potential.

entry.title: Journal | RSER | Study on how the digital economy affects urban carbon emissions
entry.content: 封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·ECover:ChatGPT-4 DALL·E Abstract: The digital economy is crucial in advancing an economically sustainable and low-carbon future and plays a key role in achieving carbon neutrality and carbon peaking. We measured the digital economy development level of each prefecture-level city in China form 2011to 2019, then investigated the impact of on carbon emissions and its mechanism using the panel fixed effects model and the spatial Durbin model. Our results indicated that: Firstly, an inverted U-shaped relationship had been observed between the impact of digital economy on carbon emissions, which increased and then decreased, and this result was again verified by a threshold regression. Secondly, there was a nonlinear relationship between digital economy and green innovation and the digital economy had an indirect effect on carbon emissions through green innovation. Thirdly, the influence of digital economy on carbon emissions in the area followed an inverted U-shaped relationship that stimulated initially and then restrained, with spatial spillover effects. Fourth, the heterogeneity analysis found that digital economy had a greater emission reduction effect in areas east of the “Hu Huanyong line” and in in cities without resources. The conclusions provide new highlights on the relationship between digital economy and carbon emissions, along with policy recommendations for attaining the objectives of peak carbon and carbon-neutral strategies. 摘要： 数字经济在促进经济可持续发展和低碳未来中至关重要，并在实现碳中和和碳达峰目标中发挥了关键作用。作者测算了2011年至2019年期间中国各地级市的数字经济发展水平，采用面板固定效应模型和空间杜宾模型，研究了数字经济对碳排放的影响及其作用机制。研究结果表明：首先，数字经济对碳排放的影响呈现倒U型关系，即碳排放先增加后减少，这一结果通过门槛回归模型再次得到验证。其次，数字经济与绿色创新之间存在非线性关系，数字经济通过绿色创新对碳排放产生了间接影响。第三，数字经济对该地区碳排放的影响呈现先刺激后抑制的倒U型关系，并具有空间溢出效应。第四，异质性分析发现，数字经济在“胡焕庸线”以东地区和无资源型城市的减排效果更为显著。研究结论为理解数字经济与碳排放的关系提供了新的亮点，并为实现碳达峰和碳中和战略目标提出了政策建议。 引用：Citation: Ma, Z., Xiao, H., Li, J., Chen, H., & Chen, W. (2025). Study on how the digital economy affects urban carbon emissions. Renewable and Sustainable Energy Reviews, 207, 114910. 文献：Paper:

entry.title: Draft | Unusual financial communication: Evidence from ChatGPT, earnings calls, and the stock market
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：ChatGPT的引入改变了人们处理文本数据的方式。本文为ChatGPT设计了一种提示策略（prompting strategy），用于识别和分析财务沟通中的异常之处，重点关注S&P 500公司的电话财报会议（earnings call）。利用最新的GPT-4-Turbo模型，从25个维度对异常财务沟通进行识别和分类，分为四类：高管的异常沟通、财务分析师的异常沟通、异常内容和技术问题。大部分电话财报会议存在异常财务沟通，这与公司的某些特征相关，并随商业周期波动。股市对异常信息反应消极，交易频繁。ChatGPT等大型语言模型具有金融分析潜力，可为解读复杂文本数据及其对市场影响的经济后果提供新的见解。
Abstract: The introduction of ChatGPT has transformed the way people process textual data. This paper designs a prompting strategy for ChatGPT to identify and analyze anomalies in financial communications, focusing on earnings calls of S&P 500 companies. Using the latest GPT-4-Turbo model, unusual financial communications are identified and classified across 25 dimensions into four categories: unusual communication by executives, by financial analysts, unusual contents, and technical issues. Most earnings calls contain unusual financial communications, which correlate with certain firm characteristics and fluctuate with the business cycle. The stock market reacts negatively to unusual information, with elevated trading activity. LLMs such as ChatGPT hold potential for financial analysis, offering new insights into interpreting complex textual data and its economic consequences on the market.
引用：Beckmann, L., Beckmeyer, H., Filippou, I., Menze, S., & Zhou, G. (2024). Unusual Financial Communication-Evidence from ChatGPT, Earnings Calls, and the Stock Market. Earnings Calls, and the Stock Market (January 15, 2024).
Citation: Beckmann, L., Beckmeyer, H., Filippou, I., Menze, S., & Zhou, G. (2024). Unusual Financial Communication-Evidence from ChatGPT, Earnings Calls, and the Stock Market. Earnings Calls, and the Stock Market (January 15, 2024).
文献：
Paper:
Introduction 数据
Data
S&P 500中的公司通常拥有最多的金融分析师关注，且比小公司更快地反映新信息 ($\color{blue}{Zhang,\ 2006}$)。 对于小公司，数据数量和质量大幅降低，价格延迟可能过大，从而模糊了相关的定价渠道 ($\color{blue}{Hou\ and\ Moskowitz,\ 2005}$)。
Firms in the S&P 500 typically enjoy the largest following by financial analysts and reflect novel information sooner than smaller firms ($\color{blue}{Zhang,\ 2006}$). For smaller firms the data quantity and quality are much reduced and the price delay may be too large, obscuring the pricing channel in question ($\color{blue}{Hou\ and\ Moskowitz,\ 2005}$).
贡献
Contribution
为ChatGPT设计合适的提示方法，以识别和理解电话财报会议中的异常之处。 - 采用三步提示策略识别电话财报会议中的异常（完全基于ChatGPT的通用知识，不需要外部定义）。 - 向ChatGPT输入S&P 500公司的电话财报会议数据，输出"正常"或"异常"的结果并给出理由。 识别电话财报会议中的异常财务沟通，并考察其与公司特征、行业归属和宏观经济指标在不同商业周期中的相关性。 - 识别出25个异常维度，归为四大类：高管异常沟通、分析师异常沟通、异常内容和技术问题。 - 具有异常沟通的公司往往规模更大，但利润较低，更有可能成为动量的输家。 - 将异常沟通的不同维度与各种宏观经济指标联系起来。 - 异常沟通的程度随商业周期变化。 第三个贡献：考察市场参与者对异常电话财报会议的反应程度，以及哪些异常维度驱动了这种反应。 - 公司通常在财报公告日获得较高收益 ($\color{blue}{Savor\ and\ Wilson,\ 2016}$)。 - 存在异常沟通的公司收益显著更低，实际上与零无异。 - 该结果在加权和等权条件下均成立。 - 25个维度之间存在较大差异。 - 技术困难与正收益影响（等权）或无收益影响（加权）相关。 - 最大的负收益影响来自分析师缺乏关键性提问（-4.24%）、重复提问（-2.83%）或意外信息的公告（-2.03%）。
Engineer a suitable prompting approach for ChatGPT to identify and understand unusualness in earnings calls. - Apply a three-step prompting strategy to identify unusualness in earnings calls (solely based on ChatGPT's general knowledge, requiring no external definition of unusualness). - Feed S&P 500 earnings call data to ChatGPT, which outputs "usual" or "unusual" classifications with justifications. Identify unusual financial communication in earnings calls and investigate its correlation with firm characteristics, industry affiliation, and macroeconomic indicators across various business cycles. - Identify 25 dimensions of unusualness, classified into four broader categories: unusual communication by executives, by financial analysts, unusual contents, and unusual technical issues. - Firms with unusual communication tend to be larger but less profitable, and more likely to be momentum losers. - Relate the different dimensions of unusual communication to various macroeconomic indicators. - The degree of unusual communication varies with the business cycle. Third, investigate the extent to which market participants react to unusual earnings calls, and which dimensions of unusual communication drive this reaction. - Firms typically earn high returns on earnings announcement dates ($\color{blue}{Savor\ and\ Wilson,\ 2016}$). - Firms with unusual communication earn significantly lower returns, indistinguishable from zero. - This result holds under both value-weighting and equal-weighting. - Large differences exist across the 25 dimensions. - Technical difficulties are related to a positive return impact (equally-weighted) or no return impact (value-weighted). - The largest negative return impact stems from a lack of critical questioning by analysts (-4.24%), repetitive questions (-2.83%), or surprising announcements (-2.03%).
发现
Discoveries
存在异常沟通的公司在多个维度上交易活动显著增加。 - 文献提出交易量作为投资者分歧的衡量指标。 - $\color{blue}{Hong\ and\ Stein\ (2007)}$认为，当投资者拥有不同信息集或新信息导致他们更新信念时，分歧可能产生。 异常沟通对公告收益有显著的负面影响。
Trading activity is significantly elevated for firms with unusual communication along many of the 25 identified dimensions. - The literature has proposed trading volume as a measure for disagreement across investors. - $\color{blue}{Hong\ and\ Stein\ (2007)}$ argue that disagreement may arise when investors possess different information sets or if new information leads them to update their beliefs. Unusual communication has a negative and highly significant announcement return impact.
优势
Advantages
使用同一模型判断特定电话财报会议是否异常，确保可复现性。 模型处理文本信息的速度远快于人类，并结合了训练语料库中的大量信息进行综合推理 ($\color{blue}{de\ Kok,\ 2023}$)。 采用最新的GPT-4-Turbo模型，可在单次提示中处理多达128000个tokens的文本。
Rely on the same model for all classification decisions, ensuring reproducibility. The model processes textual information significantly faster than humans and combines aggregate reasoning from its training corpus ($\color{blue}{de\ Kok,\ 2023}$). Utilize the most recent GPT-4-Turbo, which can process texts of up to 128,000 tokens in a single prompt.
MethodologyData Description 对象：S&P 500公司 - 演示部分（Presentation） - 问答环节（Q&A session）※ - .txt文件 - 包含电话财报会议的日期和时间、报告季度、公司名称和代码 - 仅向ChatGPT输入问答环节内容 数据来源：Refinitiv 样本期间：2015.01 - 2022.12
Object: Firms in the S&P 500 - Presentation - Q&A session※ - .txt files - The date and time of the earnings call, reporting quarter, company name and ticker - Feed ChatGPT with the Q&A session only Data source: Refinitiv Sample period: 2015.01 - 2022.12
访问ChatGPT：通过应用程序编程接口（API）访问GPT-4-Turbo。
Accessing ChatGPT: Access GPT-4-Turbo via its application programming interface (API).
GPT-4的升级版本 2023年11月6日发布 Token上限提升至128000
An updated version of GPT-4 Released on November 6, 2023 Token limit increased to 128,000
Prompt Engineering
三步提示方法（判断电话财报会议是否异常以及异常特征）：
Three-step prompting approach (determine whether an earnings call is unusual and characterize the unusualness):
第一步，随机向ChatGPT提供2015年至2023年间1000份问答会话记录的样本，并要求该模型判断某个问答会是否异常，如果存在异常则提供文字说明。
Step 1: Randomly provide ChatGPT with a sample of 1,000 Q&A session transcripts from 2015-2023, asking the model to determine whether a given Q&A session is unusual and, if so, provide a textual justification.
Prompt 1: Please read the following transcript of a Question-and-Answer session from the earn-ings conference call of company {firm} ({ticker}) carefully. Determine whether the Question-and-Answer session of this earnings conference call is 'usual' or 'unusual': If the Question-and-Answer session is classified as 'usual', state 'usual' without any justifications or further output. If the Question-and-Answer session is classified as 'unusual', state 'unusual' and provide a justification for this classification. Transcript of the Question-and-Answer Session: '{qa}'.
第二步，将异常结果系统化。收集第一步中的异常理由，并提供给新prompt，令ChatGPT从中归纳出高层次类别。
Step 2: Systematize the unusual results. Collect the justifications from Step 1 and provide them to a new prompt, asking ChatGPT to derive high-level categories.
Prompt 2: Please read the provided text file with justifications for unusual Q&A sessions from earnings conference calls carefully. What are high-level categories to identify unusual Q&A ses-sions? Make sure that each statement from the text file can be assigned to one of the categories.
第三步，检查以上维度的所有异常问答。
Step 3: Examine all Q&A sessions across the identified dimensions.
Prompt 3: Please read the following transcript of a Question-and-Answer session from the earnings conference call of company {firm} (ticker) carefully. Determine whether the Question-and-Answer session of this earnings conference call is 'usual' or 'unusual' in the following {len(categories)} categories: {categories}For each category, state whether the Question-and-Answer session is 'usual' or 'unusual'. If the Question-and-Answer session is classified as 'usual' in the respective category, state 'usual' without any justifications or further output. If the Question-and-Answer session is classified as 'unusual' in the respective category, state 'unusual', print a '/', and provide a justification for this classification. Transcript of the Question-and-Answer Session: '{qa}'
- 以特斯拉2018年第一季度电话财报会议中的异常沟通为例。
- Example: unusual communication in Tesla's Q1 2018 earnings call.
表1概括了异常沟通维度，ChatGPT提供25个high-level categories，且降维至四个维度： - 管理层沟通异常：指的是公司管理层在回答分析师提问时表现出的异常行为，如回答过于冗长、情绪化，或者含糊其辞、逃避问题，甚至出现自相矛盾的情况，显露出管理团队可能没有充分准备； - 分析师互动异常：包括分析师提出的问题离题或重复、问题数量异常多、反复关注特定参与者或议题、频繁询问与财务无关的问题，以及缺少对关键问题的探讨； - 沟通内容异常：涵盖公司变革、领导层变动、战略洞察、法律与监管议题讨论、运营和管理问题、对其市场行为的分析、对特定（财务或非财务）主题的详细讨论、外部事件的影响、宏观经济因素和公告等，以及对公司产品或服务的深入讨论； - 技术难题：识别在沟通过程中遇到的技术执行或操作问题，可能会影响到信息或数据传输的准确性。
Table 1 summarizes the dimensions of unusual communication. ChatGPT identifies 25 high-level categories, reduced to four broader dimensions: - Unusual executive communication: Abnormal behavior by management when responding to analyst questions, such as overly lengthy or emotional responses, vague or evasive answers, contradictions, or signs of insufficient preparation. - Unusual analyst interaction: Off-topic or repetitive questions, an unusual number of questions, repeated focus on specific participants or topics, frequent non-financial inquiries, and a lack of critical questioning. - Unusual content: Corporate changes, leadership transitions, strategic insights, legal and regulatory discussions, operational and management issues, market behavior analysis, detailed discussions of specific (financial or non-financial) topics, external event impacts, macroeconomic factors and announcements, and in-depth product/service discussions. - Technical issues: Technical execution or operational problems during the call that may affect the accuracy of information or data transmission.
Advantages of ChatGPT 可复现性； 解释文本信息速度更快、结合了更多信息； 优于人工方法或者LM词典；
Reproducibility; Faster interpretation of textual information with broader information synthesis; Superior to manual methods or LM dictionaries;
Identifying Unusual CommunicationFrequency of Unusual Communication 表1展示了异常沟通频率。
Table 1 presents the frequency of unusual communication.
表2表明在某一维度上存在异常沟通的公司，在同一类的另一维度上也存在异常沟通可能性有多大。
Table 2 shows the probability that a firm with unusual communication in one dimension also exhibits unusual communication in another dimension within the same category.
表3说明在某一类别（高管）的至少一个维度上存在异常交流的公司，在另一类别（分析师）上也出现异常交流迹象的频率。
Table 3 reports how frequently a firm with unusual communication in at least one dimension of one category (e.g., executives) also shows unusual communication in another category (e.g., analysts).
- 金融分析师在电话财报会议讨论中对设定讨论基调起重要作用。
- Financial analysts are important in setting the tone of earnings call discussions.
表1至表3可得：异常沟通模式存在较大的异质性。
Tables 1-3 reveal substantial heterogeneity in unusual communication patterns.
Unusual Firms 表4为异常沟通与公司特征的关系。
Table 4 examines the relationship between unusual communication and firm characteristics.
- 表4整体介绍 - 通过比较在4类别的25个维度，查看存在异常沟通与否，公司在财务比率和公司特征方面是否存在系统性差异，进而分析特定公司是否更容易出现异常沟通； - 考虑如下因素：市值（Size）、账面市值比（B2M）、投资行为（Inv）、盈利能力（Prof）、动量（Mom）、21天最大收益率（MAX）、流动性（Illiq）和名义股价（PRC）； - Prof（-2%）：即存在异常沟通公司的盈利能力平均低2%； - 高管异常沟通 - 存在高管异常沟通的公司盈利能力（Prof）明显较低、平均规模（Size）略大，股票流动性（Illiq）略高； - 高管异常沟通的不同维度存较大异质性； - 分析师异常沟通 - 公司市值（Size）较大、投资额（Inv）略高但盈利能力（Prof）较低、$\color{red}{股票流动性（Illiq）较强}$、交易价格（PRC）较高有关； - 技术难题 - 与公司特征无关； - 以上说明：ChatGPT可以从电话财报会议记录中识别出异常沟通对应的意义。 - 沟通内容异常 - 股票平均规模（Size）较大，盈利能力（Prof）较差 ，动量（Mom）输家、股票流动性（Illiq）更强，交易的名义价格（PRC）更高。 - 讨论法律问题（Legal）的公司往往是价值股，账面市值比高； - 宣布意外消息（Surprise）和公司或管理层变动（Changes）的公司更有可能是动量（Mom）输家，表明其以往股票收益率已低于同行； - 存在Legal、Surprise和Changes等维度异常沟通的公司盈利能力（Prof）较差；
- Overview of Table 4 - Compare firms with and without unusual communication across 25 dimensions in 4 categories to determine whether systematic differences exist in financial ratios and firm characteristics, thereby analyzing whether certain firms are more prone to unusual communication; - Factors considered: market capitalization (Size), book-to-market ratio (B2M), investment (Inv), profitability (Prof), momentum (Mom), 21-day maximum return (MAX), illiquidity (Illiq), and nominal share price (PRC); - Prof (-2%): firms with unusual communication have 2% lower profitability on average; - Unusual executive communication - Firms with unusual executive communication have notably lower profitability (Prof), slightly larger average size (Size), and slightly higher stock liquidity (Illiq); - Substantial heterogeneity across dimensions of executive unusual communication; - Unusual analyst communication - Associated with larger market capitalization (Size), slightly higher investment (Inv) but lower profitability (Prof), $\color{red}{stronger\ stock\ liquidity\ (Illiq)}$, and higher trading price (PRC); - Technical issues - Unrelated to firm characteristics; - These findings confirm that ChatGPT can identify meaningful unusual communication from earnings call transcripts. - Unusual content - Larger average size (Size), lower profitability (Prof), momentum (Mom) losers, stronger stock liquidity (Illiq), and higher nominal trading price (PRC). - Firms discussing legal issues (Legal) tend to be value stocks with high book-to-market ratios; - Firms announcing surprises (Surprise) or corporate/management changes (Changes) are more likely to be momentum (Mom) losers, indicating prior stock underperformance relative to peers; - Firms with unusual communication in the Legal, Surprise, and Changes dimensions have lower profitability (Prof);
表5展示了不同行业的企业是否在异常沟通倾向方面存在系统性差异。
Table 5 examines whether firms in different industries exhibit systematic differences in the propensity for unusual communication.
- 步骤：收集样本中各公司的SIC，根据$\color{blue}{Kenneth\ French}$的定义将其归入12个行业之一； - 异常沟通与公司行业归属基本无关，即异常沟通并非某些行业独有；
- Procedure: collect SIC codes for each firm and classify them into one of 12 industries following $\color{blue}{Kenneth\ French}$; - Unusual communication is largely unrelated to industry affiliation, i.e., it is not specific to certain industries;
Periods of Unusual Communication 图1显示了随时间推移，外部事件影响（External Events Impact）、运营和管理问题（Operational and Management Issues）以及意外公告（Surprising Announcements）等三方面异常沟通的相对发生率；
Figure 1 displays the relative incidence over time of unusual communication in three dimensions: External Events Impact, Operational and Management Issues, and Surprising Announcements;
- 外部事件影响（External Events Impact） - 2018-2019年约为10%，在2020年Q1达到峰值，超过25%（即四分之一的企业讨论了外部事件的影响，主要是Covid crisis）； - 【Interestingly, however, ChatGPT considers the communication style with this regard of only 1/4 of all firms as unusual.】 - 2022年初再次达到峰值（俄乌战争）； - 运营和管理问题（Operational and Management Issues） - 平稳 - 意外公告（Surprising Announcements） - 2018年、2021年Q1达到高峰；
- External Events Impact - Approximately 10% during 2018-2019, peaking above 25% in Q1 2020 (a quarter of all firms discussed external event impacts, primarily the Covid crisis); - Interestingly, ChatGPT considers the communication style of only 1/4 of all firms as unusual in this regard. - A second peak occurred in early 2022 (Russia-Ukraine war); - Operational and Management Issues - Remained stable over time; - Surprising Announcements - Peaked in 2018 and Q1 2021;
表6揭示了异常沟通与商业周期的关系（单变量回归）。
Table 6 reveals the relationship between unusual communication and the business cycle (univariate regressions).
- 宏观经济指标 - 标准普尔 500 指数的市盈率（the price-dividend ratio of the S&P 500）、股票净发行量（net share issuance）、当前国库券利率（the current Treasury bill rate）、期限（term）和违约利差（default spread）($\color{blue}{Welch\ and\ Goyal,\ 2008}$)； - VIX； - intermediary capital ratio (ICR) ($\color{blue}{He\ et\ al.,\ 2017}$)； - 芝加哥联储全国活动指数 (Chicago Fed National Activity Index, $\color{blue}{CFNAI}$)；
- Macroeconomic indicators - The price-dividend ratio of the S&P 500, net share issuance, the current Treasury bill rate, term spread, and default spread ($\color{blue}{Welch\ and\ Goyal,\ 2008}$); - VIX; - Intermediary capital ratio (ICR) ($\color{blue}{He\ et\ al.,\ 2017}$); - Chicago Fed National Activity Index ($\color{blue}{CFNAI}$);
- 结果分析
- Results analysis
Stock Market Reaction to Unusual Communication 计算步骤 - 采用每日股价，计算事件发生日$t$与前后一天（$t-1$、$t+1$）时间窗口内累计收益率； - 分别计算4类、25维度异常沟通的等权、加权收益率；
Computation procedure - Using daily stock prices, compute cumulative returns over the event window of day $t$ and adjacent days ($t-1$, $t+1$); - Calculate equal-weighted and value-weighted returns for each of the 4 categories and 25 dimensions of unusual communication;
表7表明了异常沟通与公告收益的关系。
Table 7 documents the relationship between unusual communication and announcement returns.
- 异常沟通与较低的公告收益率相关，正常沟通收益为正 ($\color{blue}{Savor\ and\ Wilson,\ 2016}$)，异常沟通收益率无异于零; - 公司高管异常沟通与显著较小的公告收益有关（等权、加权均适用）； - 分析师异常互动与负收益显著相关； - 异常内容产生显著较小的公告收益；
- Unusual communication is associated with lower announcement returns; usual communication yields positive returns ($\color{blue}{Savor\ and\ Wilson,\ 2016}$), while unusual communication returns are indistinguishable from zero; - Unusual executive communication is associated with significantly smaller announcement returns (both equal-weighted and value-weighted); - Unusual analyst interaction is significantly associated with negative returns; - Unusual content produces significantly smaller announcement returns;
- 更换计算累计异常收益方法：$\color{blue}{Fama\ and\ French\ (1993)}$
- Alternative cumulative abnormal return method: $\color{blue}{Fama\ and\ French\ (1993)}$
<img src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/zhichonglyu/blogimg/img/202403030235406.png" style="zoom:80%;" />![Figure](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/zhichonglyu/blogimg/img/202403030238889.png)
<img src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/zhichonglyu/blogimg/img/202403030238889.png" />
<img src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/zhichonglyu/blogimg/img/202403030240311.png" style="zoom:80%;" />
表8表明异常沟通导致交易频繁。
Table 8 shows that unusual communication leads to elevated trading activity.
- 异常沟通交易量显著增加； - 分析师对特定主题或参与者的反复关注，以及重复提问和大量提问，均导致交易量显著增加； - 技术困难不会使交易量增加，即异常沟通交易量增加是由于投资者对信息存在分歧；
- Trading volume increases significantly with unusual communication; - Analysts' repeated focus on specific topics or participants, repetitive questions, and a high volume of questions all lead to significantly increased trading volume; - Technical difficulties do not increase trading volume, suggesting that the elevated trading activity from unusual communication is driven by investor disagreement;
表9面板回归。
Table 9 presents panel regression results.
- 异常沟通导致公告收益下降35个基点； - 技术困难对收益率存在正向影响； - 异常沟通与异常内容都和公告收益率大幅下降相关； - 加入SIC行业固定效应，各类异常沟通系数几乎不变。
- Unusual communication reduces announcement returns by 35 basis points; - Technical difficulties have a positive impact on returns; - Both unusual communication and unusual content are associated with substantial declines in announcement returns; - Adding SIC industry fixed effects leaves the coefficients for all categories of unusual communication virtually unchanged.

entry.title: Tool | Claude Code | From Terminal to Productivity: A Complete Guide to Claude Code
entry.content:
A Counterintuitive Product Decision 当市面上每一款 AI 编程工具都在争夺 IDE 的地盘——Cursor 做了一整个编辑器，Copilot 嵌进了 VSCode 的骨髓，Windsurf 也在走同样的路——Anthropic 的 Claude Code 选择了一个看似荒谬的方向：纯终端。
没有语法高亮，没有文件树，没有侧边栏。一个黑底白字的命令行界面。
这不是倒退，而是一个精确的设计取舍。终端是 Unix 哲学的原生载体：可组合、可脚本化、可嵌入 CI/CD pipeline。当你的 AI 助手栖息在终端中，它天然就获得了对操作系统的完整访问权限——文件系统、shell 命令、git 操作、环境变量。它不再是一个在侧边栏里提供建议的顾问，而是一个能亲手执行任务的代理（Agent）。
这个区别是本质性的。
While every AI coding tool competes for IDE territory — Cursor built an entire editor, Copilot embedded itself in VSCode's core, Windsurf follows the same path — Anthropic's Claude Code chose a seemingly absurd direction: the bare terminal.
No syntax highlighting, no file tree, no sidebar. A black-and-white command-line interface.
This is not regression but a precise design tradeoff. The terminal is the native carrier of Unix philosophy: composable, scriptable, embeddable in CI/CD pipelines. When your AI assistant inhabits the terminal, it gains full operating system access by nature — filesystem, shell commands, git operations, environment variables. It ceases to be an advisor offering suggestions in a sidebar and becomes an agent capable of executing tasks firsthand.
This distinction is fundamental.
Installation 前提条件：Node.js 18+。
Prerequisite: Node.js 18+.
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
安装完成后，进入你的项目目录运行：
After installation, navigate to your project directory and run:
cd your-projectclaude
首次启动需要登录 Anthropic 账户或配置 API key。选择 API key 的用户设置环境变量即可：
First launch requires an Anthropic account login or API key configuration. For API key users, set the environment variable:
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-xxxxx
Windows 用户建议将此行写入 shell 配置文件（如 .bashrc），免去每次手动设置。
Windows users should add this line to their shell profile (e.g., .bashrc) to avoid manual setup each time.
Core Commands 以下命令构成日常操作的基本词汇表：
命令功能/help列出所有可用命令/cost查看当前会话的 token 消耗与费用/compact压缩对话历史，释放上下文空间/model切换模型（Opus / Sonnet / Haiku）/clear清空当前对话，重置上下文Esc取消正在编辑的输入Ctrl+C中断当前操作或退出其中 /cost 和 /compact 是使用频率最高的两个。前者提供实时的费用感知，后者在对话变长时压缩上下文以遏制 token 膨胀。两者的具体用法将在本系列"省钱篇"中展开。
The following commands form the essential vocabulary for daily operations:
CommandFunction/helpList all available commands/costView token consumption and cost for the current session/compactCompress conversation history to free context space/modelSwitch models (Opus / Sonnet / Haiku)/clearClear the current conversation and reset contextEscCancel the input being editedCtrl+CInterrupt current operation or exit/cost and /compact see the highest usage frequency. The former provides real-time cost awareness; the latter compresses context as conversations grow to curb token inflation. Detailed usage for both is covered in the "cost optimization" post in this series.
Permission Model Claude Code 提供三级权限控制，本质上是"自主性"和"安全性"之间的滑动标尺：
1. 交互确认模式（默认）
每次文件编辑、命令执行等操作都需要用户逐一审批。安全性最高，但频繁的确认提示会打断工作节奏。适合初次使用或处理敏感项目。
2. 自动编辑模式
文件读写操作自动执行，shell 命令仍需确认。这是效率与安全之间的帕累托最优点。启动时指定允许的工具集：
Claude Code offers three tiers of permission control — essentially a sliding scale between autonomy and safety:
1. Interactive Confirmation (Default)
Every file edit, command execution, and similar operation requires individual user approval. Maximum safety, but frequent confirmation prompts interrupt workflow. Best for initial use or sensitive projects.
2. Auto-Edit Mode
File read/write operations execute automatically; shell commands still require confirmation. This is the Pareto-optimal point between efficiency and safety. Specify allowed tools at launch:
claude --allowedTools "Edit,Write,Read,Glob,Grep"
3. 全自动模式
所有操作无需确认直接执行。仅建议在以下条件同时满足时使用：任务边界清晰、项目处于 git 版本控制之下、你对当前操作有充分预期。
3. Full Autonomy Mode
All operations execute without confirmation. Only recommended when the following conditions are simultaneously met: the task boundary is clear, the project is under git version control, and you have full expectation of the operations involved.
claude --dangerouslySkipPermissions
命令名本身就是一句警告。日常开发中，自动编辑模式足以覆盖绝大多数场景。
The command name itself serves as a warning. For daily development, auto-edit mode covers the vast majority of scenarios.
Four Core Usage Paradigms 配置就绪之后，Claude Code 的价值体现在四类典型任务上。
范式一：代码库理解
接手陌生项目时，与其花半天翻目录结构，不如直接提问：
With configuration complete, Claude Code's value manifests across four categories of typical tasks.
Paradigm 1: Codebase Comprehension
When inheriting an unfamiliar project, rather than spending half a day navigating directory structures, ask directly:
> 这个项目的整体架构是什么？入口文件在哪？> Explain the authentication flow in this codebase> 找到所有处理支付逻辑的文件
Claude 会自主搜索文件、追踪依赖、分析调用关系，最终给出结构化的架构概览。这个过程对人来说可能需要数小时，对 Claude 来说通常在一分钟以内。
范式二：定向修复
将报错信息直接作为输入：
Claude autonomously searches files, traces dependencies, and analyzes call relationships, ultimately delivering a structured architectural overview. What might take a human hours typically takes Claude under a minute.
Paradigm 2: Targeted Fixes
Feed error messages directly as input:
> 运行 npm test 报错：TypeError: Cannot read property 'map' of undefined> 在 src/utils/parser.ts 第 42 行，帮我看看什么问题
Claude 不只是诊断问题——它会定位代码、分析根因、直接修改文件。从"建议你怎么改"到"已经帮你改好了"，这是 AI 编程工具从顾问到代理的质变。
范式三：功能实现
Claude does not merely diagnose — it locates the code, analyzes root causes, and modifies the files directly. The leap from "suggesting what to change" to "having already changed it" marks the qualitative shift of AI coding tools from advisor to agent.
Paradigm 3: Feature Implementation
> 给这个 Express 应用加一个 /api/health 端点，返回服务状态和启动时间> 在 User model 里加一个 lastLoginAt 字段，并在登录逻辑里更新它
这是 Claude Code 投入产出比最高的场景。它会读取现有代码以理解项目规范，编写符合规范的新代码，并同步更新相关的测试文件。你的角色从"编码者"转变为"需求定义者与审查者"。
范式四：批量重构
This is Claude Code's highest-ROI scenario. It reads existing code to understand project conventions, writes convention-compliant new code, and synchronously updates related test files. Your role shifts from "coder" to "requirements definer and reviewer."
Paradigm 4: Batch Refactoring
> 把 src/utils/ 下所有的 callback 风格函数改成 async/await> 把这个 class component 重构成 functional component with hooks
批量重构对人类而言是高认知负荷、低创造性的苦差事——恰好是 AI 代理最适合接管的任务类型。Claude Code 能在保持代码风格一致的前提下完成全量修改，且不会遗漏。
Batch refactoring imposes high cognitive load with low creativity on humans — precisely the task type best suited for AI agent takeover. Claude Code completes full-scope modifications while maintaining code style consistency, with no omissions.
CLAUDE.md: The Prerequisite for All Efficiency 在项目根目录放置一个 CLAUDE.md 文件。Claude Code 每次启动时自动读取它，将其作为项目上下文注入对话。这份文件的质量直接决定了 Claude 的输出质量——它是否了解你的技术栈、代码规范、目录结构、禁忌操作。
CLAUDE.md 的写法值得独立成篇。本系列的下一篇将专门探讨这个话题。
Place a CLAUDE.md file in your project root. Claude Code reads it automatically on every launch, injecting it as project context into the conversation. The quality of this file directly determines Claude's output quality — whether it understands your tech stack, coding conventions, directory structure, and forbidden operations.
Writing CLAUDE.md deserves its own article. The next post in this series addresses this topic in depth.
An Honest Statement of Limitations Claude Code 不适合以下场景：需要深层领域知识的架构决策、对业务逻辑正确性有严格要求的核心路径、需要实时 UI 反馈的前端开发。它的优势区间非常明确——代码理解、生成、修改和命令执行——而在这个区间内，它是当前最高效的工具形态。
终端界面起初可能令人不适。但当你适应了"用自然语言操控操作系统"这一交互范式之后，回到手动操作会显得格外笨拙。
Claude Code is unsuited for: architecture decisions requiring deep domain knowledge, critical paths with strict business logic correctness requirements, and frontend development needing real-time UI feedback. Its advantage zone is clearly defined — code comprehension, generation, modification, and command execution — and within that zone, it represents the most efficient tool form available.
The terminal interface may feel uncomfortable initially. But once you adapt to the interaction paradigm of "directing an operating system with natural language," returning to manual operations feels remarkably clumsy.

entry.title: Journal | JFuM | Anger in predicting the index futures returns
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：本文旨在研究不同情绪如何影响指数期货收益。采用滞后的基于文本的情绪（愤怒、喜悦、恐惧、乐观和悲观）指数对预测标普500指数期货收益的预测回归进行了检验，发现悲观和乐观情绪指数之间存在不对称的预测能力。研究表明，只有基于文本的愤怒指数能可靠地预测指数期货的样本内收益率，并在样本外优于无条件平均值。值得注意的是，在控制了其他情绪指数、投资者情绪指数和已知可预测期货市场的基本变量后，基于文本的愤怒指数的预测能力依然存在。以基于文本的愤怒指数为条件的资产配置可以产生巨大的经济效益。此外，愤怒指数还通过贴现率和现金流两个渠道影响股指期货收益率。
Abstract: This paper investigates how different emotions affect index futures returns. Using lagged text-based emotion indices (anger, joy, fear, optimism, and pessimism), the study tests predictive regressions for S&P 500 index futures returns and finds asymmetric predictive ability between pessimism and optimism indices. Only the text-based anger index reliably predicts in-sample index futures returns and outperforms the unconditional mean out-of-sample. Notably, the predictive power of the anger index persists after controlling for other emotion indices, investor sentiment indices, and fundamental variables known to predict futures markets. Asset allocation conditioned on the anger index generates substantial economic gains. Furthermore, the anger index affects index futures returns through both discount rate and cash flow channels.
引用：Cao, Z., Shen, J., Wei, X., & Zhang, Q. (2023). Anger in predicting the index futures returns. Journal of Futures Markets, 43(4), 437-454.
Citation: Cao, Z., Shen, J., Wei, X., & Zhang, Q. (2023). Anger in predicting the index futures returns. Journal of Futures Markets, 43(4), 437-454.
文献：
Paper:
Keywords: anger, emotions, index futures return predictability. Introduction 引出话题
（1）情绪与资产定价、解释定价错误（$\color{blue}{Brown\ and\ Cliff,\ 2004}$; $\color{blue}{Neal\ and\ Wheatley,\ 1998}$; $\color{blue}{Stambaugh\ et\ al.,\ 2012}$）;
（2）投资者受情绪影响（$\color{blue}{De\ Long\ et\ al.,\ 1990}$）【不同于传统理性预期假说，噪声交易者对非基本面信号采取行动，投资者情绪波动会导致市场价格偏离其基本面价值】;
（3）投资者情绪指数（$\color{blue}{Manela\ and\ Moreira,\ 2017}$; $\color{blue}{Baker\ and\ Wurgler,\ 2006}$; $\color{blue}{Huang\ et\ al.,\ 2015}$），通过不同方式构建，并从理论和实证上阐明了投资者情绪如何影响股票市场；
（4）现有指数较为单一，其他情绪（$\color{blue}{Bodenhausen\ et\ al.,\ 1994}$; $\color{blue}{Griffith\ et\ al.,\ 2020}$; $\color{blue}{Shen\ et\ al.,\ 2023}$）【愤怒与市场的均值-方差风险厌恶和未来市场收益有关；恐惧、喜悦、悲观和压力指数对股票市场和债券市场的预测能力】;
（5）$\color{blue}{Han\ et\ al.(2022)}$期货市场通过提供全市场信息并在价格和回报方面引领现货市场，在全球市场中发挥着重要作用。→→→投资者情绪波动会影响规避风险的投资者对现货市场（如股票市场）的偏好，也可以传达大量信息，拉低或提高投资者对期货市场的需求；
Motivation
(1) Emotions relate to asset pricing and explain mispricing ($\color{blue}{Brown\ and\ Cliff,\ 2004}$; $\color{blue}{Neal\ and\ Wheatley,\ 1998}$; $\color{blue}{Stambaugh\ et\ al.,\ 2012}$);
(2) Investors are affected by emotions ($\color{blue}{De\ Long\ et\ al.,\ 1990}$) — unlike the rational expectations hypothesis, noise traders act on non-fundamental signals, and investor sentiment fluctuations cause market prices to deviate from fundamental values;
(3) Investor sentiment indices ($\color{blue}{Manela\ and\ Moreira,\ 2017}$; $\color{blue}{Baker\ and\ Wurgler,\ 2006}$; $\color{blue}{Huang\ et\ al.,\ 2015}$), constructed through various methods, theoretically and empirically illustrate how investor sentiment affects stock markets;
(4) Existing indices are relatively uni-dimensional; other emotions matter ($\color{blue}{Bodenhausen\ et\ al.,\ 1994}$; $\color{blue}{Griffith\ et\ al.,\ 2020}$; $\color{blue}{Shen\ et\ al.,\ 2023}$) — anger relates to mean-variance risk aversion and future market returns; fear, joy, gloom, and stress indices possess predictive power for stock and bond markets;
(5) $\color{blue}{Han\ et\ al.(2022)}$ — futures markets play a vital role in global markets by providing market-wide information and leading spot markets in prices and returns. Investor sentiment fluctuations affect risk-averse investors' preferences for spot markets (e.g., equities) and convey substantial information that lowers or raises investor demand for futures markets;
贡献
（1）将整体投资者情绪划分为多维度，揭示多维投资者情绪对期货市场的不对称可预测性；【anger、fear和gloom代表消极情绪，joy和optimism代表积极情绪。消极情绪显著可预测性，积极情绪的几乎无可预测性，即不对称的可预测性。（$\color{blue}{Smales\ (2014)}$投资者在消极情绪下表现出更多非理性，需要更高风险溢价）】
（2）为数不多研究投资者情绪与股指期货收益率之间联系的研究者，并揭示了愤怒在期货市场中的重要性【第一篇实证论文证明愤怒指数传达了关于期货市场的有价值的信息，样本内和样本外均带来正向收益】；
（3）首次探讨愤怒指数预测能力的微观经济mechanism【利用VAR模型阐明愤怒指数通过贴现率和现金流channels影响指数期货收益】；
Contributions
(1) Decomposes overall investor sentiment into multiple dimensions, revealing asymmetric predictability of multi-dimensional investor emotions on futures markets. Anger, fear, and gloom represent negative emotions; joy and optimism represent positive emotions. Negative emotions exhibit significant predictability while positive emotions show almost none — i.e., asymmetric predictability. ($\color{blue}{Smales\ (2014)}$ — investors exhibit greater irrationality under negative emotions, requiring higher risk premiums.)
(2) Among the few studies examining the link between investor sentiment and index futures returns, this paper reveals the importance of anger in futures markets — the first empirical paper demonstrating that the anger index conveys valuable information about futures markets with positive returns both in-sample and out-of-sample.
(3) First to explore the microeconomic mechanism behind the anger index's predictive power — uses a VAR model to show that the anger index affects index futures returns through discount rate and cash flow channels.
Data and Measuring Anger 数据：Datastream数据库的S&P 500 Index Futures日度简单收益（1998.01-2019.12），$R_{m,t}$表示月度收益；
TRMI 愤怒指数（TRMI数据库）从0到1，表示提及anger和disgust时的平均得分。构建愤怒指数：
Data: Daily simple returns of S&P 500 Index Futures from the Datastream database (1998.01--2019.12); $R_{m,t}$ denotes monthly returns.
TRMI anger index (from the TRMI database) ranges from 0 to 1, representing the average score when anger and disgust are mentioned. The anger index is constructed as:
Anger_t=\frac{TRMIAnger_t-TRMIAnger_{t-1}}{TRMIAnger_{t-1}} 图1为愤怒指数时序，阴影条为NBER衰退，在1.19%左右波动。
Figure 1 shows the anger index time series; shaded bars indicate NBER recessions, fluctuating around 1.19%.
相同方法构建：joy指数，fear指数，optimism指数，gloom指数。
其他变量：economic variables（Amit Goyal's website），implied volatility index (NVIX)，investor sentiment index（$\color{blue}{Baker\ and\ Wurgler,\ 2006}$; $\color{blue}{Huang\ et\ al.,\ 2015}$）
Constructed analogously: joy index, fear index, optimism index, gloom index.
Other variables: economic variables (Amit Goyal's website), implied volatility index (NVIX), investor sentiment index ($\color{blue}{Baker\ and\ Wurgler,\ 2006}$; $\color{blue}{Huang\ et\ al.,\ 2015}$)
In-Sample Predictive PerformanceBaseline Regression R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta Anger_t+\epsilon_{t+1} 正向（$\color{blue}{Bodenhausen\ et\ al.,\ 1994}$）【愤怒投资者做出刻板的判断和考虑不周的决定，往往会对市场失去信心并反应过度，从而减少对股票的需求和持有量。而需求的下降将拉低同期价格，并导致指数期货的未来回报率更高。总体而言，愤怒的投资者需要溢价才能购买和持有指数期货，这意味着愤怒指数与随后的指数期货回报之间存在正相关联系】
Positive relationship ($\color{blue}{Bodenhausen\ et\ al.,\ 1994}$) — Angry investors make stereotypical judgments and ill-considered decisions, tend to lose confidence in the market and overreact, thereby reducing demand for and holdings of equities. The decline in demand depresses contemporaneous prices and leads to higher future index futures returns. Overall, angry investors require a premium to buy and hold index futures, implying a positive association between the anger index and subsequent index futures returns.
Asymmetric Predictive Ability R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta Emotion_t+\varepsilon_{t+1} R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta_1Anger_t+\beta_2Emotion_t+\varepsilon_{t+1} Controlling for Business CycleD/P, DEF, TERM, detrended riskless rate (RREL)
R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta_1Anger_t+\beta_2Cycle_t+\varepsilon_{t+1} Controlling for Economic Variables R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta_{1}Anger_{t}+\beta_{2}ECON_{t}+\varepsilon_{t+1} Comparing with Other Sentiment Indices R_{m,t+1}=\alpha+\beta_1Anger_t+\beta_2Sentiment_t+\epsilon_{t+1} Out-of-Sample ForecastingOut-of-Sample MethodologyOut-of-Sample Forecasting Performance
OOS_t=\sum_{t=1}^t(R_{m,t+1}-\bar{R}_{m,t})^2-\sum_{t=1}^t(R_{m,t+1}-\hat{R}_{m,t})^2,\quad t=M,...,T1 Conditional Out-of-Sample Performance Economic Explanation References Brown, G. W., & Cliff, M. T. (2004). Investor sentiment and the near-term stock market. Journal of Empirical Finance, 11(1), 1-27. Neal, R., & Wheatley, S. M. (1998). Do measures of investor sentiment predict returns?. Journal of Financial and Quantitative Analysis, 33(4), 523-547. Stambaugh, R. F., Yu, J., & Yuan, Y. (2012). The short of it: Investor sentiment and anomalies. Journal of Financial Economics, 104(2), 288-302. De Long, J. B., Shleifer, A., Summers, L. H., & Waldmann, R. J. (1990). Noise trader risk in financial markets. Journal of Political Economy, 98(4), 703-738. Manela, A., & Moreira, A. (2017). News implied volatility and disaster concerns. Journal of Financial Economics, 123(1), 137-162. Baker, M., & Wurgler, J. (2006). Investor sentiment and the cross-section of stock returns. The Journal of Finance, 61(4), 1645-1680. Huang, D., Jiang, F., Tu, J., & Zhou, G. (2015). Investor sentiment aligned: A powerful predictor of stock returns. The Review of Financial Studies, 28(3), 791-837. Bodenhausen, G. V., Sheppard, L. A., & Kramer, G. P. (1994). Negative affect and social judgment: The differential impact of anger and sadness. European Journal of Social Psychology, 24(1), 45-62. Griffith, J., Najand, M., & Shen, J. (2020). Emotions in the stock market. Journal of Behavioral Finance, 21(1), 42-56. Shen, J., Griffith, J., Najand, M., & Sun, L. (2023). Predicting stock and bond market returns with emotions: Evidence from futures markets. Journal of Behavioral Finance, 24(3), 333-344. Han, L., Wei, X., Yan, S., & Zhang, Q. (2022). Analyst rating matters for index futures. Journal of Futures Markets, 42(11), 2084-2100. Smales, L. A. (2014). News sentiment in the gold futures market. Journal of Banking & Finance, 49, 275-286. Baker, M., & Wurgler, J. (2006). Investor sentiment and the cross-section of stock returns. The Journal of Finance, 61(4), 1645-1680.

entry.title: Software | R | Application of TF-IDF and cosine similarity
entry.content:
tf-idf（英语：term frequency–inverse document frequency）是一种用于信息检索与文本挖掘的常用加权技术。tf-idf是一种统计方法，用以评估一字词对于一个文件集或一个语料库中的其中一份文件的重要程度。字词的重要性随着它在文件中出现的次数成正比增加，但同时会随着它在语料库中出现的频率成反比下降。tf-idf加权的各种形式常被搜索引擎应用，作为文件与用户查询之间相关程度的度量或评级。除了tf-idf以外，互联网上的搜索引擎还会使用基于链接分析的评级方法，以确定文件在搜索结果中出现的顺序。
tf-idf (short for term frequency–inverse document frequency) is a commonly used weighting technique in information retrieval and text mining. tf-idf is a statistical method used to evaluate the importance of a word to a document within a document set or a corpus. The importance of a word increases proportionally with the number of times it appears in the document, but decreases inversely with its frequency across the corpus. Various forms of tf-idf weighting are commonly used by search engines as a measure or ranking of the relevance between a document and a user query. In addition to tf-idf, search engines on the internet also use link-analysis-based ranking methods to determine the order in which documents appear in search results.
https://www.ruanyifeng.com/blog/2013/03/tf-idf.html

entry.title: Tool | Claude Code | Writing CLAUDE.md: Making AI Truly Understand Your Project
entry.content:
An Overlooked Critical Variable 大量用户在安装 Claude Code 两三天后得出"不过如此"的结论。典型症状：生成的代码风格与项目格格不入，测试命令选错了包管理器，新文件放进了错误的目录。
这些失败几乎都指向同一个根因：Claude 缺乏项目上下文。
Claude Code 在启动时自动加载项目根目录下的 CLAUDE.md 文件。这份文件的本质是一个隐式知识的显式化容器——把团队成员"默认都知道"但从未写下来的规则，转化为 Claude 可以消费的结构化输入。
写好这个文件，输出质量立即跃升。不写，则 Claude 只能依赖统计推断来猜测你的项目规范——而统计推断在面对项目特异性时，错误率令人沮丧。
A significant number of users conclude Claude Code is "nothing special" after two or three days. Typical symptoms: generated code clashes with project style, test commands invoke the wrong package manager, new files land in incorrect directories.
These failures almost invariably trace to the same root cause: Claude lacks project context.
Claude Code automatically loads a CLAUDE.md file from the project root on startup. This file is essentially a container for making implicit knowledge explicit — converting the rules that team members "just know" but never documented into structured input Claude can consume.
A well-crafted file produces an immediate leap in output quality. Without it, Claude relies on statistical inference to guess project conventions — and statistical inference, when facing project-specific idiosyncrasies, yields frustrating error rates.
Three-Tier Configuration CLAUDE.md 并非只有项目根目录一个挂载点。Claude Code 的配置系统分为三个层级，遵循"就近优先"原则：
1. 用户级 ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
全局生效，对所有项目共享。适合存放个人维度的通用偏好——编程语言、沟通风格、提交习惯：
CLAUDE.md is not limited to a single mount point at the project root. Claude Code's configuration system spans three tiers, following a "nearest-first" priority:
1. User-level ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
Globally effective, shared across all projects. Suitable for personal preferences — programming language, communication style, commit habits:
# Personal Preferences- Always respond in Chinese unless the code/comments should be in English- Use concise commit messages in English- Prefer functional programming patterns when possible- Never auto-commit without asking me first
2. 项目级 项目根目录/CLAUDE.md
最核心的一层。项目技术栈、代码规范、目录约定、安全禁忌——所有项目特异性信息的归属地。
3. 目录级 子目录/CLAUDE.md
面向 monorepo 或模块化架构。前端和后端使用不同的代码规范？分别在 frontend/CLAUDE.md 和 backend/CLAUDE.md 中声明。
三个层级在运行时合并生效。当存在冲突时，更具体的层级覆盖更通用的层级——与 CSS 的级联规则类似。
2. Project-level project-root/CLAUDE.md
The most critical tier. Project tech stack, code conventions, directory structure, security constraints — the home for all project-specific information.
3. Directory-level subdirectory/CLAUDE.md
Designed for monorepos or modular architectures. Frontend and backend use different coding conventions? Declare them separately in frontend/CLAUDE.md and backend/CLAUDE.md.
All three tiers merge at runtime. When conflicts exist, more specific tiers override more general ones — similar to CSS cascade rules.
What to Include: Five Core Modules1. Tech Stack Overview## Project Overview- Next.js 14 app with App Router, deployed on Vercel- Database: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM- Auth: NextAuth.js with Google/GitHub providers- Styling: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui components
无需面面俱到——Claude 能通过读取代码自行补全细节。此处的目标是提供顶层架构决策，帮助 Claude 建立正确的心理模型。
No need to be exhaustive — Claude can fill in details by reading the code. The goal here is to provide top-level architectural decisions that help Claude build the correct mental model.
2. Command Registry## Commands- `pnpm dev` — Start dev server (port 3000)- `pnpm test` — Run vitest- `pnpm test:e2e` — Run Playwright e2e tests- `pnpm lint` — ESLint + Prettier check- `pnpm db:push` — Push Prisma schema to database- `pnpm db:seed` — Seed test data
这个模块的价值被严重低估。没有它，Claude 面临一个 npm / pnpm / yarn / bun 的四选一猜测题，误判率极高。明确声明命令可以消除这一整个类别的错误。
This module's value is severely underestimated. Without it, Claude faces a four-way guessing game among npm / pnpm / yarn / bun, with a high error rate. Explicit command declarations eliminate this entire category of mistakes.
3. Code Conventions## Code Style- Use named exports, not default exports- React components: functional components with TypeScript interfaces- File naming: kebab-case for files, PascalCase for components- API routes return { data, error } envelope format- All database queries go through service layer (src/services/)- Error handling: use custom AppError class, never throw raw strings
这是影响日常体验最深的模块。缺少这些规范声明，Claude 会退化到"统计上最常见的写法"——生成 export default function 而不是 named export，使用 throw new Error() 而不是自定义错误类。单次修正耗时不过几秒，但日积月累是惊人的摩擦成本。
This is the module with the deepest impact on daily experience. Without these convention declarations, Claude degrades to "statistically most common patterns" — generating export default function instead of named exports, using throw new Error() instead of custom error classes. Each correction takes only seconds, but the cumulative friction cost is staggering.
4. Directory Structure## Structuresrc/ app/ # Next.js App Router pages components/ # Shared UI components services/ # Business logic + DB queries lib/ # Utilities and helpers types/ # TypeScript type definitionstests/ unit/ # Vitest unit tests e2e/ # Playwright e2e tests
列出关键目录及其职责即可。Claude 据此判断新文件的归属位置。
List key directories and their responsibilities. Claude uses this to determine where new files belong.
5. Constraint Declarations## Important- NEVER modify files in src/generated/ — these are auto-generated by Prisma- The .env file contains production secrets — do not read or output its contents- Always run `pnpm lint` after modifying TypeScript files- src/legacy/ contains deprecated code scheduled for removal — do not import from it
"不要做什么"的信息密度远高于"要做什么"。自动生成的目录、包含密钥的文件、已废弃的模块——这些"地雷"只有通过显式声明才能让 Claude 规避。仅靠 Claude 自行推断，踩雷只是时间问题。
"What not to do" carries far higher information density than "what to do." Auto-generated directories, files containing secrets, deprecated modules — these "landmines" can only be avoided through explicit declaration. Relying on Claude's inference alone, stepping on one is merely a matter of time.
Anti-Patterns: Four Categories to Avoid 冗余信息。"这是一个 JavaScript 项目"——package.json 的存在已经传达了这一信息。CLAUDE.md 应当只包含不可从代码中推断的知识。
过度篇幅。CLAUDE.md 在每轮对话中都作为系统上下文被消费。一份千行级的文档意味着每条消息开头就要消耗数千 token。控制在 100-200 行以内是一个合理的上限。
易变内容。"当前正在开发用户注册功能"——这类状态信息的半衰期以天计。将其留给对话本身，而不是固化到配置文件中。
可推断的细节。函数签名、API 参数等信息 Claude 可以直接从源代码中获取，无需在 CLAUDE.md 中复述。
Redundant information. "This is a JavaScript project" — the existence of package.json already communicates this. CLAUDE.md should contain only knowledge that cannot be inferred from the code.
Excessive length. CLAUDE.md is consumed as system context in every conversation turn. A thousand-line document means thousands of tokens consumed at the start of each message. 100-200 lines is a reasonable upper bound.
Volatile content. "Currently developing user registration feature" — such state information has a half-life measured in days. Leave it to the conversation itself rather than crystallizing it in configuration.
Inferable details. Function signatures, API parameters — Claude can obtain these directly from source code without CLAUDE.md repeating them.
Template# Project Name## Overview- Tech stack summary (framework, database, deployment)- Key architecture decisions## Commands- dev / test / lint / build / deploy commands- Database commands if applicable## Code Conventions- Export style, naming conventions- Component patterns- Error handling approach- Where different types of code should live## Directory Structure- Key directories and their purposes (brief)## Constraints- Files/directories not to modify- Security-sensitive files- Auto-generated code locations- Deprecated code to avoid## Workflow- Branch naming convention- PR/commit message format- Any CI/CD considerations Quantified Impact 基于个人的持续使用观察：配置了完善的 CLAUDE.md 之后，Claude 首次输出即符合项目规范的比率从约 40-50% 提升至约 80%。提升幅度看似有限，但其经济学意义值得细算——每一次"不对，我们用 pnpm 不用 npm""这个文件应该放在 services 目录下"的纠正，都是一次额外的 API 调用，消耗的 token 远超那行纠正文本本身。
花 20 分钟写一份好的 CLAUDE.md，其收益在此后的每一次对话中持续兑现。从投入产出比的角度看，这大概是 Claude Code 使用流程中回报率最高的单项投资。
Based on sustained personal observation: after configuring a thorough CLAUDE.md, the rate at which Claude's first output conforms to project conventions rose from approximately 40-50% to roughly 80%. The improvement appears modest, but its economic significance merits analysis — each correction like "no, we use pnpm not npm" or "this file belongs in the services directory" constitutes an additional API call, consuming tokens far exceeding the correction text itself.
Spending 20 minutes writing a good CLAUDE.md yields returns in every subsequent conversation. From an ROI perspective, this is likely the highest-return single investment in the Claude Code workflow.

entry.title: Tool | Claude Code | Advanced Techniques: Hooks, MCP, and Automated Workflows
entry.content:
Claude Code 的基础功能——读写文件、运行命令、对话式交互——已经能覆盖大部分日常编程任务。但真正将其从"一个好用的工具"转化为"一套完整工作流"的，是下面这些进阶机制。它们的共同特征是：将人类的重复性判断固化为自动化规则。
Claude Code's basic capabilities — reading/writing files, running commands, conversational interaction — cover most daily programming tasks. But what truly transforms it from "a useful tool" into "a complete workflow" are the following advanced mechanisms. Their shared characteristic: crystallizing repetitive human judgments into automated rules.
Hooks: Programmatic Behavior Constraints Hooks 允许你在 Claude 执行特定工具调用的前后，自动运行 shell 命令。这不是一个锦上添花的功能——它从根本上改变了你与 AI 代理之间的信任模型。
在 .claude/settings.json 中配置：
Hooks allow you to automatically run shell commands before or after Claude executes specific tool calls. This is not a cosmetic feature — it fundamentally changes the trust model between you and the AI agent.
Configure in .claude/settings.json:
{ "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Edit|Write", "command": "echo 'File operation triggered'" } ], "PostToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Edit|Write", "command": "npx prettier --write \"$FILEPATH\" 2>/dev/null || true" } ], "Notification": [ { "command": "echo 'Claude Code notification received'" } ] }}
注意事件名称采用 PascalCase：PreToolUse、PostToolUse、Notification、Stop。matcher 字段支持正则表达式，匹配工具名称。
Hook 命令可以通过环境变量获取上下文信息（具体可用的变量请参考官方文档，因版本迭代可能有所变化）。
三种典型应用模式：
自动格式化。Claude 生成的代码未必完全符合你的 Prettier/ESLint 配置。PostToolUse hook 在每次文件修改后自动格式化，将代码风格的一致性从"Claude 的努力"升级为"系统保证"。
自动测试。每次编辑测试文件后自动运行对应的测试套件，实现即时反馈循环。
硬性安全边界。这是 Hooks 最具架构意义的应用：
Note the PascalCase event names: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Notification, Stop. The matcher field supports regex patterns matching tool names.
Hook commands can access context information through environment variables (refer to official documentation for specific available variables, as they may change across versions).
Three typical application patterns:
Auto-formatting. Code generated by Claude may not perfectly match your Prettier/ESLint configuration. A PostToolUse hook that auto-formats after each file modification upgrades code style consistency from "Claude's best effort" to "system guarantee."
Auto-testing. Automatically run the corresponding test suite after each test file edit, creating an instant feedback loop.
Hard safety boundaries. This is the most architecturally significant application of Hooks:
{ "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [ { "matcher": "Edit|Write", "command": "echo \"$TOOL_INPUT\" | grep -qE '(\\.env|credentials|secrets)' && echo 'BLOCKED: sensitive file' >&2 && exit 1 || true" } ] }}
当 PreToolUse hook 返回非零退出码时，Claude 会中止该工具调用。这是一道不依赖于 AI 自律性的硬性防线——无论 Claude 的意图如何，敏感文件都不会被修改。
这一机制的意义超越了单纯的安全防护。它建立了一种人机协作的新范式：人类通过声明式规则定义边界，AI 在边界内自由行动。信任不再是一个二元选择（完全信任 vs 逐条审批），而成为一个可编程的连续体。
When a PreToolUse hook returns a non-zero exit code, Claude aborts that tool call. This is a hard boundary that does not rely on AI self-discipline — regardless of Claude's intent, sensitive files will not be modified.
The significance of this mechanism extends beyond mere security. It establishes a new paradigm for human-AI collaboration: humans define boundaries through declarative rules, and AI operates freely within those boundaries. Trust is no longer a binary choice (full trust vs. per-action approval) but becomes a programmable continuum.
MCP: Connecting Claude to External Systems MCP（Model Context Protocol）是 Anthropic 推出的开放协议，使 AI 模型能够调用外部工具和服务。Claude Code 原生支持 MCP 服务器，意味着你可以将数据库、GitHub、Slack 等外部系统纳入 Claude 的操作范围。
在 .claude/settings.json 中配置（包名仅为示例，请以实际发布的包名为准）：
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open protocol by Anthropic enabling AI models to call external tools and services. Claude Code natively supports MCP servers, meaning you can bring databases, GitHub, Slack, and other external systems into Claude's operational scope.
Configure in .claude/settings.json (package names are illustrative — verify actual published package names):
{ "mcpServers": { "github": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"], "env": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "ghp_xxxxx" } }, "postgres": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres", "postgresql://localhost/mydb"] } }}
社区已构建了覆盖面广泛的 MCP 服务器生态：
数据库：PostgreSQL、MySQL、SQLite——直接查询和修改数据 GitHub：创建 issue、提交 PR、查看 CI 状态 Slack：读写消息 浏览器自动化：通过 Puppeteer/Playwright 控制浏览器 文件系统：更细粒度的文件访问控制
MCP 的战略价值在于它改变了 Claude Code 的能力边界。没有 MCP 的 Claude Code 是一个代码编辑代理；有了 MCP 的 Claude Code 是一个全栈工作台。你可以在一个对话中完成"查询数据库 → 分析结果 → 编写代码 → 提交 PR"的完整链路，无需在工具之间切换。
The community has built a broad MCP server ecosystem:
Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite — direct data queries and modifications GitHub: Create issues, submit PRs, check CI status Slack: Read and write messages Browser automation: Control browsers via Puppeteer/Playwright Filesystem: Fine-grained file access control
MCP's strategic value lies in redefining Claude Code's capability boundary. Without MCP, Claude Code is a code editing agent; with MCP, it becomes a full-stack workstation. You can complete the full chain of "query database → analyze results → write code → submit PR" in a single conversation, without switching between tools.
Memory: Cross-Session Knowledge Persistence Claude Code 的 Memory 系统通过在 ~/.claude/ 目录下维护记忆文件，实现跨对话的偏好和知识持久化。
触发方式直截了当：
Claude Code's Memory system maintains memory files in the ~/.claude/ directory, enabling preference and knowledge persistence across conversations.
The trigger mechanism is straightforward:
> 记住：这个项目用 pnpm，不要用 npm> Remember: always use tabs for indentation in this project> 以后帮我写 commit message 的时候用中文
Claude 将这些偏好写入记忆文件，后续新对话自动继承。
Memory 与 CLAUDE.md 的职责分野值得明确：
维度CLAUDE.mdMemory作用域项目级跨项目版本控制是（提交到 git）否（本地存储）共享性团队共享个人私有内容类型项目规范、技术栈、命令个人偏好、工作流习惯二者是互补关系而非替代关系。CLAUDE.md 回答"这个项目如何运作"，Memory 回答"这个用户如何偏好"。
Memory 适合存储：个人编码偏好、工作流习惯、反复出现的问题和解决方案。不适合存储：临时任务状态、可能过期的信息、已经在 CLAUDE.md 中声明过的内容。
Claude writes these preferences to memory files, which are automatically inherited by subsequent conversations.
The division of responsibility between Memory and CLAUDE.md is worth clarifying:
DimensionCLAUDE.mdMemoryScopeProject-levelCross-projectVersion controlYes (committed to git)No (local storage)SharingTeam-sharedPersonal/privateContent typeProject specs, tech stack, commandsPersonal preferences, workflow habitsThe two are complementary, not substitutive. CLAUDE.md answers "how this project works"; Memory answers "how this user prefers to work."
Memory is suitable for: personal coding preferences, workflow habits, recurring problems and solutions. Not suitable for: temporary task states, potentially expirable information, content already declared in CLAUDE.md.
Git Workflow Integration Claude Code 对 git 的支持不止于"能执行 git 命令"。它理解版本控制的语义结构，能在更高的抽象层次上协助 git 操作。
智能提交
Claude Code's git support goes beyond "can execute git commands." It understands the semantic structure of version control and assists at a higher level of abstraction.
Smart commits
> 看看我改了什么，帮我写个 commit message 然后提交
Claude 执行 git diff，分析所有变更的语义（而非仅仅列出文件名），生成一条准确反映变更意图的 commit message，然后执行提交。在赶工期的日子里，这比你随手写的"fix bug"或"update code"有价值得多。
PR 创建
Claude runs git diff, analyzes the semantics of all changes (not merely listing filenames), generates a commit message that accurately reflects the intent of the changes, and executes the commit. During crunch time, this is far more valuable than a hastily written "fix bug" or "update code."
PR creation
> 基于当前分支创建一个 PR 到 main，描述清楚这次改了什么
Claude 分析分支上的全部 commit，生成包含变更摘要、影响范围和测试建议的 PR 描述，然后通过 gh CLI 完成创建。
代码审查
Claude analyzes all commits on the branch, generates a PR description including change summary, impact scope, and testing suggestions, then completes creation via gh CLI.
Code review
> review 一下 src/services/ 目录下最近的改动，有没有潜在问题 Custom Keybindings Claude Code 支持通过 ~/.claude/keybindings.json 配置自定义键盘快捷键：
Claude Code supports custom keyboard shortcuts via ~/.claude/keybindings.json:
[ { "key": "ctrl+k", "command": "clear", "description": "Clear conversation" }, { "key": "ctrl+m", "command": "model", "description": "Switch model" }]
还支持 chord 组合键（连续按两个快捷键触发一个命令），在不制造按键冲突的前提下扩展快捷操作空间。
Chord bindings (pressing two shortcuts in sequence to trigger a command) are also supported, expanding shortcut space without creating key conflicts.
Multi-Agent Parallelism 处理大型任务时，Claude Code 可以同时派出多个子代理并行工作。要在 10 个文件中执行类似修改？Claude 可以启动多个子代理分别处理不同文件，而非串行逐一操作。
For large tasks, Claude Code can dispatch multiple subagents to work in parallel. Need similar modifications across 10 files? Claude can launch multiple subagents to handle different files simultaneously rather than processing them sequentially.
> 这 8 个 controller 文件都需要加上错误处理中间件，并行处理
并行处理不仅更快，而且更经济——每个子代理的上下文独立运作，不会互相叠加膨胀。
Parallel processing is not only faster but more economical — each subagent's context operates independently, with no mutual inflation.
Assembly: A Complete Workflow 将以上机制组装起来，一个成熟的 Claude Code 工作流长这样：
CLAUDE.md 声明项目规范和命令（见本系列第二篇） Hooks 保障代码风格一致性和安全边界 MCP 打通 GitHub 等外部系统 Memory 持久化个人偏好
配置到位后，日常流程简化为：打开终端 → claude → 自然语言描述任务 → 审查结果 → 下一个任务。
这套工作流对终端重度用户尤为自然。如果你的日常已经在 tmux/terminal 中度过，Claude Code 不要求你改变任何习惯——它只是让你的终端多了一个能理解自然语言的强力代理。
Combining the above mechanisms, a mature Claude Code workflow looks like:
CLAUDE.md declares project conventions and commands (see the second post in this series) Hooks ensure code style consistency and safety boundaries MCP connects to external systems like GitHub Memory persists personal preferences
With configuration in place, the daily flow simplifies to: open terminal → claude → describe task in natural language → review results → next task.
This workflow is particularly natural for heavy terminal users. If your daily life already takes place in tmux/terminal, Claude Code requires no habit changes — it simply adds a powerful natural-language-understanding agent to your terminal.

entry.title: Journal | JBF | How cheap talk in climate disclosures relates to climate initiatives, corporate emissions, and reputation risk
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover Source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：了解企业气候信息披露的复杂情况及其实际影响，对于管理与气候相关的金融风险至关重要。然而，目前的信息披露往往存在不精确、不准确和"漂绿"的问题。本文引入深度学习算法$ClimateBert_{CTI}$，以识别MSCI世界指数公司年度报告中与气候相关的空谈。研究发现只有有针对性的气候参与才会减少空谈。而自愿性气候披露则与更多的空谈相关。此外，空谈与负面新闻报道的增加和排放量的增长相关。因此，"空谈 "有助于评估气候倡议的有效性，并预测声誉和转型风险。
Abstract: Understanding the complexities of corporate climate disclosures and their real-world implications is critical for managing climate-related financial risks. However, current disclosures often suffer from imprecision, inaccuracy, and greenwashing. This paper introduces the deep learning algorithm $ClimateBert_{CTI}$ to identify climate-related cheap talk in the annual reports of MSCI World Index companies. The study finds that only targeted climate engagement reduces cheap talk, while voluntary climate disclosures are associated with more cheap talk. Furthermore, cheap talk is linked to increased negative news coverage and rising emissions. Thus, "cheap talk" serves as a metric for evaluating climate initiative effectiveness and predicting reputation and transition risks.
引用：Bingler, J. A., Kraus, M., Leippold, M., & Webersinke, N. (2024). How cheap talk in climate disclosures relates to climate initiatives, corporate emissions, and reputation risk. Journal of Banking & Finance, 107191.
Citation: Bingler, J. A., Kraus, M., Leippold, M., & Webersinke, N. (2024). How cheap talk in climate disclosures relates to climate initiatives, corporate emissions, and reputation risk. Journal of Banking & Finance, 107191.
文献/Paper： Keywords: Corporate climate disclosures; Voluntary reporting; Commitments; Greenhouse gas emissions; Negative news coverage; Textual analysis. Introduction 当前经济深刻转型时期，评估气候变化、气候政策和企业气候雄心声明对公司价值和投资组合策略的影响对投资者来说是一项重大挑战（例如，[$\color{blue}{Deng\ et\ al.\ (2012SFIRP)}$]()）。财务利益相关者不仅必须了解公司的气候风险敞口，还必须了解其所传达的气候行动和承诺在缓解前瞻性转型和物理风险方面的有效性（$\color{blue}{Bingler\ and\ Colesanti\ Senni,\ 2022CP}$），这就需要进行前瞻性气候风险分析。随着金融市场向可持续做法转变，投资者和金融监管机构必须区分空谈和真实气候风险管理承诺和策略，使市场参与者能够准确评估前瞻性气候风险。
本文通过引入$ClimateBert_{CTI}$解决这些问题，其为基于神经网络的$ClimateBert$模型构建的自然语言处理 （NLP）方法（$\color{blue}{Webersinke\ et\ al.,\ 2022arXiv}$）1。$ClimateBert$使研究人员和利益相关者能够辨别真实和非具体的气候承诺，并揭示公司披露的气候相关承诺的质量。这使得随后可以对企业的气候承诺及其与实际行动和结果的一致性进行大规模分析。具体而言，作者利用$ClimateBert_{CTI}$开发一个"空谈指数"，量化公司气候承诺中肤浅的比例。该指数有助于系统分析空谈与自愿气候倡议、企业气候绩效以及媒体对环境事件的报道之间的关系。通过这项调查，旨在帮助人们更深入地了解企业沟通、利益相关者的看法和气候承诺的真正实施之间的动态，最终实现与气候相一致的商业实践，提高市场效率。
During the current period of profound economic transformation, assessing the impact of climate change, climate policy, and corporate climate ambition statements on firm value and portfolio strategies presents a significant challenge for investors (e.g., [$\color{blue}{Deng\ et\ al.\ (2012SFIRP)}$]()). Financial stakeholders must understand not only a company's climate risk exposure but also the effectiveness of its communicated climate actions and commitments in mitigating forward-looking transition and physical risks ($\color{blue}{Bingler\ and\ Colesanti\ Senni,\ 2022CP}$), necessitating forward-looking climate risk analysis. As financial markets shift toward sustainable practices, investors and financial regulators must distinguish between cheap talk and genuine climate risk management commitments and strategies, enabling market participants to accurately assess forward-looking climate risks.
This paper addresses these issues by introducing $ClimateBert_{CTI}$, a natural language processing (NLP) method built on the neural network-based $ClimateBert$ model ($\color{blue}{Webersinke\ et\ al.,\ 2022arXiv}$)1. $ClimateBert$ enables researchers and stakeholders to distinguish between genuine and non-specific climate commitments, revealing the quality of climate-related commitments disclosed by companies. This facilitates large-scale analysis of corporate climate commitments and their alignment with actual actions and outcomes. Specifically, the authors develop a "cheap talk index" using $ClimateBert_{CTI}$ to quantify the proportion of superficial content in corporate climate commitments. This index enables systematic analysis of the relationship between cheap talk and voluntary climate initiatives, corporate climate performance, and media coverage of environmental events. The investigation aims to deepen understanding of the dynamics among corporate communication, stakeholder perceptions, and the genuine implementation of climate commitments, ultimately promoting climate-aligned business practices and improving market efficiency.
1. Our trained models are available at https://huggingface.co/climatebert. ↩
越来越多的文献强调气候相关信息（以新闻或公司披露的形式）对于积极管理气候相关金融风险和机遇的重要性2。 例如，$\color{blue}{Engle\ et\ al.\ (2020RFS)}$、$\color{blue}{Monasterolo\ and\ De\ Angelis\ (2020EcE)}$、$\color{blue}{Hsu\ et\ al.\ (2023JF)}$、$\color{blue}{Carbone\ et\ al.\ (2021SSRN)}$、$\color{blue}{Bolton\ et\ al.\ (2022JACF)}$的研究表明，金融市场已开始将企业与气候相关的转型风险和气候承诺纳入定价，并正在考虑气候对冲策略。然而，正如$\color{blue}{Hong\ et\ al.\ (2019JoE)}$、$\color{blue}{Baldauf\ et\ al.\ (2020RFS)}$、$\color{blue}{Bakkensen\ and\ Barrage\ (2004RFS)}$、$\color{blue}{Gostlow\ (2022SSRN)}$的研究表明，人们对市场效率低下的担忧持续存在，特别是在风险溢价中准确反映物理气候风险方面。一个主要挑战在于目前气候风险披露缺乏标准化和精确性，导致人们担心公司在气候相关报告中没有提供足够细致和对决策有用的信息3。 一般来说，公司可能有动机在气候信息披露中使用含糊的语言，以降低因潜在误导性信息而被起诉的风险。与此同时，他们面临着越来越大的压力，要求采取积极的气候行动，并透明地传达他们的承诺，以减少由于不作为或遗漏实质性相关信息而导致的诉讼风险，正如$\color{blue}{Weber\ and\ Hoesli\ (2020CL)}$和$\color{blue}{Hoesli\ (2021CL)}$所讨论的那样。
A growing body of literature underscores the importance of climate-related information (in the form of news or corporate disclosures) for actively managing climate-related financial risks and opportunities2. For instance, studies by $\color{blue}{Engle\ et\ al.\ (2020RFS)}$, $\color{blue}{Monasterolo\ and\ De\ Angelis\ (2020EcE)}$, $\color{blue}{Hsu\ et\ al.\ (2023JF)}$, $\color{blue}{Carbone\ et\ al.\ (2021SSRN)}$, and $\color{blue}{Bolton\ et\ al.\ (2022JACF)}$ demonstrate that financial markets have begun pricing corporate climate-related transition risks and climate commitments, and are considering climate hedging strategies. However, as shown by $\color{blue}{Hong\ et\ al.\ (2019JoE)}$, $\color{blue}{Baldauf\ et\ al.\ (2020RFS)}$, $\color{blue}{Bakkensen\ and\ Barrage\ (2004RFS)}$, and $\color{blue}{Gostlow\ (2022SSRN)}$, concerns about market inefficiency persist, particularly regarding the accurate reflection of physical climate risks in risk premia. A key challenge lies in the current lack of standardization and precision in climate risk disclosures, raising concerns that companies fail to provide sufficiently granular and decision-useful information in climate-related reports3. In general, firms may be incentivized to use vague language in climate disclosures to reduce litigation risk from potentially misleading information. Simultaneously, they face mounting pressure to take proactive climate action and transparently communicate their commitments to mitigate litigation risk arising from inaction or omission of materially relevant information, as discussed by $\color{blue}{Weber\ and\ Hoesli\ (2020CL)}$ and $\color{blue}{Hoesli\ (2021CL)}$.
2:Diamond (1985) provides a positive theory of voluntary disclosure by firms. In the environmental context, see e.g., Clarkson et al., 2013, Plumlee et al., 2015, Grewal et al., 2019, Hong et al., 2019, Krueger et al., 2020, Kölbel et al., 2022, Demaria and Rigot, 2021, Bolton and Kacperczyk, 2021b, Carbone et al., 2021.3: See, e.g., O’Dwyer and Unerman, 2020, Bolton et al., 2021, Griffin and Jaffe, 2021, Hoepner and Rogelj, 2021. In addition, previous research finds that companies report mainly positive information and that disclosures suffer from greenwashing and severe inaccuracies. See, e.g., Kim and Lyon, 2015, Marquis et al., 2016, Fabrizio and Kim, 2019, Bingler et al., 2022.
近年来，为促进企业气候行动和决策有用信息的披露，出现了许多行业主导或自愿的市场倡议。其中，"基于科学的目标倡议"（SBTi）所倡导的气候行动、"气候相关财务披露特别工作组"（TCFD）的披露建议以及 "气候行动 100+"（CA100+）参与倡议都是最突出的例子。然而，研究表明，特别是SBTi和TCFD举措在改善气候信息披露方面效果有限，并且不会在最需要的地方引发雄心勃勃的气候行动（$\color{blue}{Bingler\ et\ al.,\ 2022FRL}$；$\color{blue}{Bolton\ and\ Kacperczyk,\ 2021cNBER}$）4。鉴于这些倡议的重要存在和市场重要性，作者以$ClimateBert_{CTI}$的空谈指数为指标，调查了其与企业气候行动披露的关联。
In recent years, numerous industry-led or voluntary market initiatives have emerged to promote corporate climate action and decision-useful disclosures. Among them, the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for climate action, the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) recommendations, and the Climate Action 100+ (CA100+) engagement initiative are the most prominent examples. However, research indicates that the SBTi and TCFD initiatives in particular have limited effectiveness in improving climate disclosures and do not trigger ambitious climate action where it is most needed ($\color{blue}{Bingler\ et\ al.,\ 2022FRL}$; $\color{blue}{Bolton\ and\ Kacperczyk,\ 2021cNBER}$)4. Given the significant presence and market importance of these initiatives, the authors use the cheap talk index from $ClimateBert_{CTI}$ as a metric to investigate their association with corporate climate action disclosures.
4:For critiques on these initiatives, see for example https://www.climatechangenews.com/2022/02/06/science-based-targets-initiative-accused-providing-platform-greenwashing/, https://bridgingandcommercial.co.uk/article/18207/majority-of-uk-firms-see-tcfd-climate-reporting-as-box-ticking-exercise, and https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=a33eb47c-a3fb-4222-a769-ebbe4ddde31f. These concerns have already been discussed for the United Nations’ Principles for Responsible Investment (PRI), where academic literature (Gibson et al., 2021, Kim and Yoon, 2022, Bauckloh et al., 2021) finds that the PRI does not meet expectations and does not deliver on its promise. References Deng, M., Leippold, M., Wagner, A. F., & Wang, Q. (2022). The Russia-Ukraine War and climate policy expectations: Evidence from the stock market. Swiss Finance Institute Research Paper, (22-29). Bingler, J. A., & Colesanti Senni, C. (2022). Taming the Green Swan: a criteria-based analysis to improve the understanding of climate-related financial risk assessment tools. Climate Policy, 22(3), 356-370. Webersinke, N., Kraus, M., Bingler, J. A., & Leippold, M. (2021). Climatebert: A pretrained language model for climate-related text. arXiv preprint arXiv:2110.12010. Engle, R. F., Giglio, S., Kelly, B., Lee, H., & Stroebel, J. (2020). Hedging climate change news. The Review of Financial Studies, 33(3), 1184-1216. Monasterolo, I., & De Angelis, L. (2020). Blind to carbon risk? An analysis of stock market reaction to the Paris Agreement. Ecological Economics, 170, 106571. Hsu, P. H., Li, K., & Tsou, C. Y. (2023). The pollution premium. The Journal of Finance, 78(3), 1343-1392. Carbone, S., Giuzio, M., Kapadia, S., Krämer, J. S., Nyholm, K., & Vozian, K. (2021). The low-carbon transition, climate commitments and firm credit risk. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3991358 Bolton, P., Halem, Z., & Kacperczyk, M. (2022). The financial cost of carbon. Journal of Applied Corporate Finance, 34(2), 17-29. Hong, H., Li, F. W., & Xu, J. (2019). Climate risks and market efficiency. Journal of econometrics, 208(1), 265-281. Baldauf, M., Garlappi, L., & Yannelis, C. (2020). Does climate change affect real estate prices? Only if you believe in it. The Review of Financial Studies, 33(3), 1256-1295. Bakkensen, L. A., & Barrage, L. (2022). Going underwater? Flood risk belief heterogeneity and coastal home price dynamics. The Review of Financial Studies, 35(8), 3666-3709. Gostlow, G. (2021).
