Pricing physical climate risk in the cross-section of returns. Available at SSRN 3501013. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3501013 Weber, R. H., & Hösli, A. (2020). Climate change liability: Comparing risks for directors in jurisdictions of the common and civil law. Climate Law, 10(2), 151-196. Hösli, A. (2021). Milieudefensie et al. v. Shell: a tipping point in climate change litigation against corporations?. Climate Law, 11(2), 195-209. Bolton, P., & Kacperczyk, M. (2023). Firm commitments (No. w31244). National Bureau of Economic Research. https://www.nber.org/papers/w31244 Bingler, J. A., Kraus, M., Leippold, M., & Webersinke, N. (2022). Cheap talk and cherry-picking: What ClimateBert has to say on corporate climate risk disclosures. Finance Research Letters, 47, 102776.

entry.title: Journal | MS | ChatGPT for textual analysis? How to use generative LLMs in accounting research
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover Source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：生成式大语言模型（GLLMs），如OpenAI的ChatGPT和GPT-4，正成为会计研究中文本分析任务的强大工具。GLLMs不仅能解决非生成式方法可完成的所有文本分析任务，还能处理以往只能通过人工编码完成的任务。尽管GLLMs功能强大，但也存在局限性，并带来了需要谨慎应对的新挑战。本文重点介绍了GLLMs在会计研究中的应用，并与现有方法进行了比较。文章还提供了一个有效使用GLLMs的框架，涵盖模型选择、提示工程和确保构念效度等关键问题。在案例研究中，作者通过检测财报电话会议中的回避性回答（一项传统上难以自动化的任务）展示了GLLMs的能力。新的GPT方法达到了96%的准确率，并将回避性回答的错误率相对于现有的Gow et al. (2021)方法降低了70%。最后，文章讨论了使用GLLMs时应关注的偏差、可复制性和数据共享问题。总体而言，本文为研究者、审稿人和编辑提供了有效使用和评估GLLMs进行学术研究的知识与工具。
Abstract: Generative large language models (GLLMs), such as ChatGPT and GPT-4 by OpenAI, are emerging as powerful tools for textual analysis in accounting research. GLLMs can solve any textual analysis task solvable using nongenerative methods as well as tasks previously only solvable using human coding. While powerful, GLLMs also come with limitations and new challenges requiring care and due diligence. This paper highlights GLLM applications for accounting research and compares them with existing methods. It provides a framework for effectively using GLLMs, addressing key considerations such as model selection, prompt engineering, and construct validity. In a case study, the author demonstrates GLLM capabilities by detecting nonanswers in earnings conference calls, a traditionally challenging task to automate. The new GPT method achieves 96% accuracy and reduces the nonanswer error rate by 70% relative to the Gow et al. (2021) method. Finally, the paper discusses the importance of addressing bias, replicability, and data sharing concerns when using GLLMs. Overall, this paper equips researchers, reviewers, and editors with the knowledge and tools to effectively use and evaluate GLLMs for academic research.
引用：de Kok, T. (2025). ChatGPT for textual analysis? How to use generative LLMs in accounting research. Management Science. https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2023.03253.
Citation: de Kok, T. (2025). ChatGPT for textual analysis? How to use generative LLMs in accounting research. Management Science. https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2023.03253.
文献：
Paper:

entry.title: Journal | JFE | A picture is worth a thousand words: Measuring investor sentiment by combining machine learning and photos from news
Tool | In-Depth Guide: How to Efficiently Use DeepSeek Model APIs
entry.content:
随着AI技术的迅猛发展，DeepSeek模型的API服务逐渐成为开发者的热捧选择。如今，百度、字节跳动和阿里云等平台纷纷扩展了DeepSeek模型的应用接入方式，让广大开发者和AI爱好者能够更方便地调用强大的深度学习模型，无需本地部署，直接享受在线API带来的最大性价比。
With the rapid advancement of AI technology, DeepSeek model APIs have become an increasingly popular choice among developers. Platforms such as Baidu, ByteDance, and Alibaba Cloud have all expanded access to DeepSeek models, making it easier for developers and AI enthusiasts to leverage powerful deep learning models without local deployment, enjoying the best cost-effectiveness that online APIs offer.
Why Choose Online APIs Over Local Deployment? 使用在线API的优势显而易见：无需担心本地环境配置、硬件要求以及模型更新。最重要的是，几乎所有平台都保持兼容，开发者只需更改URL、API KEY和模型ID，便能轻松接入。同时，许多平台还支持OpenAI的SDK，极大简化了开发流程。
不想安装SDK？没问题！你可以通过简单的HTTP协议直接进行API调用，仅需二十行左右的代码，轻松实现模型的调用（参考各平台的文档，操作十分简单）。
The advantages of using online APIs are clear: no need to worry about local environment setup, hardware requirements, or model updates. Most importantly, nearly all platforms maintain compatibility — developers only need to change the URL, API KEY, and model ID to switch between services. Many platforms also support the OpenAI SDK, greatly simplifying the development workflow.
Don't want to install an SDK? No problem! You can make API calls directly via simple HTTP requests with roughly twenty lines of code (refer to each platform's documentation for straightforward instructions).
Latest Updates: Baidu, ByteDance, and Alibaba Cloud Launch DeepSeek Model API Services 在春节开工的最后一天，百度和字节跳动两大平台终于官宣上线了DeepSeek R1和V3模型的API服务，目前都提供免费额度。普通的AI代码生成、Agent开发等场景，可以优先选择V3模型；而复杂的逻辑推理任务则推荐使用R1模型。
On the last day back from the Chinese New Year holiday, both Baidu and ByteDance officially announced the launch of DeepSeek R1 and V3 model API services, currently offering free quotas. For typical AI code generation and Agent development scenarios, the V3 model is recommended; for complex logical reasoning tasks, the R1 model is the better choice.
1. Baidu Qianfan ModeBuilder Platform 网址：https://console.bce.baidu.com/qianfan/overview
百度千帆的API服务控制台经过几次升级简化，目前登录后可以轻松找到【千帆ModeBuilder】控制台：
URL: https://console.bce.baidu.com/qianfan/overview
The Baidu Qianfan API console has been simplified through several upgrades. After logging in, you can easily find the Qianfan ModeBuilder console:
创建API Key：
Create an API Key:
操作步骤：只需创建API KEY并充值（免费额度），无须再开通具体的模型，直接可用。
Steps: Simply create an API KEY and top up (free quota available). There is no need to activate specific models — they are ready to use immediately.
2. ByteDance Volcengine ARK Platform 网址：https://console.volcengine.com/ark
登录字节的火山引擎后，找到【火山方舟】的控制台。
URL: https://console.volcengine.com/ark
After logging into ByteDance's Volcengine, navigate to the Volcengine ARK console.
在此平台，你需要创建API KEY并充值，同时需要在【模型广场】找到并开通DeepSeek模型，然后点击【模型推理】进行接入点创建。
On this platform, you need to create an API KEY and top up your account. You also need to find and activate the DeepSeek model in the Model Plaza, then click Model Inference to create an endpoint.
操作步骤：创建接入点后，复制接入点的前端代码作为API调用时的model ID。
Steps: After creating the endpoint, copy the endpoint code to use as the model ID in your API calls.
3. Alibaba Cloud Bailian Platform 网址：https://bailian.console.aliyun.com
阿里云百炼平台的API服务同样经历了几次变革。当前可以在百炼控制台操作。
URL: https://bailian.console.aliyun.com
The Alibaba Cloud Bailian platform's API service has also undergone several changes. Currently, operations can be performed in the Bailian console.
该平台与百度相似，不需要选择具体模型开通，只需创建API KEY并充值（免费额度可用）。
Similar to Baidu, this platform does not require activating specific models. Simply create an API KEY and top up (free quota available).
注意事项：目前虽然平台支持DeepSeek模型，但在调用统计界面中仍标注"目前仅供免费体验，免费额度用完后不可调用，敬请关注后续动态。"，不排除阿里云将推出新的Qwen-Max模型，性能据说优于DeepSeek V3。
Note: Although the platform supports DeepSeek models, the usage statistics page still states that it is "currently available for free trial only — once the free quota is exhausted, the API cannot be called. Please stay tuned for updates." Alibaba Cloud may also release a new Qwen-Max model, reportedly outperforming DeepSeek V3.
Stable and Efficient API Calls Through Three Major Platforms 有了百度、字节跳动和阿里云这三大平台的强大算力支持，开发者可以不再担心API调用的流量限制。每个平台提供的稳定性和灵活性使得在不同场景下的使用都变得更加可靠。无论是快速切换平台以应对不同需求，还是根据项目的具体要求选择最适合的API版本，开发者都能够享受流畅、无缝的服务体验。
这些平台的免费额度为初学者和中小型项目提供了极大的便利，而对于更复杂的商业应用，丰富的服务选项和强大的后端计算能力将有效保障大规模API调用的持续稳定性。随着平台不断优化服务，未来API的使用将变得更加便捷、经济，并具备更强的扩展性。
With the powerful computing resources provided by Baidu, ByteDance, and Alibaba Cloud, developers no longer need to worry about API rate limits. The stability and flexibility offered by each platform make usage across different scenarios more reliable. Whether quickly switching between platforms to meet varying needs or selecting the most suitable API version based on specific project requirements, developers can enjoy a smooth, seamless experience.
The free quotas from these platforms provide great convenience for beginners and small-to-medium projects. For more complex commercial applications, the rich service options and robust backend computing power effectively ensure the sustained stability of large-scale API calls. As these platforms continue to optimize their services, API usage will become even more convenient, cost-effective, and scalable in the future.

entry.title: Journal | AER | Economic research evolves Fields and styles
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：We examine the evolution of economics research using a machine-learning-based classification of publications into fields and styles. The changing field distribution of publications would not seem to favor empirical papers. But economics' empirical shift is a within-field phenomenon; even fields that traditionally emphasize theory have gotten more empirical. Empirical work has also come to be more cited than theoretical work. The citation shift is sharpened when citations are weighted by journal importance. Regression analyses of citations per paper show empirical publications reaching citation parity with theoretical publications around 2000. Within fields and journals, however, empirical work is now cited more.
Abstract: We examine the evolution of economics research using a machine-learning-based classification of publications into fields and styles. The changing field distribution of publications would not seem to favor empirical papers. But economics' empirical shift is a within-field phenomenon; even fields that traditionally emphasize theory have gotten more empirical. Empirical work has also come to be more cited than theoretical work. The citation shift is sharpened when citations are weighted by journal importance. Regression analyses of citations per paper show empirical publications reaching citation parity with theoretical publications around 2000. Within fields and journals, however, empirical work is now cited more.
引用：Angrist, J., Azoulay, P., Ellison, G., Hill, R., & Lu, S. F. (2017). Economic research evolves: Fields and styles. American Economic Review, 107(5), 293-297.
Citation: Angrist, J., Azoulay, P., Ellison, G., Hill, R., & Lu, S. F. (2017). Economic research evolves: Fields and styles. American Economic Review, 107(5), 293-297.
文献：
Paper:
Introduction 经济学研究越来越趋向于实证化（Hamermesh，2013），本文深入研究了这一变化，揭示其主要体现在经济学领域内的研究风格变化上，并非跨领域的转变。基于机器学习模型，作者根据内容将经济学论文进行领域和风格分类（Angrist，2017），研究出版风格的演变是否伴随着影响力的改变。
Economics research has become increasingly empirical (Hamermesh, 2013). This paper investigates this shift, revealing that it is primarily a within-field change in research style rather than a cross-field transition. Using machine learning models, the authors classify economics papers by field and style based on content (Angrist, 2017), examining whether the evolution of publication styles is accompanied by changes in scholarly impact.
Field and Style Classification 本文使用Web of Science和EconLit数据库中经济学期刊的相关数据，分析论文发表量和引用率。
This paper uses data from economics journals indexed in Web of Science and EconLit to analyze publication volumes and citation rates.
Field Classification 领域分类基于JEL代码、标题和关键词、论文及其引用文献的领域等四个信息来源。参考Ellison（2002）将JEL分类映射到17个领域的做法，作者手工将论文分配到相应领域，并将其作为训练数据来源。机器学习算法为每篇论文生成初始领域分类，聚类算法利用论文的初始分类及其所引用论文的初始分类，为每篇论文分配最终领域（详细说明可参考在线附录，https://www.aeaweb.org/content/file?id=4416 ）。 根据论文内容，作者将其聚类为微观经济学、宏观经济学、计量经济学、公共财政、劳动经济学、国际经济学、金融学、产业组织，发展经济学和综合经济学等十个领域。其中，综合经济学是较小领域的综合，包括经济史、环境经济学、实验经济学、法律与经济学、政治经济学和城市经济学等。
Field classification draws on four information sources: JEL codes, titles and keywords, paper fields, and the fields of cited references. Following Ellison (2002), who mapped JEL codes to 17 fields, the authors manually assigned papers to corresponding fields as training data. A machine learning algorithm generates an initial field classification for each paper, and a clustering algorithm then assigns a final field using both the paper's initial classification and the initial classifications of its cited references (see the online appendix at https://www.aeaweb.org/content/file?id=4416 for details). Based on paper content, the authors cluster papers into ten fields: microeconomics, macroeconomics, econometrics, public finance, labor economics, international economics, finance, industrial organization, development economics, and miscellaneous economics. The miscellaneous category aggregates smaller fields including economic history, environmental economics, experimental economics, law and economics, political economy, and urban economics.
Style Classification 本文根据论文内容将其划分为三种研究风格，包括理论研究、实证研究和计量经济学风格。首先，计量经济学领域分类的论文被归类为计量经济学风格。然后，将使用数据估计经济上有意义参数的论文标记为实证研究。最后，将其余论文分类为理论研究。 本文通过机器学习算法进行风格分类，训练集包含5950篇手工分类的论文，但这一做法过度分类了顶级期刊。其中，训练集包括：
（1）Ellison（2002）最初分类的文章，1971年至1998年前六本经济学期刊，共计1507篇论文； （2）来自AER、JPE和Econometrica的论文： - AER1992年至2004年的436篇论文； - Econometrica从1998年至2013年的822篇论文； - JPE从1987年至2014年的933篇论文；
（3）本文采用的列表期刊，从1980年至1989年发表的文章中随机选择的15篇论文，共计1080篇论文；
（4） 从1990年至1999年、2000年至2013年AER引用量排名前20的期刊中，每十年随机选择每个期刊的15篇论文；每十年随机选择其他期刊的5篇论文，总计1172篇文章。
This paper classifies papers into three research styles based on content: theoretical, empirical, and econometric. First, papers classified in the econometrics field are assigned the econometric style. Next, papers that use data to estimate economically meaningful parameters are labeled as empirical. The remaining papers are classified as theoretical. Style classification is performed via a machine learning algorithm trained on 5,950 manually classified papers, though this procedure over-samples top journals. The training set comprises:
(1) Articles originally classified by Ellison (2002) from the top six economics journals, 1971--1998, totaling 1,507 papers; (2) Papers from AER, JPE, and Econometrica: - 436 AER papers from 1992--2004; - 822 Econometrica papers from 1998--2013; - 933 JPE papers from 1987--2014;
(3) 15 randomly selected papers per journal from articles published between 1980 and 1989 in the journals used in this study, totaling 1,080 papers;
(4) From the top 20 journals by AER citation count in 1990--1999 and 2000--2013, 15 randomly selected papers per journal per decade; 5 randomly selected papers from other journals per decade, totaling 1,172 articles.
Journal Weights 学者们更关注声望高、被广泛引用的综合期刊上的论文，为了表达期刊的层次结构，本文引入重要性权重，并使用符号$w_{j}^{t}$表示。
$w_{j}^{t}$反映了期刊$j$ （本文的期刊列表）被六本顶级期刊加权组合引用的情况。首先，将Google PageRank算法应用于矩阵（详见在线附录https://www.aeaweb.org/content/file?id=4416 ）。该矩阵计算了六本期刊内的引用比例$μ_k^t$。然后，计算引用比例的加权平均，即。最后，五年移动平均值为。
Scholars pay greater attention to papers in prestigious, widely cited general-interest journals. To capture this journal hierarchy, the paper introduces importance weights denoted $w_{j}^{t}$.
$w_{j}^{t}$ reflects the extent to which journal $j$ (from the paper's journal list) is cited by a weighted combination of the top six journals. The Google PageRank algorithm is first applied to the citation matrix (see the online appendix at https://www.aeaweb.org/content/file?id=4416 for details). This matrix yields within-journal citation shares $μ_k^t$, from which a weighted average of citation shares is computed. The final measure is a five-year moving average.
References Angrist, J., Azoulay, P., Ellison, G., Hill, R., & Lu, S. F. (2020). Inside job or deep impact? Extramural citations and the influence of economic scholarship. Journal of Economic Literature, 58(1), 3-52. Ellison, G. (2002). The slowdown of the economics publishing process. Journal of political Economy, 110(5), 947-993. Hamermesh, D. S. (2013). Six decades of top economics publishing: Who and how?. Journal of Economic literature, 51(1), 162-172.
Angrist, J., Azoulay, P., Ellison, G., Hill, R., & Lu, S. F. (2020). Inside job or deep impact? Extramural citations and the influence of economic scholarship. Journal of Economic Literature, 58(1), 3-52. Ellison, G. (2002). The slowdown of the economics publishing process. Journal of political Economy, 110(5), 947-993. Hamermesh, D. S. (2013). Six decades of top economics publishing: Who and how?. Journal of Economic literature, 51(1), 162-172.

entry.title: Literature | EAPS | Can ChatGPT reduce human financial analysts' optimistic biases?
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：本文探究了大语言模型ChatGPT作为上市公司业绩预测财务顾问的潜力。利用中国证券指数300成分股，将ChatGPT对主要财务业绩指标的预测与人类分析师的预测和真实值进行比较，结果表明ChatGPT可以纠正人类分析师的乐观偏见。
Abstract: This paper explores the potential of ChatGPT as a financial advisor for forecasting the performance of publicly listed companies. Using the CSI 300 index constituents, ChatGPT's predictions for key financial performance indicators are compared with those of human analysts and actual values. The results suggest that ChatGPT can correct the optimistic biases of human analysts.
引用：Li, X., Feng, H., Yang, H., & Huang, J. (2024). Can ChatGPT reduce human financial analysts' optimistic biases?. Economic and Political Studies, 12(1), 20-33.
Citation: Li, X., Feng, H., Yang, H., & Huang, J. (2024). Can ChatGPT reduce human financial analysts' optimistic biases?. Economic and Political Studies, 12(1), 20-33.
文献：
Paper:
Introduction https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/_n0HnVh5GbIglIMZFx51_A (文献一览)
本文研究了ChatGPT预测上市公司业绩方面的能力，以及ChatGPT是否可以减少人类分析师的乐观偏见。
尽管ChatGPT主要是一种语言模型，不是专门为财务决策设计的（Ko and Lee Citation, 2023），但其高效提取和处理广泛信息的能力使其适合担任财务顾问。
分析师预测涉及密集的信息提取和处理活动，在这些活动中，机器具有比人类更高的计算效率（Boyacı et al., 2024）,研究一致表明，机器或机器增强分析师在收益和股价预测方面优于人类（Chen et al., 2024; Coleman et al., 2022; Cao et al, 2023）。因此，作者预计ChatGPT能够产生比人类分析师更准确的预测。
此外，本文还探索了提高预测准确性的机制。人类分析师经常表现出乐观的偏见，这源于其参与预测过程和利益冲突。然而，机器是公正的并且受人类偏见的影响较小。这些偏差可以解释许多异常现象，并且可能与记忆过程有关。本文认为ChatGPT预测公司业绩的卓越能力源于减轻人类分析师的乐观偏见。
使用ChatGPT的prompt方式预测2021年至2023年CSI 300指数成分股的业绩，然后将其预测与人类分析师的预测和实现的业绩进行比较（同样为2021年9月前）。关注七个主要的财务绩效指标，即市盈率（PE）、市净率（PB）、每股收益（EPS）、资产回报率（ROA）、净资产收益率（ROE）、收入增长和利润增长。
作者发现ChatGPT在所有绩效维度和预测范围（即2021年、2022年和2023年末）上都比人类分析师更加保守。
This paper examines ChatGPT's ability to forecast the performance of publicly listed companies and whether it can reduce the optimistic biases of human analysts.
Although ChatGPT is primarily a language model not specifically designed for financial decision-making (Ko and Lee Citation, 2023), its ability to efficiently extract and process extensive information makes it suitable as a financial advisor.
Analyst forecasting involves intensive information extraction and processing, where machines exhibit higher computational efficiency than humans (Boyacı et al., 2024). Research consistently shows that machines or machine-augmented analysts outperform humans in earnings and stock price forecasting (Chen et al., 2024; Coleman et al., 2022; Cao et al, 2023). The authors therefore expect ChatGPT to produce more accurate forecasts than human analysts.
The paper also explores the mechanism behind improved forecast accuracy. Human analysts frequently exhibit optimistic biases stemming from their involvement in the forecasting process and conflicts of interest. Machines, however, are impartial and less susceptible to human biases. The paper argues that ChatGPT's superior ability to forecast corporate performance stems from mitigating the optimistic biases of human analysts.
Using ChatGPT prompts to forecast the performance of CSI 300 constituents from 2021 to 2023, the authors compare its predictions with human analyst forecasts and realised performance (also as of September 2021). Seven key financial performance indicators are examined: PE, PB, EPS, ROA, ROE, revenue growth, and profit growth.
The authors find that ChatGPT is more conservative than human analysts across all performance dimensions and forecast horizons (i.e., year-end 2021, 2022, and 2023).
本文在回归中量化了由ChatGPT纠正的人类乐观偏见：
使用向上预测误差（upward forecast errors）（计算为预测值与实现值之间的差异）作为因变量； 将预测误差与ChatGPT虚拟变量（指示预测是否由ChatGPT发布）进行回归；
The paper quantifies the human optimistic biases corrected by ChatGPT in a regression framework:
Upward forecast errors (calculated as the difference between forecasted and realised values) serve as the dependent variable; Forecast errors are regressed on a ChatGPT dummy variable (indicating whether the forecast is issued by ChatGPT);
Literature Review 三个角度：financial concept comprehension, academic use, and investment decision-making.
金融概念理解
- 理解财务和会计概念
- ChatGPT 可以准确解释阿尔法值、众筹、另类金融、金融风险、金融危机、巴塞尔框架和银行产品等金融概念（Wenzlaff and Spaeth 2022；Hofert 2023；Lakkaraju et al. 2023；Ren、Lee 和 Hu 2023；Yue et al. 2023），尽管其对数学事实的阐述需要改进（Hofert 2023）;
- Niszczota 和 Abbas（2023）研究了 ChatGPT 是否有能力充当财务顾问，发现它比随机猜测的人类投资者表现出更高水平的金融素养；
- 总体而言，ChatGPT 与金融专业人士相当，并且表现出高水平的准确性和专业知识（Ren、Lee 和 Hu 2023）；
- 在类似的环境中，Wei、Wu 和 Chu (2023) 发现 ChatGPT 对审计问题的回答模仿了经验丰富的财务审计师的回答。
- 解释专有名词
- Ren、Lee 和 Hu（2023）表明，ChatGPT 对财务和会计问题的回答比人类专家的回答更容易被外行人理解； - Neilson（2023）提供了更直接的证据，表明 ChatGPT 可以向非专业人士推荐养老金缴款计划； - Yue 等人（2023）通过向 ChatGPT 提出问题或给予指导，例如"向我的祖母解释金融中阿尔法的含义"，当给定受众指示时，ChatGPT 可以进一步定制其解释的复杂性； - 尽管有其优点，Lakkaraju 等人 (2023) 和 Neilson (2023) 警告ChatGPT在数字推理方面可能存在局限性、其不一致以及对各种相关问题（例如当地监管要求）的无知。
学术用途 - ChatGPT 的表现证据因它所承担的具体工作而异。 ChatGPT 在编码支持、数据分析和结果解释方面表现出色（Alshater 2022；Dowling 和 Lucey 2023；Feng、Hu 和 Li 2023；Korinek 2023）； - ChatGPT在文献综合、测试框架开发、特定领域专业知识和创意起源方面的表现并不令人满意（Alshater 2022；Dowling 和 Lucey 2023）。
投资决策
- 前期研究 - 关于 ChatGPT 在零样本环境下对金融文本的理解和解释，文献尚无定论（即提示中没有提供预期响应的示例）； - 结论A： - ChatGPT 在提取新闻、Fedspeak2、和公司披露中的观点和情绪方面准确且高效（Hansen 和 Kazinnik 2023；Jha 等人 2023）； - 结论B： - 其他人则声称 ChatGPT 在金融命名实体识别 (FinNER) 和情感分析等任务中表现不佳（Lan 等人，2023 年；Li 等人，2023 年）； - 王等人 (2023) 证明评估 ChatGPT 的性能很复杂：它会生成合理的答案，但可能并不总是与给出的提示相关；然而，与此同时，它在情感分析中优于 Transformer (BERT) 模型的微调双向编码器表示； - 对相互矛盾的结果的一种可能解释可能是使用了不同的数据源和绩效衡量标准，仅依赖一个数据集的研究往往会高估 ChatGPT 的性能（Jha 等人，2023 年；Hansen 和 Kazinnik，2023 年），而使用多个数据集和不同任务的研究则观察到更多其局限性（Li 等人，2023 年；Wang 等人）等2023）。
- 近期研究
- 最近的论文通过直接检验 ChatGPT 是否可以从投资决策的金融背景中提取价值相关信号来扩展这一文献；
- 优势
- $\color{blue}{Lopez-Lira\ and\ Tang\ (2023)}$使用ChatGPT对股票新闻标题进行情绪分析，将新闻分类为好、坏或无关，每个股构建了"ChatGPT 分数"，并发现其与随后的每日股票回报呈正相关；
- $\color{blue}{Kim\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$通过ChatGPT总结管理层讨论和分析、年度报告和收益电话会议中包含的信息，并生成带有明显情绪的精炼摘要，精炼后的摘要对披露日异常收益表现出比原文更显着的解释力。
- 局限性
- $\color{blue}{Xie\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$使用ChatGPT通过输入历史股票价格来预测未来股票走势的方向，结果表明：ChatGPT 的性能较差（因为预测不如逻辑回归准确）。
投资实战
- $\color{blue}{Ko\ and \ Lee\ (2023)}$将ChatGPT应用于投资，发现ChatGPT的表现优于随机选择的投资组合； - $\color{blue}{Chen\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$使用三阶段程序，提供了有关ChatGPT如何利用其提取的信息来实现卓越投资业绩的进一步证据： - 首先，向ChatGPT 提供财经新闻提示，并询问哪些公司受到正面或负面影响； - 然后，构建可视化关系的图表； - 最后，他们使用机器学习方法，包括图神经网络和长短期记忆神经网络，以比 ChatGPT 更高的准确度来预测股价走势。
2: Fedtalk是美国联邦储备委员会（Fed）就货币政策决策进行沟通时使用的技术语言。Hansen和Kazinik（2023）表示，ChatGPT能够将美联储的声明分为鹰派或鸽派。
Three perspectives: financial concept comprehension, academic use, and investment decision-making.
Financial concept comprehension
- Understanding financial and accounting concepts
- ChatGPT can accurately explain financial concepts such as alpha, crowdfunding, alternative finance, financial risk, financial crises, the Basel framework, and banking products (Wenzlaff and Spaeth 2022; Hofert 2023; Lakkaraju et al. 2023; Ren, Lee and Hu 2023; Yue et al. 2023), though its elaboration of mathematical facts requires improvement (Hofert 2023);
- Niszczota and Abbas (2023) examined whether ChatGPT can serve as a financial advisor and found it demonstrates higher financial literacy than randomly guessing human investors;
- Overall, ChatGPT is comparable to financial professionals and exhibits a high level of accuracy and expertise (Ren, Lee and Hu 2023);
- In a similar setting, Wei, Wu and Chu (2023) found that ChatGPT's responses to audit questions mimic those of experienced financial auditors.
- Explaining terminology
- Ren, Lee and Hu (2023) showed that ChatGPT's responses to financial and accounting questions are more comprehensible to laypeople than those of human experts; - Neilson (2023) provided more direct evidence that ChatGPT can recommend pension contribution plans to non-specialists; - Yue et al. (2023) demonstrated that ChatGPT can further customize the complexity of its explanations when given audience instructions, e.g., "explain the meaning of alpha in finance to my grandmother"; - Despite its merits, Lakkaraju et al. (2023) and Neilson (2023) caution about ChatGPT's potential limitations in numerical reasoning, inconsistencies, and ignorance of various relevant issues (e.g., local regulatory requirements).
Academic use - ChatGPT's performance evidence varies by specific task. It excels in coding support, data analysis, and results interpretation (Alshater 2022; Dowling and Lucey 2023; Feng, Hu and Li 2023; Korinek 2023); - Its performance in literature synthesis, test framework development, domain-specific expertise, and creative origination is unsatisfactory (Alshater 2022; Dowling and Lucey 2023).
Investment decision-making
- Earlier studies - The literature is inconclusive regarding ChatGPT's comprehension and interpretation of financial texts in a zero-shot setting (i.e., no expected response examples provided in the prompt); - Finding A: - ChatGPT is accurate and efficient in extracting opinions and sentiments from news, Fedspeak2, and corporate disclosures (Hansen and Kazinnik 2023; Jha et al. 2023); - Finding B: - Others claim ChatGPT underperforms in tasks such as financial named entity recognition (FinNER) and sentiment analysis (Lan et al. 2023; Li et al. 2023); - Wang et al. (2023) demonstrate that evaluating ChatGPT's performance is complex: it generates plausible answers that may not always be relevant to the given prompt; however, it outperforms fine-tuned BERT in sentiment analysis; - One possible explanation for contradictory results is the use of different data sources and performance measures. Studies relying on a single dataset tend to overestimate ChatGPT's performance (Jha et al. 2023; Hansen and Kazinnik 2023), while those using multiple datasets and tasks observe more limitations (Li et al. 2023; Wang et al. 2023).
- Recent studies
- Recent papers extend this literature by directly testing whether ChatGPT can extract value-relevant signals from financial contexts for investment decisions;
- Strengths
- $\color{blue}{Lopez-Lira\ and\ Tang\ (2023)}$ used ChatGPT for sentiment analysis of stock news headlines, classifying news as good, bad, or irrelevant, constructing a "ChatGPT score" for each stock, and found it positively correlated with subsequent daily stock returns;
- $\color{blue}{Kim\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$: ChatGPT summarises information from MD&A, annual reports, and earnings calls, generating refined summaries with clear sentiment; the refined summaries exhibit significantly greater explanatory power for disclosure-day abnormal returns than original texts.
- Limitations
- $\color{blue}{Xie\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$ used ChatGPT to predict the direction of future stock movements by inputting historical stock prices; results show ChatGPT performs poorly (predictions are less accurate than logistic regression).
- Investment practice
- $\color{blue}{Ko\ and \ Lee\ (2023)}$ applied ChatGPT to investment and found it outperforms randomly selected portfolios; - $\color{blue}{Chen\ et\ al.\ (2023)}$ used a three-stage procedure providing further evidence on how ChatGPT leverages extracted information to achieve superior investment performance: - First, prompt ChatGPT with financial news and ask which companies are positively or negatively affected; - Then, construct graphs to visualise relationships; - Finally, use machine learning methods, including graph neural networks and LSTM networks, to predict stock price movements with higher accuracy than ChatGPT.
2: Fedtalk is the technical language used by the U.S. Federal Reserve when communicating monetary policy decisions. Hansen and Kazinnik (2023) show that ChatGPT can classify Fed statements as hawkish or dovish.
DataData Description CSI 300指数被广泛认为是中国股市广泛走势的综合指标（$\color{blue}{Hou\ and\ Li,\ 2014}$） The CSI 300 was introduced on 8 April 2005 by the Shanghai and Shenzhen stock exchanges. It consists of the 300 most actively traded Chinese A-share stocks, which account for over 70% of the combined market capitalisation of the two exchanges. The index is widely recognised as a comprehensive indicator of broad movements in the Chinese stock markets ($\color{blue}{Hou\ and\ Li,\ 2014}$).
沪深300指数成分股
采用七种公司绩效衡量标准： - 股票估值： - PE（按股价除以每股收益计算） - PB（按股价除以每股账面价值计算） - 盈利能力 - 利润除以已发行股票（EPS） - 净利润除以总资产（ROA） - 净利润除以总权益（ROE） - 公司增长 - 收入增长率 - 利润增长率
CSI 300 index constituents
Seven corporate performance measures are adopted: - Stock valuation: - PE (stock price divided by earnings per share) - PB (stock price divided by book value per share) - Profitability: - Earnings divided by outstanding shares (EPS) - Net income divided by total assets (ROA) - Net income divided by total equity (ROE) - Corporate growth: - Revenue growth rate - Profit growth rate
Prompt 输入个股名称和股票代码，并要求ChatGPT预测2021年、2022年和2023年末的PE、PB、EPS、ROA、ROE、收入增长和利润增长。
ChatGPT的回复是高度依赖于提示，而提示本质上是用户的问题。 $\color{blue}{Korinek\ (2023) }$发现提示中的微小调整可能会导致不同的结果。因此，我们尝试不同的提示来检索所需的结果。遵循 OpenAI3和$\color{blue}{M\ Alshater\ (2022) }$ 的指导，构建提示如下：
Individual stock names and ticker codes are input, and ChatGPT is asked to forecast PE, PB, EPS, ROA, ROE, revenue growth, and profit growth at the end of 2021, 2022, and 2023.
ChatGPT's responses are highly dependent on the prompt, which is essentially the user's question. $\color{blue}{Korinek\ (2023) }$ found that minor adjustments in prompts can lead to different results. Different prompts are therefore tested to retrieve the desired output. Following the guidance of OpenAI3 and $\color{blue}{M\ Alshater\ (2022) }$, the prompt is constructed as follows:
Provide a table of price-to-earnings (P/E) forecasts at the end of 2021, 2022 and 2023 for the firms below, as of September 2021:[A list of firm names and tickers]There should be four columns in the table: Ticker, P/E 2021, P/E 2022, P/E 2023.
提示尽可能清晰，明确指定了度量、时间范围、公司和截至时间。在没有时间范围的情况下，ChatGPT会以截至 2021年9月的最新实现值进行响应。在没有截至时间的情况下，ChatGPT拒绝分配并解释其无法提供2021年9月之后的信息。ChatGPT做出的预测是：仅当提示包含时间范围和当前时间时我们才需要。我们要求的表格输出格式隐藏了ChatGPT对每个公司未来业绩的语言分析，并仅保留预测值4。使用分隔符划分提示：使用一个换行符指示公司，使用两个换行符指示指令和公司列表。
单个提示-响应对中存在上下文长度限制，将 300 家样本企业分为 10 批，每批 30 家企业。相当小的批量大小避免长度溢出，并消除了公司内部（跨范围）预测中断和恢复提示干扰所引入的偏差，从而使预测能够继续。
在某些文本生成任务中，少量提示或在提示中提供对话示例可以提高生成质量。通过采用零样本提示而不是少样本提示，我们采取严格中立的立场，并且不会因人类预测示例而对 ChatGPT 产生偏见（$\color{blue}{Zhao\ et\ al.\ 2021}$）。当进行下一个绩效衡量时，总是开始新的聊天，以确保输出不会受到先前指令的影响。
另一个挑战是，即使多次给出相同的提示，ChatGPT 也可能会产生略有不同的预测。幸运的是，尽管存在细微差异，但结果高度相似且具有可比性（$\color{blue}{Ko\ and \ Lee,\ 2023}$）。为了减轻一代特质，本文独立重复该过程 35 次并取平均值5。 使用该方法提供了对300家公司七项指标的三年预测。
从CSMAR数据库获取金融分析师的预测，为了确保人类分析师和ChatGPT使用可比较的信息集，将人类预测限制为2021年9月前发布的预测。一些报告提供了不同时间范围的预测。作者从582份分析师报告中总共获得了10550个预测年观察结果。真实数据来自CSMAR和Wind，但仅有2021年和2022年的数据，在撰写本文时还没有2023年的数据。
The prompt is made as clear as possible, explicitly specifying the metric, time horizon, firms, and as-of date. Without the time horizon, ChatGPT responds with the latest realised values as of September 2021. Without the as-of date, ChatGPT refuses to forecast and explains its inability to provide information beyond September 2021. ChatGPT's forecasts are generated only when both the time horizon and the current date are included. The requested table output format suppresses ChatGPT's linguistic analysis of each firm's future performance and retains only the forecasted values4. Delimiters are used to structure the prompt: one line break to indicate firms, and two line breaks to separate instructions from the firm list.
Due to context length limits in a single prompt-response pair, the 300 sample firms are divided into 10 batches of 30. The small batch size avoids length overflow and eliminates biases introduced by within-firm (cross-horizon) forecast interruptions and resume-prompt interference, thus allowing forecasts to proceed.
In certain text generation tasks, few-shot prompting or providing conversation examples can improve generation quality. By adopting zero-shot rather than few-shot prompting, the authors take a strictly neutral stance and avoid biasing ChatGPT with human forecast examples ($\color{blue}{Zhao\ et\ al.\ 2021}$). A new chat session is always started when moving to the next performance measure to ensure output is not influenced by prior instructions.
Another challenge is that ChatGPT may produce slightly different predictions even when given the same prompt multiple times. Fortunately, despite minor differences, results are highly similar and comparable ($\color{blue}{Ko\ and \ Lee,\ 2023}$). To mitigate generation idiosyncrasies, the paper independently repeats the process 35 times and takes the average5, yielding three-year forecasts across seven indicators for 300 companies.
Human analyst forecasts are obtained from the CSMAR database. To ensure comparable information sets between human analysts and ChatGPT, human forecasts are restricted to those issued before September 2021. Some reports provide forecasts for different time horizons. The authors obtain a total of 10,550 forecast-year observations from 582 analyst reports. Realised data come from CSMAR and Wind, but are only available for 2021 and 2022; 2023 data were not yet available at the time of writing.
3. Available at: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/gpt-best-practices. ↩
4:ChatGPT can provide reasons using human-like language on why it issues such forecasts along with the forecasted values, which basically include analyses of a firm’s fundamental outlook. In this paper, we keep the forecasted values only by requesting the table output format. ChatGPT可以使用类似人类的语言提供理由，说明它为什么发布这样的预测以及预测值，其中基本上包括对公司基本前景的分析。在本文中，我们仅通过请求表输出格式来保持预测值。 5:For instance, ChatGPT’s 35 forecast responses for the PE of Kweichow Moutai at the end of 2021 are 28.5, 39.2, 33.8, 34.8, 29.1, 32.1, 33.5, 34.2, 34.3, 34.5, 34.2, 33.2, 34.2, 34.6, 37.2, 35.2, 39.2, 27.4, 43.2, 39.2, 37.5, 32.8, 30.2, 42.3, 35.4, 30.5, 41.2, 34.6, 35.2, 36.7, 28.2, 35.2, 38.1, 38.2, and 40.1. We calculate the average, 35.1, as the final forecast.
$\color{purple}{表\ 1}$列出了ChatGPT预测、人类分析师预测以及真实值的汇总统计数据，每个指标分三行显示。
第一行包含 ChatGPT 的预测，包含 900 个观察值（3 年 × 每年 300 家公司）； 第二行包含人类分析师的预测； 最后一行包含已实现绩效的 600 个观察值（2 年 × 每年 300 家公司） 。 由于我们样本中的许多股票都有多个分析师团队跟踪，因此人类分析师对某一年的预测可能不止一个。因此，人类分析师预测的样本可能会超过 900 个。此外，分析师报告可能不包括所有七个指标，导致不同绩效指标的观察数量不平衡6。
$\color{purple}{表\ 1}$揭示了四种值得注意的模式:
首先，ChatGPT 的平均预测值和中值预测值在所有七项指标中均低于人类分析师的预测值; 其次，人类分析师始终表现出向上的偏见； 第三，除了PE的预测外，ChatGPT的预测值更接近实际值； 第四，ChatGPT的预测错误是双向的：高估了PB、EPS、ROE、收入增长和利润增长，但PE和ROA的预测值低于实现值； $\color{purple}{表\ 1}$中的汇总统计数据与我们的猜想一致，即ChatGPT通过减少乐观偏见而优于人类分析师。
$\color{purple}{Table\ 1}$ presents summary statistics of ChatGPT forecasts, human analyst forecasts, and realised values, with three rows per indicator.
The first row contains ChatGPT's forecasts with 900 observations (3 years x 300 firms per year); The second row contains human analyst forecasts; The last row contains 600 observations of realised performance (2 years x 300 firms per year). Since many stocks in the sample are covered by multiple analyst teams, there may be more than one human analyst forecast per stock-year. Consequently, the human analyst forecast sample may exceed 900. Additionally, analyst reports may not cover all seven indicators, leading to unbalanced observation counts across performance measures6.
$\color{purple}{Table\ 1}$ reveals four notable patterns:
First, ChatGPT's mean and median forecasts are lower than human analyst forecasts across all seven indicators; Second, human analysts consistently exhibit upward biases; Third, except for PE, ChatGPT's forecasts are closer to realised values; Fourth, ChatGPT's forecast errors are bidirectional: it overestimates PB, EPS, ROE, revenue growth, and profit growth, but underestimates PE and ROA; The summary statistics in $\color{purple}{Table\ 1}$ are consistent with the conjecture that ChatGPT outperforms human analysts by reducing optimistic biases.
上述结果将不同时间范围的预测汇总在一起，因此很难解释差异。
$\color{purple}{表\ 2}$：
对第 (1)-(3) 列中 ChatGPT 和人类分析师之间的每个指标和每个时间范围执行均值差 t 检验，发现有证据表明人类分析师比 ChatGPT 更乐观； 在第 (4) 和 (5) 列中，将人类分析师的预测与真实值进行比较； 只有 2021 年和 2022 年的结果，因为 2023 年的实现绩效数据尚未提供； 人类分析师显着高估了所有绩效指标，并且向上偏差的程度随着时间的推移而增加。 相比之下，ChatGPT 对 2021 年没有表现出任何乐观偏见（第 (6) 列）；除了略微低估的 ROA 和收入增长之外，其预测与五项指标的实际表现没有显着差异； 然而，ChatGPT 的准确性在 2022 年的长期范围内不会持续存在； 第 (7) 列中的预测误差在七个绩效指标中的六个中显着非零（PE 除外）； ChatGPT 高估了七项指标中的五项，但偏差比人类分析师的偏差要少得多；
The above results aggregate forecasts across different time horizons, making it difficult to interpret the differences.
$\color{purple}{Table\ 2}$:
Mean-difference t-tests are performed for each indicator and each time horizon between ChatGPT and human analysts in columns (1)-(3), finding evidence that human analysts are more optimistic than ChatGPT; Columns (4) and (5) compare human analyst forecasts with realised values; Only 2021 and 2022 results are available, as 2023 realised performance data were not yet available; Human analysts significantly overestimate all performance indicators, and the degree of upward bias increases over time; In contrast, ChatGPT exhibits no optimistic bias for 2021 (column (6)); aside from slightly underestimated ROA and revenue growth, its forecasts do not significantly differ from realised performance on five indicators; However, ChatGPT's accuracy does not persist in the longer 2022 horizon; Forecast errors in column (7) are significantly non-zero for six of seven performance indicators (PE being the exception); ChatGPT overestimates five of seven indicators, but the biases are much smaller than those of human analysts;
6. One potential problem in Table 1 is that it uses 2021–2023 forecasts from ChatGPT and human analysts, but only 2021–2022 data for realised performance. In unreported results, we exclude ChatGPT’s and human analysts’ forecasts for 2023 and find consistent patterns. ↩
Empirical Results 回归量化 ChatGPT 纠正的人类分析师的乐观偏见，使用 ChatGPT 和人类分析师对 2021 年和 2022 年预测的完整样本（即 2021 年底和 2022 年公司业绩的预测值）。因变量是向上预测误差，计算为预测 $E(y)$ 与真实值$y$之间的差异。解释变量是虚拟变量ChatGPT，表明预测由 ChatGPT 还是人类分析师发布。
包括一组与文献中记载的分析师预测误差相关的因素的控制变量。 $\color{blue}{Abarbanell\ (1991)}$表明，分析师的预测没有完全纳入过去的股票价格信息，并且效率不够，意味着价格上涨预示着向下偏差的预测误差。为了控制这种影响，将 2021年9月之前52周的年化收益率纳入其中。
不确定性会影响预测误差（Das、Levine 和 Sivaramakrishnan 1998；Lim 2001）。分析师倾向于发布有利于公司的预测，以换取管理团队的私人信息。继 Lim（2001）之后，我们使用 2021 年 9 月之前 52 周的年化波动率作为基于市场的不确定性控制。
分析师在预测长期业绩方面表现不佳（Harris 1999）。预测误差随着时间范围的增加而增加，这意味着分析师更难准确预测更遥远的未来的表现。参考Dong et al. (2021) 和 Bolliger (2004)，本文控制了预测范围。
随着公司的老化，市场会积累更多有关公司的信息。 Maskara and Mullineaux（2011）表明，预测误差和企业年龄都与信息不对称有关。参考 Amir、Lev and Sougiannis（2003），本文将公司年龄作为控制变量。
此外，所有权结构会影响分析师的预测误差（Ackert 和 Athanassakos，2003），特别是在中国资本市场的独特环境下（Huang 和 Wright，2015；Liu，2016），国有企业和非国有企业之间存在明显区别。 我们包括一个控制变量来表明公司是否国有。
最后，我们遵循 Ali、Klein and Rosenfeld (1992)、Cen、Hilary and Wei (2013)、So (2013) 和 Dong et al.（2021）的做法，纳入了滞后的企业特征指标。
The regression quantifies the optimistic biases of human analysts corrected by ChatGPT, using the full sample of ChatGPT and human analyst forecasts for 2021 and 2022 (i.e., forecasted values of year-end 2021 and 2022 firm performance). The dependent variable is the upward forecast error, calculated as the difference between the forecast $E(y)$ and the realised value $y$. The explanatory variable is a ChatGPT dummy indicating whether the forecast is issued by ChatGPT or a human analyst.
A set of control variables associated with analyst forecast errors documented in the literature is included. $\color{blue}{Abarbanell\ (1991)}$ showed that analysts' forecasts do not fully incorporate past stock price information and are inefficient, implying that price increases portend downward-biased forecast errors. To control for this, the 52-week annualised return prior to September 2021 is included.
Uncertainty affects forecast errors (Das, Levine and Sivaramakrishnan 1998; Lim 2001). Analysts tend to issue forecasts favourable to firms in exchange for private information from management teams. Following Lim (2001), the 52-week annualised volatility prior to September 2021 is used as a market-based uncertainty control.
Analysts perform poorly in forecasting long-term performance (Harris 1999). Forecast errors increase with the time horizon, meaning it is more difficult to accurately predict performance further into the future. Following Dong et al. (2021) and Bolliger (2004), the paper controls for forecast horizon.
As firms age, the market accumulates more information about them. Maskara and Mullineaux (2011) show that both forecast errors and firm age are related to information asymmetry. Following Amir, Lev and Sougiannis (2003), firm age is included as a control variable.
Furthermore, ownership structure affects analyst forecast errors (Ackert and Athanassakos 2003), particularly in the unique context of the Chinese capital market (Huang and Wright 2015; Liu 2016), where a clear distinction exists between SOEs and non-SOEs. A control variable indicating whether the firm is state-owned is included.
Finally, following Ali, Klein and Rosenfeld (1992), Cen, Hilary and Wei (2013), So (2013), and Dong et al. (2021), lagged firm characteristic indicators are included.
回归方程如下：
The regression equation is as follows:
E\big(y_{ijt}\big)-y_{ijt}=\beta_0+\beta_1ChatGPT_{ijt}+\beta_2^{\prime}\boldsymbol{z}_i+\varepsilon_{ijt} 其中，下标表示第$t$年的公司$i$和绩效指标$j$。
where the subscripts denote firm $i$ and performance indicator $j$ in year $t$.
$\color{purple}{表\ 3}$列出了横截面回归结果。相较人类分析师，ChatGPT 在七项指标中的四项（ROA、ROE、收入增长和利润增长）中表现出明显较少的乐观偏差。当因变量为 PE、PB 和 EPS 的向上预测误差时，ChatGPT 的系数也为负（但不显著），这些结果与表 2 中的均差$t$检验结果一致。有关控制变量的结果也符合预期。乐观偏差随着预测范围的增加而增加，并随着预测时的年化收益而减少，这与 Abarbanell (1991) 的发现是一致的。The optimistic biases increase with the forecast horizon and decrease with the annualised return at the time when the forecast is made, which is consistent with the finding of Abarbanell (Citation1991).
$\color{purple}{Table\ 3}$ reports the cross-sectional regression results. Compared with human analysts, ChatGPT exhibits significantly less optimistic bias in four of seven indicators (ROA, ROE, revenue growth, and profit growth). The ChatGPT coefficient is also negative (but insignificant) when the dependent variable is the upward forecast error for PE, PB, and EPS. These results are consistent with the mean-difference $t$-test results in Table 2. Control variable results are also as expected. Optimistic biases increase with the forecast horizon and decrease with the annualised return at the time the forecast is made, consistent with Abarbanell (Citation1991).
受Adiwardana et al. （2020）提出了样本排序方法的启发，本文通过选择置信度最高的预测进行稳健性测试以解决 ChatGPT 预测中的随机性。在样本排序方法中，LLM 选择具有最高预测概率的候选文本序列作为最终输出。遵循同样的思路，选择 35 个响应的中位数作为 ChatGPT 的最终预测。
$\color{purple}{表\ 4}$中报告了稳健性检验，结果优于$\color{purple}{表\ 3}$，可观察到 ChatGPT 在七个维度中的六个维度上减少了人类分析师的乐观偏见，PE 是唯一的例外。
Inspired by the sample ranking method proposed by Adiwardana et al. (2020), the paper conducts a robustness test by selecting the highest-confidence forecasts to address randomness in ChatGPT's predictions. In the sample ranking method, the LLM selects the candidate text sequence with the highest predicted probability as the final output. Following the same logic, the median of the 35 responses is selected as ChatGPT's final forecast.
$\color{purple}{Table\ 4}$ reports the robustness test results, which are stronger than $\color{purple}{Table\ 3}$: ChatGPT reduces human analysts' optimistic biases in six of seven dimensions, with PE being the only exception.
Conclusion 本文利用 ChatGPT（LLM）来预测CSI 300 指数公司的业绩，并将其预测与 2021 年 9 月发布的人类分析师的预测进行比较，该日期恰逢 ChatGPT 训练集截止日期。通过使用真实值作为基准，发现 ChatGPT 的表现稳定优于人类分析师，实现更小的向上预测误差。人类分析师倾向于提供乐观的预测，而 ChatGPT 则更为保守。 ChatGPT 预测的卓越准确性可归因于它能够纠正人类分析师预测中固有的乐观偏差。
我们认为 ChatGPT 等 LLM 应用程序并不意味着从根本上取代人类金融分析师。相反，ChatGPT 可以协助和改进人力财务预测。在本文中，我们提供了 ChatGPT 预测财务业绩和减少人类乐观偏见能力的证据，这表明 ChatGPT 具有协助分析师和投资者的潜力。 ChatGPT 有望减少人类分析师的过度自信或利益冲突。需要注意的是，我们警告不要过度扩展我们的结果，因为投资者不应仅仅依赖 ChatGPT，也不应将其预测用作"正确"答案。 ChatGPT 平均优于人类分析师的结果并不意味着它总是比人类分析师更准确。我们将我们的发现解释为证明了 ChatGPT 在补充人类分析师和投资者预测方面的价值。由于本文是揭示 ChatGPT 和人类分析师之间预测差异的首次尝试，我们鼓励未来的研究人员深入探讨这些差异的原因。
这项研究有一定的局限性，这也给我们的读者敲响了警钟。首先，分析仅涵盖了短期的两年预测；因此，在考虑广泛的市场动态时，这是不够的。因此，需要更多证据来证明 ChatGPT 跨长周期的预测性能。较短的时间跨度引发了人们对我们研究结果的普遍性的担忧，特别是当市场条件发生变化时。随着数据可用性的增加，未来的研究人员应该将 ChatGPT 的预测与人类分析师在更长的历史跨度上的预测进行比较。
其次，由于 LLMs 的黑匣子性质，我们对 ChatGPT 进行财务预测并以比人类更少的乐观偏差来提供预测的内部流程知之甚少。可能的渠道可能包括 ChatGPT 处理基本信息和综合信念的卓越能力和/或其与人类相比更高的公正性。这一限制表明了未来研究的另一个方向，即利用新颖的研究设计并揭示 ChatGPT 的内部机制，通过该机制可以减少乐观偏差的预测。
总之，本文提供了 ChatGPT（越来越多的 LLMs 之一）在预测上市公司业绩方面的应用的经验证据，并强调了 ChatGPT 在提供财务建议方面的潜力。此外，我们的结果阐明了 LLMs 在减轻金融决策中的人类偏见方面的作用。展望未来，未来的研究人员可能会考虑探索 LLMs 在金融领域的其他应用，并研究其在各种决策环境中的有效性。
This paper uses ChatGPT (an LLM) to forecast the performance of CSI 300 index companies and compares its predictions with those of human analysts issued as of September 2021, coinciding with ChatGPT's training data cutoff. Using realised values as benchmarks, ChatGPT consistently outperforms human analysts with smaller upward forecast errors. Human analysts tend to provide optimistic forecasts, while ChatGPT is more conservative. ChatGPT's superior forecast accuracy is attributable to its ability to correct the optimistic biases inherent in human analyst forecasts.
The authors argue that LLM applications such as ChatGPT are not intended to fundamentally replace human financial analysts. Rather, ChatGPT can assist and improve human financial forecasting. This paper provides evidence of ChatGPT's ability to forecast financial performance and reduce human optimistic biases, suggesting its potential to assist analysts and investors. ChatGPT may reduce human analysts' overconfidence or conflicts of interest. However, the authors caution against over-extending these results: investors should not rely solely on ChatGPT or treat its predictions as "correct" answers. The finding that ChatGPT outperforms human analysts on average does not imply it is always more accurate. The findings are interpreted as demonstrating ChatGPT's value in complementing human analysts' and investors' forecasts. As this paper is an initial attempt to reveal forecast differences between ChatGPT and human analysts, future researchers are encouraged to explore the underlying reasons for these differences.
This study has certain limitations. First, the analysis covers only a short two-year forecast period, which is insufficient when considering broader market dynamics. More evidence is needed regarding ChatGPT's forecasting performance across longer cycles. The short time span raises concerns about the generalisability of the findings, particularly when market conditions change. Future researchers should compare ChatGPT's forecasts with those of human analysts over longer historical spans as data availability increases.
Second, due to the black-box nature of LLMs, little is known about the internal processes by which ChatGPT generates financial forecasts with less optimistic bias than humans. Possible channels may include ChatGPT's superior ability to process fundamental information and synthesise beliefs, and/or its greater impartiality compared to humans. This limitation suggests another direction for future research: employing novel research designs to uncover ChatGPT's internal mechanisms through which it produces forecasts with reduced optimistic bias.
In summary, this paper provides empirical evidence on the application of ChatGPT (one of an increasing number of LLMs) in forecasting the performance of listed companies and highlights ChatGPT's potential in providing financial advice. Furthermore, the results illuminate the role of LLMs in mitigating human biases in financial decision-making. Looking ahead, future researchers may consider exploring other applications of LLMs in finance and examining their effectiveness in various decision-making contexts.
References Ko, H., & Lee, J. (2023). Can chatgpt improve investment decision? from a portfolio management perspective. From a portfolio management perspective. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4390529. Boyacı, T., Canyakmaz, C., & de Véricourt, F. (2024). Human and machine: The impact of machine input on decision making under cognitive limitations. Management Science, 70(2), 1258-1275. Chen, X., Cho, Y. H., Dou, Y., & Lev, B. (2022). Human versus machine: A comparison of robo-analyst and traditional research analyst investment recommendations. The Accounting Review, 97(5), 221-244. Kim, A. G., Muhn, M., & Nikolaev, V. V. (2023). Bloated disclosures: can ChatGPT help investors process information?. Chicago Booth Research Paper, (23-07). https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4425527. Xie, Q., Han, W., Lai, Y., Peng, M., & Huang, J. (2023). The wall street neophyte: A zero-shot analysis of chatgpt over multimodal stock movement prediction challenges. arXiv preprint arXiv:2304.05351. Ko, H., & Lee, J. (2023). Can chatgpt improve investment decision? from a portfolio management perspective. From a portfolio management perspective. Available at SSRN 4390529. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4390529. Chen, Z., Zheng, L. N., Lu, C., Yuan, J., & Zhu, D. (2023). ==ChatGPT informed graph neural network for stock movement prediction==. arXiv preprint arXiv:2306.03763. Hou, Y., & Li, S. (2014). The impact of the CSI 300 stock index futures: Positive feedback trading and autocorrelation of stock returns. International Review of Economics & Finance, 33, 319-337. M Alshater, M. (2022). Exploring the role of artificial intelligence in enhancing academic performance: A case study of ChatGPT. Available at SSRN 4312358. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4312358. Korinek, A. (2023). Language models and cognitive automation for economic research (No. w30957). National Bureau of Economic Research. https://www.nber.org/papers/w30957. Zhao, Z., Wallace, E., Feng, S., Klein, D., & Singh, S. (2021, July). Calibrate before use: Improving few-shot performance of language models. In International conference on machine learning (pp. 12697-12706). PMLR. http://proceedings.mlr.press/v139/zhao21c.html. Abarbanell, J. S. (1991). Do analysts’ earnings forecasts incorporate information in prior stock price changes?. Journal of Accounting and Economics, 14(2), 147-165.

Tech | Stanford and UW Researchers Train a Powerful AI Reasoning Model for Under $50
entry.content:
2025年1月31日，斯坦福大学和华盛顿大学的AI研究团队发布了一篇引发行业轰动的研究论文，他们成功以不到50美元的训练成本打造了一个高性能AI推理模型——s1，其在数学推理和编程能力测试中的表现，足以媲美当前最前沿的推理模型，如 OpenAI 的 o1 和 DeepSeek 的 R1！
On January 31, 2025, AI researchers from Stanford University and the University of Washington published a groundbreaking paper. They successfully built a high-performance AI reasoning model — s1 — for a training cost of under $50. Its performance on mathematical reasoning and coding benchmarks rivals the most advanced reasoning models available, such as OpenAI's o1 and DeepSeek's R1.
更令人瞩目的是，s1 已在 GitHub 上开源，不仅提供了完整的训练代码和数据，还为学术界、开源社区乃至 AI 初创公司提供了低成本构建高性能 AI 模型的新范式。这不仅是 AI 研究的一次突破，更可能引发推理模型开发的"平民化革命"。
Even more remarkably, s1 has been open-sourced on GitHub, providing complete training code and data. It offers academia, the open-source community, and AI startups a new paradigm for building high-performance AI models at low cost. This is not just a breakthrough in AI research — it could spark a democratization revolution in reasoning model development.
这一成果表明，先进的推理能力不再是科技巨头的专属，通过高效的训练方法与创新的模型优化，即便是资源有限的研究团队，也能在 AI 竞赛中占据一席之地。s1 的成功，让人不禁思考：未来的 AI 发展，是否还依赖巨额算力投入，还是会向更加开放、普惠的方向演进？
This achievement demonstrates that advanced reasoning capabilities are no longer exclusive to tech giants. Through efficient training methods and innovative model optimization, even resource-limited research teams can compete in the AI race. The success of s1 raises an important question: will future AI development continue to depend on massive computational investments, or will it evolve toward a more open and accessible direction?
工作论文：
Research Paper:
Github仓库：
GitHub Repository:
Technical Breakthrough: Distillation and Fine-Tuning for Low-Cost Replication of Frontier Reasoning s1 模型的研究团队采用了创新性的"蒸馏"技术，从 Google 的推理模型——Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 中提取推理能力，并通过精细化的监督微调（SFT） 使其适应新的任务环境。相比于传统的大规模预训练+强化学习方法，这种技术路径显著降低了计算成本，同时保留了强大的逻辑推理能力。
The s1 research team employed an innovative "distillation" technique, extracting reasoning capabilities from Google's reasoning model — Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental — and adapting them to new tasks through supervised fine-tuning (SFT). Compared to traditional large-scale pretraining + reinforcement learning approaches, this technical path significantly reduces computational costs while preserving strong logical reasoning capabilities.
更令人惊讶的是，整个训练过程仅耗时不到 30 分钟，并且在 16 台 Nvidia H100 GPU 上完成，这意味着在极短的时间内，研究团队成功构建了一个能够媲美 OpenAI o1 和 DeepSeek R1 的推理模型。这一成果不仅证明了蒸馏技术的可行性，也展示了如何以极低的成本实现高性能 AI 推理，为学术界和开源社区提供了全新的研究范式。
Even more surprisingly, the entire training process took less than 30 minutes on 16 Nvidia H100 GPUs, meaning that in a remarkably short time, the team built a reasoning model comparable to OpenAI o1 and DeepSeek R1. This result not only proves the viability of distillation but also demonstrates how to achieve high-performance AI reasoning at minimal cost, offering a new research paradigm for academia and the open-source community.
此外，研究人员还采用了巧妙的优化策略，例如在推理过程中引入"wait"机制，让模型在回答问题前"思考"更久，以提高答案的准确性。这种方法突破了传统推理模型的速度-精度权衡，使得 s1 在多个 AI 基准测试中表现优异。
Additionally, the researchers adopted clever optimization strategies, such as introducing a "wait" mechanism during inference, allowing the model to "think" longer before answering to improve accuracy. This approach breaks the traditional speed-accuracy tradeoff in reasoning models, enabling s1 to perform exceptionally well across multiple AI benchmarks.
这一突破性进展不仅让低成本 AI 训练成为可能，也引发了关于 AI 训练范式转变的深远讨论——当蒸馏和微调技术逐渐成熟，是否意味着算力和数据不再是 AI 进步的唯一壁垒？ 未来，依靠更高效的训练方法和优化策略，是否能催生一波"轻量级高性能 AI" 的浪潮？
This breakthrough not only makes low-cost AI training possible but also sparks profound discussions about a paradigm shift in AI training — as distillation and fine-tuning techniques mature, does this mean compute and data are no longer the sole barriers to AI progress? Could more efficient training methods and optimization strategies give rise to a wave of "lightweight, high-performance AI"?
A Low-Cost “AI Revolution”: Are AI Moats Disappearing? 与传统的高成本、大规模训练方法不同，s1 的训练成本极为低廉。研究人员仅使用1000 个精心挑选的问题构建训练数据，并结合监督微调（SFT） 技术，使得这一推理模型具备了媲美最前沿 AI 推理系统的能力。整个训练过程在 16 台 Nvidia H100 GPU 上仅运行了不到 30 分钟，成本不足 50 美元，这一突破无疑挑战了 AI 领域"算力决定一切"的传统认知。
Unlike traditional high-cost, large-scale training methods, s1's training cost is remarkably low. Researchers used only 1,000 carefully selected problems to build the training data, combined with supervised fine-tuning (SFT), enabling this reasoning model to achieve capabilities rivaling frontier AI reasoning systems. The entire training process ran for less than 30 minutes on 16 Nvidia H100 GPUs at a cost under $50, undoubtedly challenging the conventional belief that "compute determines everything" in AI.
相较于 DeepSeek 依靠数百万美元的算力进行强化学习训练，s1 的低成本策略不仅打破了"资金壁垒"，更让 AI 研究从资源密集型产业迈向更开放、更可复制的阶段。这种模式让更多学术研究机构、中小企业乃至个人开发者都能低成本进入 AI 竞赛，从而加速 AI 技术的普及化。
Compared to DeepSeek's reliance on millions of dollars in compute for reinforcement learning training, s1's low-cost strategy not only breaks the "funding barrier" but also moves AI research from a resource-intensive industry toward a more open and reproducible stage. This model allows more academic institutions, small businesses, and individual developers to enter the AI race at low cost, accelerating the popularization of AI technology.
然而，这一趋势也引发了行业对 AI "商品化" 的深刻讨论。如果一个价值数百万美元的大模型可以通过低成本蒸馏技术被高效复刻，AI 产业的"护城河"究竟在哪里？ 未来，AI 是否会像云计算或开源软件一样，从封闭式竞争走向更加模块化、低成本的开放生态？或者，科技巨头是否会通过数据垄断、模型封闭化等方式，进一步巩固自身的领先地位？
However, this trend also triggers deep industry discussions about AI "commoditization". If a multi-million-dollar large model can be efficiently replicated through low-cost distillation, where exactly are AI's "moats"? Will AI follow the path of cloud computing or open-source software, shifting from closed competition toward a more modular, low-cost, open ecosystem? Or will tech giants further consolidate their lead through data monopolies and model lock-in?
s1 的成功无疑给 AI 产业带来了新的思考。是资本堆砌的 AI 时代仍将主导未来，还是更加去中心化、普惠化的 AI 体系将逐渐崛起？ 这一低成本革命，或许才刚刚开始。
The success of s1 undoubtedly brings new thinking to the AI industry. Will the capital-heavy AI era continue to dominate, or will a more decentralized and accessible AI ecosystem gradually emerge? This low-cost revolution may only be just beginning.
Challenges and Reflections Behind the Innovation 尽管s1的创新令人振奋，但也引发了大AI实验室的关注。OpenAI 近期指控 DeepSeek 滥用其 API 进行模型蒸馏，尽管这一指控与 s1 无关，但AI行业对蒸馏技术的争议也因此升温。DeepSeek的做法和s1的技术路径完全不同，但由于两者都涉及到AI推理模型的蒸馏，OpenAI的指控给整个AI领域的模型蒸馏技术带来了更大的关注。
While s1's innovation is exciting, it has also drawn attention from major AI labs. OpenAI recently accused DeepSeek of misusing its API for model distillation. Although this accusation is unrelated to s1, it has intensified the debate around distillation techniques in the AI industry. DeepSeek's approach and s1's technical path are entirely different, but since both involve distillation of AI reasoning models, OpenAI's accusation has brought greater scrutiny to model distillation across the entire AI field.
Google 明确禁止对其 Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental 进行逆向工程，以开发与其自家 AI 竞争的产品。尽管 s1 研究团队通过 API 获取数据，并未涉及逆向工程，但这一规定仍引发关于 AI 训练数据边界的讨论。
Google explicitly prohibits reverse-engineering its Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking Experimental to develop products that compete with its own AI. Although the s1 research team obtained data through the API and did not engage in reverse engineering, this policy still raises discussions about the boundaries of AI training data usage.
The Future of AI: Relying on Massive Investment, or Moving Toward Accessible Intelligence? 尽管蒸馏技术证明了低成本也能复制强大推理能力，但 AI 领域的核心竞争仍然掌握在算力、数据和算法优化等关键要素之上。Meta、Google、Microsoft 等科技巨头计划在 2025 年投入数千亿美元用于 AI 基础设施建设，进一步优化大模型的训练效率，推动更复杂、更智能的 AI 解决方案。这种资本密集型的投入仍然是当前 AI 产业创新的主流路径，而算力资源的垄断也让这些企业在 AI 竞争中保持着天然优势。
Although distillation has proven that powerful reasoning can be replicated at low cost, core competition in AI still revolves around key factors such as compute, data, and algorithm optimization. Tech giants like Meta, Google, and Microsoft plan to invest hundreds of billions of dollars in AI infrastructure in 2025, further optimizing large model training efficiency and driving more complex, intelligent AI solutions. This capital-intensive approach remains the mainstream path for AI innovation, and the monopolization of compute resources gives these companies a natural advantage.
然而，s1 的成功正在改变这一传统认知。它表明，即便没有大规模资金支持，依靠更优化的训练方法、合理的数据选择和算法改进，同样可以取得接近最前沿模型的推理能力。这一突破可能为学术研究、开源社区和中小型 AI 创企提供新的发展方向，让 AI 技术的应用不再局限于少数资本雄厚的企业，而是逐步迈向去中心化、开放共享的创新模式。
However, s1's success is changing this conventional wisdom. It shows that even without large-scale funding, relying on better-optimized training methods, smart data selection, and algorithmic improvements can achieve reasoning capabilities approaching frontier models. This breakthrough could provide new directions for academic research, open-source communities, and small AI startups, moving AI applications beyond a few well-funded corporations toward a decentralized, open-sharing innovation model.
但与此同时，低成本 AI 模型的发展也带来了新的挑战——模型知识产权如何界定？训练数据是否合规？算力成本降低是否会导致 AI 滥用问题？ 这些问题将在未来几年成为业界关注的焦点。科技巨头的巨额投资仍然在引领 AI 的前沿突破，而开源社区和学术界的技术创新则在推动 AI 的平民化和普惠化。未来，AI 产业可能会形成高投入大模型与低成本开源模型并存的双轨发展格局，既有资本驱动的超大规模 AI 竞赛，也有面向大众的轻量级 AI 生态。
At the same time, the development of low-cost AI models brings new challenges — How should model intellectual property be defined? Is training data usage compliant? Could reduced compute costs lead to AI misuse? These questions will become focal points for the industry in the coming years. Tech giants' massive investments continue to drive frontier AI breakthroughs, while technical innovations from open-source communities and academia push AI toward democratization and accessibility. In the future, the AI industry may form a dual-track development pattern where high-investment large models coexist with low-cost open-source models — featuring both capital-driven ultra-large-scale AI races and lightweight AI ecosystems for the masses.
AI 的未来不只是巨头的游戏，更可能是一场由全球研究者共同推动的技术变革。
The future of AI is not just a game for giants — it may well be a technological transformation driven collectively by researchers worldwide.
Conclusion s1 的成功不仅标志着 AI 推理模型的一次突破，更揭示了低成本 AI 研究的巨大潜力。过去，高性能 AI 推理模型的训练往往需要数百万美元的算力支出，使得这一领域长期被大型科技公司主导。然而，s1 研究团队证明，借助巧妙的算法优化、有效的数据选择和高效的训练策略，即便是在资源受限的情况下，也能复刻甚至接近最前沿的推理能力。
The success of s1 marks not only a breakthrough in AI reasoning models but also reveals the enormous potential of low-cost AI research. Previously, training high-performance AI reasoning models typically required millions of dollars in compute, keeping this field dominated by large tech companies. However, the s1 team proved that with clever algorithm optimization, effective data selection, and efficient training strategies, frontier-level reasoning capabilities can be replicated even under resource constraints.
这一研究不仅为学术界和开源社区提供了重要的借鉴，也可能为 AI 产业带来深远影响。随着模型蒸馏和监督微调（SFT）等技术的成熟，未来或许会涌现更多类似 s1 的开源推理模型，推动 AI 技术的民主化、平民化和普惠化。这意味着，不仅是资金雄厚的大型科技公司，高校、研究机构乃至个人开发者都有机会在 AI 推理领域取得突破，从而进一步加速 AI 研究和应用的创新步伐。
This research provides important references for academia and the open-source community and could have far-reaching impacts on the AI industry. As techniques like model distillation and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) mature, more open-source reasoning models similar to s1 may emerge, driving the democratization and accessibility of AI technology. This means not only well-funded tech companies but also universities, research institutions, and individual developers will have opportunities to achieve breakthroughs in AI reasoning, further accelerating the pace of AI research and application innovation.
然而，低成本模型的兴起也引发了一系列挑战。AI 技术的知识产权、数据使用边界、算法安全性等问题正变得愈发突出。s1 的问世让人重新思考，如果昂贵的专有模型能够被低成本复刻，AI 产业的商业模式是否会发生根本性变革？ 大公司依靠海量数据和算力建立的护城河是否依然坚固？监管机构是否会介入 AI 训练数据的使用？这些问题仍然有待时间和市场的进一步检验。
However, the rise of low-cost models also raises a series of challenges. Issues such as AI intellectual property, data usage boundaries, and algorithm safety are becoming increasingly prominent. The advent of s1 prompts us to reconsider: if expensive proprietary models can be replicated at low cost, will the AI industry's business model undergo fundamental change? Are the moats built by large companies through massive data and compute still solid? Will regulators intervene in AI training data usage? These questions still await further testing by time and the market.
未来，s1 的成功或许只是一个开端。当计算资源的获取成本持续下降，当算法优化能力不断提升，"高性能 AI ≠ 高昂成本" 可能成为行业共识。在这一变革浪潮中，开源社区、学术机构和创业团队或将迎来前所未有的创新机遇，而 AI 产业的竞争格局也可能因这一波低成本革命而被重新定义。
Looking ahead, s1's success may be just the beginning. As the cost of accessing compute continues to fall and algorithmic optimization capabilities keep improving, "high-performance AI ≠ high cost" could become an industry consensus. In this wave of transformation, open-source communities, academic institutions, and startup teams may encounter unprecedented innovation opportunities, and the competitive landscape of the AI industry could be redefined by this low-cost revolution.
References s1: Simple test-time scaling https://github.com/simplescaling/s1 Researchers created an open rival to OpenAI’s o1 ‘reasoning’ model for under $50

entry.title: Draft | Can ChatGPT forecast stock price movements?
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover Source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：本文研究了ChatGPT和其他大型语言模型在使用新闻标题预测股市收益方面的潜力。通过ChatGPT判断新闻标题对公司股价所产生的积极、中性和消极作用，产生的得分与随后的每日股票收益之间显著正相关。尽管ChatGPT优于传统的情绪分析方法，然而GPT-1、GPT-2和BERT等更基本的模型无法准确预测收益，基于ChatGPT-4的长短期策略可获得最高的夏普比率，这说明收益的可预测性是复杂语言模型的一种新兴能力。此外，小盘股和大盘股均具有可预测性，这表明市场对公司新闻的反应不足。较小的股票和有坏消息的股票的可预测性更强，这与套利限制也起着重要作用的观点一致。总之，将先进大语言模型纳入投资决策更精准预测，并提高量化交易策略的性能。
Abstract: This paper investigates the potential of ChatGPT and other large language models in predicting stock market returns using news headlines. The scores produced by ChatGPT, which assess whether headlines have positive, neutral, or negative effects on stock prices, are significantly and positively correlated with subsequent daily stock returns. While ChatGPT outperforms traditional sentiment analysis methods, more basic models such as GPT-1, GPT-2, and BERT fail to predict returns accurately. The long-short strategy based on ChatGPT-4 achieves the highest Sharpe ratio, suggesting that return predictability is an emergent capability of sophisticated language models. Moreover, predictability exists for both small-cap and large-cap stocks, indicating market underreaction to firm-specific news. Predictability is stronger for smaller stocks and those with bad news, consistent with limits-to-arbitrage playing an important role. Overall, incorporating advanced LLMs into investment decisions enables more accurate predictions and enhances quantitative trading strategy performance.
引用：Lopez-Lira, A., & Tang, Y. (2023). Can chatgpt forecast stock price movements? return predictability and large language models. arXiv preprint arXiv:2304.07619. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2304.07619.
Citation: Lopez-Lira, A., & Tang, Y. (2023). Can chatgpt forecast stock price movements? return predictability and large language models. arXiv preprint arXiv:2304.07619. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2304.07619.
文献：Paper: Introduction 有限套利或称套利限制是指由于风险、成本、信息不对称和制度性约束等因素的影响，使得套利者的套利行为受到限制。 现有研究发现，套利限制会影响投资者的交易活动，使更多错误定价信息融入到交易中去，进而导致资产价格偏离其基本价值，加剧市场的错误定价和价格波动（Lam和Wei，2011；Gu等，2018）。
Limits-to-arbitrage refers to constraints on arbitrageurs' activities due to risk, cost, information asymmetry, and institutional restrictions. Existing research finds that these constraints affect investors' trading activities, allowing more mispricing to persist, causing asset prices to deviate from fundamental values and exacerbating market mispricing and price volatility (Lam and Wei, 2011; Gu et al., 2018).
金融经济领域，尤其是预测股市收益能力，使用LLMs仍然是一个相对未知的领域。LLMs预测金融市场走势表现如何是一个悬而未决的问题，本研究旨在通过LLMs提取新闻标题中的上下文预测股票收益方面的潜力填补空白。
The use of LLMs in financial economics, particularly for predicting stock market returns, remains relatively unexplored. How well LLMs predict financial market movements is an open question, and this study aims to fill this gap by exploring the potential of LLMs to extract contextual information from news headlines for predicting stock returns.
如$\color{blue}{Tetlock(2007)}$、$\color{blue}{Tetlock\ et\ al.(2008)}$ 和 $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2011)}$ 使用新闻和训练的算法成功预测股票收益，可能是因为组合新信息很复杂（$\color{blue}{Fedyk\ and\ Hodson,2023}$）。
Prior studies such as $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2007)}$, $\color{blue}{Tetlock\ et\ al.(2008)}$, and $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2011)}$ successfully predicted stock returns using news and trained algorithms, possibly because combining new information is complex ($\color{blue}{Fedyk\ and\ Hodson,2023}$).
具体做法：
本文通过利用大语言模型分析新闻标题情绪，比较包括ChatGPT在内的各种LLM的性能，并将其与领先数据供应商提供的现有情绪分析方法进行比较； 对于每个标题，使用ChatGPT来判断其对公司股价的作用是'good'、'bad'还是'neutral'，并转换为分数预测下一个交易日的股票收益；
Approach:
This paper analyzes news headline sentiment using LLMs, compares the performance of various LLMs including ChatGPT, and benchmarks them against existing sentiment analysis methods from leading data vendors; For each headline, ChatGPT is used to determine whether its effect on the company's stock price is 'good', 'bad', or 'neutral', and the result is converted into a score to predict the next trading day's stock return;
实证发现：
ChatGPT优于传统情绪分析方法； GPT-1、GPT-2和BERT等更基本的模型预测股票的能力较弱，ChatGPT-4观察到最高的可预测性； 样本期内ChatGPT-4获得最高的夏普比率3.8，而基于ChatGPT-3.5策略的夏普比率为3.1； ChatGPT可预测性存在于小盘股和大盘股以及具有积极和消极消息的股票中，市场似乎对公司新闻反应不足，这与现有文献中的观点一致； 可预测性在小盘股中更为明显；
Empirical Findings:
ChatGPT outperforms traditional sentiment analysis methods; More basic models such as GPT-1, GPT-2, and BERT exhibit weaker predictive ability, while ChatGPT-4 demonstrates the highest predictability; ChatGPT-4 achieves the highest Sharpe ratio of 3.8 during the sample period, compared to 3.1 for ChatGPT-3.5; ChatGPT predictability exists for both small-cap and large-cap stocks and for stocks with positive and negative news, suggesting market underreaction to firm-specific news, consistent with existing literature; Predictability is more pronounced among small-cap stocks;
进一步工作：
根据以上发现，本研究提出一种新方法估计并理解模型的推理能力。利用模型解释其推理的能力，步骤如下：
首先，通过将判断结果（不包括"UNKNOWN"）与第二天的已实现收益进行比较检验判断是否正确； 其次，拟合模型，预测判断正确与否。 第三，利用模型的特征重要性（$\color{blue}{Binsbergen\ et\ al.,2023}$）理解更能准确预测判断的概念； - 当判断与stock purchases by insiders，earning guidance，dividends有关时，更可能正确； - 当判断涉及partnerships和developments时，判断准确性降低；
Further Work:
Based on these findings, this study proposes a novel method to estimate and understand the model's reasoning capabilities. Leveraging the model's ability to explain its reasoning, the steps are as follows:
First, verify whether the judgment is correct by comparing the result (excluding "UNKNOWN") with the next day's realized returns; Second, fit a model to predict whether the judgment is correct; Third, use feature importance ($\color{blue}{Binsbergen\ et\ al.,2023}$) to understand which concepts best predict judgment accuracy; - Judgments related to stock purchases by insiders, earnings guidance, and dividends are more likely to be correct; - Judgments involving partnerships and developments tend to be less accurate;
贡献：
帮助监管机构和政策制定者了解金融市场越来越多使用LLMs的潜在优势及风险； 为资产管理公司和机构投资者提供LLMs在预测股市收益方面的有效性的实证证据； 有助于更广泛地讨论人工智能在金融中的应用；
Contributions:
Helps regulators and policymakers understand the potential advantages and risks of increasing LLM usage in financial markets; Provides asset management firms and institutional investors with empirical evidence on the effectiveness of LLMs in predicting stock market returns; Contributes to the broader discussion on the application of artificial intelligence in finance;
Literature ReviewInstitutional BackgroundData 日度收益率：股价、交易量和市值数据（Center for Research in Security Prices (CRSP)）
新闻标题
RavenPack
数据
周期：2021年10月-2022年12月（as ChatGPT's training data stops in September 2021）【This ensures that our $\color{red}{evaluation\ is\ based\ on\ information\ not\ present\ in\ the\ model's\ training\ data}$, allowing for a more accurate assessment of its predictive capabilities.】 样本包括在纽约证券交易所（NYSE）、全国证券交易商协会自动报价系统（NASDAQ）和美国证券交易所（AMEX）上市的所有股票，至少有一篇新闻报道被主要新闻媒体或新闻专线报道； 分析重点放在股票代码为10或11的普通股上；
Daily Returns: Stock price, volume, and market capitalization data (Center for Research in Security Prices (CRSP))
News Headlines
RavenPack
Data
Period: October 2021 to December 2022 (as ChatGPT's training data stops in September 2021). This ensures that the $\color{red}{evaluation\ is\ based\ on\ information\ not\ present\ in\ the\ model's\ training\ data}$, allowing for a more accurate assessment of its predictive capabilities. The sample includes all stocks listed on the NYSE, NASDAQ, and AMEX with at least one news article covered by major news outlets or newswires; The analysis focuses on common stocks with share codes 10 or 11;
数据清洗
首先，使用网络抓取为所有CRSP公司收集一个全面新闻数据集； - 搜索所有包含公司名称或股票代码的新闻，由此产生的数据集包括来自各种来源的新闻标题，如主要新闻机构、财经新闻网站和社交媒体平台； - 收集样本期内的所有新闻； 然后，将标题与知名新闻情绪分析数据提供商（RavenPack）的标题进行匹配【其后段落重点介绍与RavenPack匹配的重要意义】： - 大多数（超过70%）匹配的标题对应于新闻稿； - 但不使用RavenPack增强标题； - 参考$\color{blue}{Jiang\ et\ al.\ (2021)}$预处理方法处理合并后的数据集，匹配了4138家公司的67586个头条新闻； 使用数据供应商的"相关性得分"，范围从0到100，指示新闻与特定公司的相关性 - 仅采用相关性得分为100的新闻； - 限制为完整的文章和新闻稿，并排除归类为"股票上涨"和"股票下跌"的标题 【only indicate the daily stock movement direction】； - 为了避免新闻重复，要求"事件相似性天数"超过90天，这确保只捕获有关公司的新信息； - 剔除同一家公司一天内的重复和过于相似的标题：使用最优字符串对齐度量（受限Damerau-Levenstein距离）衡量标题的相似性，删除同一公司一天内相似性大于0.6的标题；
Data Cleaning
First, web scraping is used to collect a comprehensive news dataset for all CRSP firms; - All news articles containing the company name or ticker symbol are searched, yielding headlines from various sources including major news agencies, financial news websites, and social media platforms; - All news during the sample period is collected; Then, headlines are matched with those from a leading news sentiment data provider (RavenPack): - The majority (over 70%) of matched headlines correspond to press releases; - RavenPack-enhanced headlines are not used; - Following the preprocessing method of $\color{blue}{Jiang\ et\ al.\ (2021)}$, the merged dataset contains 67,586 headlines for 4,138 firms; The data provider's "relevance score" (0 to 100) indicating news relevance to a specific firm is used: - Only news with a relevance score of 100 is retained; - Restricted to full articles and press releases, excluding headlines classified as "stock rises" and "stock falls" (which only indicate the daily stock movement direction); - To avoid news duplication, "event similarity days" exceeding 90 days is required, ensuring only new information about firms is captured; - Duplicate and overly similar headlines for the same firm on the same day are removed using an optimal string alignment metric (restricted Damerau-Levenshtein distance), deleting headlines with similarity greater than 0.6;
$\color{purple}{Panel A}$表明新闻标题总体上正面；$\color{purple}{Panel B}$报告了变量的相关矩阵，GPT得分与事件情绪得分之间的相关性较低，小于0.28。
$\color{purple}{Panel A}$ indicates that news headlines are generally positive; $\color{purple}{Panel B}$ reports the correlation matrix of variables, showing low correlation (less than 0.28) between GPT scores and event sentiment scores.
MethodologyPrompt 使用以下提示，并将其应用于公开新闻标题：
The following prompt is used and applied to public news headlines:
Forget all your previous instructions. Pretend you are a financial expert. You are a -financial expert with stock recommendation experience. Answer "YES" if good news, "NO" if bad news, or "UNKNOWN" if uncertain in the first line. Then elaborate with one short and concise sentence on the next line. Is this headline good or bad for the stock price of company name in the term term?Headline: _headline_
如果消息对股价为正面影响，则要求ChatGPT回答"YES"，如果负面消息则回答"NO"，如果不确定则回答"UNKNOWN"；
相对应，"YES"得分为1，"NO"得分为-1，"UNKNOWN"不计分；
举例说明ChatGPT与传统文本分析工具情绪判断差异，$\color{red}{凸显了语境在自然语言处理中的重要性}$，以及在做出投资决策之前仔细考虑新闻标题含义的必要性。【极端，大公司，热点】
ChatGPT is asked to answer "YES" for positive news, "NO" for negative news, and "UNKNOWN" if uncertain;
Correspondingly, "YES" is scored as 1, "NO" as -1, and "UNKNOWN" is not scored;
Examples illustrate the difference between ChatGPT and traditional text analysis tools in sentiment judgment, $\color{red}{highlighting\ the\ importance\ of\ context\ in\ NLP}$ and the necessity of carefully considering headline implications before making investment decisions.
Empirical Design 我们将头条新闻与下一个交易时段相匹配。对于交易日早上6点之前的头条新闻，我们假设头条新闻可以在当天开盘时交易，并在当天收盘时出售。对于早上6点之后但下午4点之前的头条新闻，我们假设头条新闻可以在当天收盘时交易，并在下一个交易日收盘时出售。对于下午4点之后的头条新闻，我们假设头条新闻可以按第二天的开盘价交易，并按第二日的收盘价出售。然后，我们对ChatGPT分数、数据供应商提供的情绪分数和其他LLM的分数进行第二天股票收益的线性回归。因此，我们所有的结果都是不样本的。
Headlines are matched with the next trading session. For headlines before 6 AM on a trading day, the position is entered at the opening and exited at the close of the same day. For headlines after 6 AM but before 4 PM, the position is entered at that day's close and exited at the next trading day's close. For headlines after 4 PM, the position is entered at the next day's opening and exited at the next day's close. Linear regressions of next-day stock returns on ChatGPT scores, data vendor sentiment scores, and other LLM scores are then performed. All results are therefore out-of-sample.
"YES"得分为1，"NO"得分为-1，"UNKNOWN"不计分；
一家公司在一天内有多个头条新闻，则对分数进行平均；
头条新闻与下一个交易时段匹配：
- 交易日早上6点之前的头条新闻，假设头条新闻可以在当天开盘时交易、在当天收盘时出售；
- 早上6点之后但下午4点之前的头条新闻，假设可以在当天收盘前交易，并在下一个交易日收盘时出售；
- 下午4点之后的头条新闻，假设可以按第二天的开盘价交易，并按第二日的收盘价出售；
"YES" is scored as 1, "NO" as -1, "UNKNOWN" is not scored;
If a firm has multiple headlines on the same day, scores are averaged;
Headlines are matched with the next trading session:
- Headlines before 6 AM: position entered at the opening, exited at the close of the same day;
- Headlines after 6 AM but before 4 PM: position entered at the close, exited at the next trading day's close;
- Headlines after 4 PM: position entered at the next day's opening, exited at the next day's close;
回归：
Regression:
r_{i,t+1}=a_i+b_t+\gamma^{\prime}x_{i,t}+\varepsilon_{i,t+1}, - 其中，$r_{i,t+1}$是股票$i$次交易日的收益率，$x_{i,t}$指个股依据新闻标题得分；$a_i$和$b_t$分别是公司、日期固定效应，控制任何可观察和不可观察的时不变公司特征以及可能影响股票收益的常见时间特定因素。标准误按日期和公司进行双重聚类。
- where $r_{i,t+1}$ is the return of stock $i$ on the next trading day, $x_{i,t}$ denotes the individual stock's headline-based score; $a_i$ and $b_t$ are firm and date fixed effects, controlling for observable and unobservable time-invariant firm characteristics and common time-specific factors that may affect stock returns. Standard errors are double-clustered by date and firm.
基本大模型 - 如BERT、GPT-1和GPT-2； - 将其性能与更高级的模型进行了比较； - 采用了不同的策略，因为这些模型无法遵循提示回答特定问题（例如，GPT-1和GPT-2是自动完成的模型；更多信息见$\color{purple}{附录B}$）；
Base LLMs - Such as BERT, GPT-1, and GPT-2; - Their performance is compared against more advanced models; - Different strategies are adopted since these models cannot follow prompts to answer specific questions (e.g., GPT-1 and GPT-2 are autocompletion models; see $\color{purple}{Appendix B}$ for details);
通过比较基本LLMs和更高级LLMs的性能，理解$\color{red}{收益可预测性为大语言模型的新兴能力}$。
By comparing the performance of basic and advanced LLMs, the study demonstrates that $\color{red}{return\ predictability\ is\ an\ emergent\ capability\ of\ large\ language\ models}$.
ResultsLong-Short Strategies based on ChatGPT Scores 为了评估ChatGPT预测股价走势的能力，首先考察了基于ChatGPT新闻标题得分形成的多空策略的表现。形成零成本投资组合，在新闻发布后购买得分为正的股票，并出售得分为负的股票。 - 如果消息在交易日早上6点之前发布，我们在开盘时进入头寸，在当天收盘时退出； - 如果消息是在早上6点之后但在收盘前发布的，将以当天的收盘价进入头寸，并在下一个交易日收盘时退出； - 如果消息是在市场收盘后宣布的，在下个工作日开盘价进入头寸，并在当日收盘时退出； - All strategies are rebalanced daily.
To evaluate ChatGPT's ability to predict stock price movements, the study first examines the performance of long-short strategies formed on ChatGPT headline scores. Zero-cost portfolios are constructed by buying stocks with positive scores and selling stocks with negative scores after news release. - If news is released before 6 AM on a trading day, the position is entered at the opening and exited at the close; - If news is released after 6 AM but before market close, the position is entered at the close and exited at the next trading day's close; - If news is released after market close, the position is entered at the next business day's opening and exited at that day's close; - All strategies are rebalanced daily.
$\color{purple}{图1}$绘制了不考虑交易成本的七种不同交易策略（投资1美元）的累积收益。七种策略包括： - 基于ChatGPT 3.5购买有好消息公司的等权重投资组合（"多头"）； - 基于ChatGPT 3.5出售有坏消息公司的等权重投资组合（"空头"）； - 基于ChatGPT 3.5的多空策略（"多空"）； - 基于ChatGPT 4的自筹资金多空策略（"多空GPT 4"）； - 等权重市场投资组合（"市场等权"）； - 市值加权市场投资投资组合（"市值加权"）； - 前一天有消息的所有股票的等权重投资组合，无论消息方向如何（"所有消息"）。
$\color{purple}{Figure\ 1}$ plots the cumulative returns of seven different trading strategies (investing \$1) without transaction costs. The seven strategies include: - An equal-weighted portfolio buying firms with good news based on ChatGPT 3.5 ("Long"); - An equal-weighted portfolio selling firms with bad news based on ChatGPT 3.5 ("Short"); - A long-short strategy based on ChatGPT 3.5 ("Long-Short"); - A self-financing long-short strategy based on ChatGPT 4 ("Long-Short GPT 4"); - An equal-weighted market portfolio ("Market EW"); - A value-weighted market portfolio ("Market VW"); - An equal-weighted portfolio of all stocks with news from the previous day regardless of direction ("All News").
$\color{red}{ChatGPT得分具有预测次交易日股票收益的能力}$ - 不考虑交易成本，2021年10月到2022年12月，基于ChatGPT 3.5的多空策略可获得超过550%的惊人累计收益； - 等权重和市值加权的市场投资组合以及等权的全新闻投资组合在同一时期都获得了负的累积收益； - 以上表明，ChatGPT可以通过从新闻标题中提取预测股市反应的有价值信息获得收益； - "多头"和"空头"都有助于ChatGPT的可预测性。 - "多头"提供了大约200%； - "空头"提供了超过250%； - $\color{red}{有坏消息的股票，可预测性更强}$。
