(2003); Ortoleva and Snowberg (2015))2. In this case, the reaction to non-novel signals may be excessive, leading to subsequent reversals.
1: For example, Bloomberg terminal provides readers with over 1 million news articles daily from thousands of different sources; RavenPack sources information from over 19,000 traditional and social media outlets; Dow Jones's Factiva platform covers over 30,000 sources; EventRegistry.org aggregates and extracts events from online news, reflecting over 150,000 different sources. 2: Laboratory experiments confirm that individuals have difficulty computing correlations when they are sufficiently complex (Eyster and Weizsacker (2010); Enke and Zimmermann (2019)).
我们通过证明即使是经验丰富的金融专业人士也很难处理来自先前多篇文章的旧消息，并且这种限制对实际资产定价产生影响，从而测试了相关性忽略的理论。
首先，我们直接对活跃的金融专业人士进行了一项实验，了解他们对新闻新颖性的看法。在这种受控环境下，与直接转载之前的单篇文章相比，金融专业人士认为重组了之前几篇新闻事实的文章具有更高的新颖性。
其次，我们使用彭博终端上出现的所有财经新闻的独特数据集来测试实验结果对资产定价的影响。彭博社的数据：
- （i）由于包含数千个不同的来源，非常适合衡量信息的重复和重组；
- (ii) 考虑到彭博社的用户群主要是机构投资者，非常适合测试所提出的认知限制的市场相关性。
与投资者处理重组消息的难度一致，较高的重组量（而不是转载）与较大的股价变动相关，而股价变动更有可能在接下来的一周内逆转。这些结果有助于揭示市场对旧信息反应的实证难题3。
3:See Huberman and Regev (2001) , Tetlock (2011) , Gilbert et al. (2012) , and Drake et al. (2016) .
We test the theory of correlation neglect by demonstrating that even experienced financial professionals struggle to process old news drawn from multiple prior articles, and that this limitation has real implications for asset pricing.
First, we conduct an experiment directly on active financial professionals to assess their perceptions of news novelty. In this controlled setting, financial professionals perceive articles that recombine facts from several prior news pieces as more novel compared to direct reprints of a single prior article.
Second, we use a unique dataset of all financial news appearing on the Bloomberg terminal to test the asset pricing implications of the experimental findings. Bloomberg data:
- (i) is well suited for measuring information repetition and recombination, as it aggregates thousands of different sources;
- (ii) is well suited for testing the market relevance of the proposed cognitive limitation, given that Bloomberg's user base consists primarily of institutional investors.
Consistent with investors' difficulty in processing recombined news, higher recombination volume (rather than reprints) is associated with larger stock price movements that are more likely to reverse over the following week. These results help illuminate the empirical puzzle of market reactions to old information3.
我们的实验从各种相关机构招募了 155 名活跃的金融专业人士，包括高盛和摩根士丹利等经纪交易商，道富银行和太平洋投资管理公司等投资管理公司，以及 Citadel 和 Point72 等对冲基金。参与者代表了金融服务行业的各种角色和层级职位，从分析师和交易员到合伙人和董事总经理。该实验提示参与者评估一系列头条新闻的新颖性。标题分为两个独立样本，每个样本有 20 个标题，涵盖两家假设的公司：Argosy Logistics Inc. 和 Laker Pharmaceuticals LLC。每个参与者都会看到这两组实验标题中的一组。
Our experiment recruited 155 active financial professionals from various relevant institutions, including broker-dealers such as Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, investment managers such as State Street and PIMCO, and hedge funds such as Citadel and Point72. Participants represented a range of roles and seniority levels in the financial services industry, from analysts and traders to partners and managing directors. The experiment prompted participants to evaluate the novelty of a series of headlines. Headlines were divided into two independent samples, each containing 20 headlines, covering two hypothetical companies: Argosy Logistics Inc. and Laker Pharmaceuticals LLC. Each participant saw one of the two sets of experimental headlines.
两组实验标题均经过专门设计，包括十个小说标题、五个转载和五个重组，所有三类新闻的长度相同，并且两类旧新闻（转载和重组）与先前的文本重叠程度相同消息。重组与转载的唯一不同之处在于，重组从多个先前显示的标题中获取信息，而重印则从单个先前的来源中获取信息。举例来说，Argosy 实验组的参与者在实验开始时遇到以下四个标题：
"Argosy's misfit design business down, some tough questions to answer" "Argosy Trucking Q3 results above expectations eps 1.2 vs 1.1" "Argosy beats expectations: Q3 trucking results eps up 0.1 on 1.1" "Argosy Q3 earnings beat expectations, but design business down"
标题3是标题2的直接转载，其中75%的词已经出现过。标题 4 是一个重组：它也只包含旧新闻，其中 82.5% 的单词以前出现过，但它结合了标题 1 和 2 的同等部分。除了有关 Argosy 的标题外，参与者还遇到了 20 个随机散布的填充标题取自路透社实际头条新闻的样本。这些用于模拟市场参与者在阅读现实世界新闻时面临的信息过载。
Both sets of experimental headlines were specifically designed to include ten novel headlines, five reprints, and five recombinations, with all three categories matched in length and the two types of old news (reprints and recombinations) sharing the same degree of textual overlap with prior messages. The only difference between recombinations and reprints is that recombinations draw information from multiple previously shown headlines, whereas reprints draw from a single prior source. For illustration, participants in the Argosy treatment group encountered the following four headlines at the start of the experiment:
"Argosy's misfit design business down, some tough questions to answer" "Argosy Trucking Q3 results above expectations eps 1.2 vs 1.1" "Argosy beats expectations: Q3 trucking results eps up 0.1 on 1.1" "Argosy Q3 earnings beat expectations, but design business down"
Headline 3 is a direct reprint of headline 2, with 75% of its words having appeared before. Headline 4 is a recombination: it also contains only old news, with 82.5% of its words previously seen, but it combines equal parts of headlines 1 and 2. In addition to Argosy headlines, participants encountered 20 randomly interspersed filler headlines drawn from actual Reuters headlines, simulating the information overload that market participants face when reading real-world news.
实验结果表明，即使是经验丰富的金融专业人士也容易受到旧信息重组的影响。作为基线，参与者正确地认为包含新信息的标题比包含旧新闻的标题更新颖：例如，在七分制中，新标题的平均新颖性评级为 4.52，而旧标题的平均新颖性评级为 2.82（两者转载和重组相结合）。然而，在旧新闻集中，与转载相比，参与者更有可能将重组误认为新新闻：他们对重组的平均新颖性得分为 3.03，统计上显着高于重印的 2.61。这些结果对于改变实验的结构特征是稳健的：Argosy Logistics 新闻样本和 Laker Pharmaceuticals 新闻样本都存在重组效应，并且在五点新颖性等级上的结果与在七点新颖性等级上保持相同。点尺度。在个人层面上，68% 的参与者平均认为重组比重印的重组更新颖。相比之下，只有 19% 的参与者认为重印比重组新闻更新颖。当我们对 776 名散户投资者重复这个实验时，我们得到了本质上相似的结果，但有一个关键的区别：散户投资者更容易受到所有旧新闻的影响，包括基本的转载。
The experimental results show that even experienced financial professionals are susceptible to recombined old information. As a baseline, participants correctly perceived headlines containing new information as more novel than those containing old news: on a seven-point scale, novel headlines received an average novelty rating of 4.52, compared to 2.82 for old headlines (reprints and recombinations combined). However, within the old news set, participants were more likely to mistake recombinations for new news compared to reprints: the average novelty score for recombinations was 3.03, statistically significantly higher than 2.61 for reprints. These results are robust to varying the structural features of the experiment: the recombination effect holds for both the Argosy Logistics and Laker Pharmaceuticals news samples, and results on a five-point novelty scale are consistent with those on a seven-point scale. At the individual level, 68% of participants on average perceived recombinations as more novel than reprints, while only 19% perceived reprints as more novel than recombinations. When we replicated the experiment with 776 retail investors, we obtained essentially similar results, with one key difference: retail investors were more susceptible to all old news, including basic reprints.
我们提取并测试了已记录的信息重组敏感性对资产定价的影响：
首先，作为基准，鉴于金融专业人士普遍认为旧新闻（尤其是转载）不如实际的新新闻新颖，我们应该观察旧新闻文章带来的较小的价格反应。 其次，由于重组被认为比简单的转载更新颖，因此我们预计会观察到与重组新闻相关的更大的价格变动。 最后，如果对重组消息的更大价格反应源于投资者的认知限制（而不是结合先前已知事实的内在价值），那么对重组消息的反应应该伴随着更大的后续反转。
We derive and test the asset pricing implications of the documented sensitivity to information recombination:
First, as a benchmark, given that financial professionals generally perceive old news (especially reprints) as less novel than genuinely new news, we should observe smaller price reactions to old news articles. Second, since recombinations are perceived as more novel than simple reprints, we expect to observe larger price movements associated with recombined news. Finally, if the larger price reaction to recombined news stems from investors' cognitive limitations (rather than intrinsic value from combining previously known facts), the reaction to recombined news should be accompanied by greater subsequent reversals.
为了对这些预测进行实证检验，我们利用了来自 Bloomberg 终端的 3.87 亿篇新闻文章的新颖数据集，该数据集特别适合研究新闻新颖性。
彭博终端汇集了来自各种新闻来源的信息，其中包括大量转载和重组。 我们的最终样本涵盖了 2000 年 1 月至 2014 年 12 月期间发表的超过 1700 万篇新闻文章4。
大约占样本的 10%由彭博社直接发布的新闻组成，其中大部分（约 60%）来自主要的国内和国际新闻通讯社，其余部分来自网络。 这使我们能够构建旧新闻的综合衡量标准，并追踪任何特定新闻的词源。
To empirically test these predictions, we utilize a novel dataset of 387 million news articles from the Bloomberg terminal, which is particularly well suited for studying news novelty.
The Bloomberg terminal aggregates information from a wide range of news sources, including substantial reprints and recombinations. Our final sample covers over 17 million news articles published between January 2000 and December 20144.
Approximately 10% of the sample consists of news published directly by Bloomberg, with the majority (about 60%) from major domestic and international wire services, and the remainder from online sources. This enables us to construct comprehensive measures of old news and trace the lexical origins of any given news article.
我们通过评估过去三天内该文章中已经出现在同一家公司的类似文章中的独特单词的百分比，将彭博新闻样本中的每篇新闻文章分类为新颖新闻、转载或重组:
在主要规范中，如果一篇文章文本中至少 40% 的独特单词没有出现在关于同一公司的前五篇文本最相似的文章中，我们将其标记为新颖。 所有其他新闻文章，其内容至少占先前文章的 60%，都被标记为旧新闻。 - 在旧新闻中，转载是指那些文本主要（至少 80% 的旧文本）被前一篇文章覆盖的文章。 - 另一方面，重组包含不新颖的内容，但也不被单个最近邻居跨越。在未制表的分析中，我们确认诸如余弦相似性度量（Cohen et al. (2020)；Fedyk (2022)）等替代方法可以产生类似的新闻分类。为了便于解释，我们根据整篇论文中跨词的百分比使用更直观的衡量标准。
We classify each news article in the Bloomberg sample as novel, reprint, or recombination by evaluating the percentage of unique words in the article that already appeared in similar articles about the same firm over the past three days:
In the main specification, an article is labeled as novel if at least 40% of its unique words did not appear in the five most textually similar prior articles about the same firm. All other news articles, with at least 60% of their content covered by prior articles, are labeled as old news. - Among old news, reprints are articles whose text is predominantly (at least 80% of old text) spanned by a single prior article. - Recombinations, on the other hand, contain non-novel content that is not spanned by a single nearest neighbor. In untabulated analysis, we confirm that alternative methods such as cosine similarity measures (Cohen et al. (2020); Fedyk (2022)) yield similar news classifications. For ease of interpretation, we use the more intuitive measure based on percentage of spanned words throughout the paper.
我们估计了每日公司层面的回报与公司旧新闻文章和特定重组新闻文章的比例之间的关系。在基线中，较高比例的旧新闻与较小的绝对异常价格变化相关：公司新闻中额外 10% 的旧新闻转化为同一交易日的绝对异常每日回报较小 11.5 个基点。但控制旧新闻的整体水平，当旧新闻更多是重组类型而不是转载类型时，市场反应就更大。将一家公司10%的新闻文章从转载改为重组，相当于同一交易日的绝对异常收益增加17.6个基点。当然，在实验设置之外，重组新闻并不是随机分配的，特别重要的内容可能会触发将两个或多个事实组合成一篇文章的选择。在这种情况下，价格对重组消息的反应将构成正确的市场反应，而不是对旧消息的过度反应。为了区分这两个故事，我们研究了对重组消息的最初反应的反转。
We estimate the relationship between daily firm-level returns and the proportion of old news articles and specifically recombined news articles for the firm. At baseline, a higher proportion of old news is associated with smaller absolute abnormal price changes: an additional 10% of old news in a firm's news translates to 11.5 basis points smaller absolute abnormal daily returns on the same trading day. However, controlling for the overall level of old news, the market reaction is larger when old news is more of the recombination type rather than the reprint type. Shifting 10% of a firm's news articles from reprints to recombinations corresponds to a 17.6 basis point increase in absolute abnormal returns on the same trading day. Of course, outside the experimental setting, recombined news is not randomly assigned, and particularly important content may trigger the editorial choice to combine two or more facts into a single article. In that case, the price reaction to recombined news would constitute a correct market response rather than an overreaction to old news. To distinguish between these two stories, we examine reversals of the initial reaction to recombined news.
与我们的概念预测一致，重组消息比例较高后的较大价格反应更有可能在接下来的一周发生逆转。
具体来说，该公司另外 10% 的新闻是旧消息，预计未来一周整体日回报率将有 13.9% 出现逆转； 另外 10% 的旧新闻是重新组合而不是重印，则逆转率又增加了 16.9%。 这表明，平均而言，对重组消息的过度反应往往会在一周内完全逆转。 5 【反转周期】 在其他分析中，我们确认我们的结果对于以下替代方案是稳健的： - (a) 我们比较之前文章的天数； - (b) 新颖性比较中包含的最接近的先前文章的数量； - (c) 新颖性、重印和重组的阈值； - (d) 异常收益措施； - (d) 对旧内容和重组内容进行连续测量，而不是离散分类。
Consistent with our conceptual predictions, the larger price reaction following a higher proportion of recombined news is more likely to reverse over the following week.
Specifically, an additional 10% of a firm's news being old news predicts a 13.9% reversal of the overall daily return over the following week; An additional 10% of old news being recombinations rather than reprints predicts a further 16.9% reversal. This suggests that, on average, the overreaction to recombined news tends to fully reverse within one week. In additional analyses, we confirm that our results are robust to alternatives in: - (a) the number of days over which prior articles are compared; - (b) the number of closest prior articles included in the novelty comparison; - (c) thresholds for novelty, reprints, and recombinations; - (d) abnormal return measures; - (e) continuous measures of old content and recombination rather than discrete classifications.
我们的市场证据强调了我们实验中记录的认知局限性的相关性，难以处理重组新闻，从而转化为相当大的回报过度反应。有趣的是，我们的结果与信息重组在其他领域发挥的更积极的作用形成对比。例如，Hirshleifer 等人。 （2018）发现，引用更广泛技术类别的先前工作的专利预示着公司生产力的持续提高和更高的异常回报。我们的研究结果表明，在财经新闻的背景下，由于相关性忽视而对重组新闻的错误过度反应超过了潜在的积极影响，例如重组新闻通过综合个别新闻来提供新颖的见解。
Our market evidence underscores the relevance of the cognitive limitation documented in our experiment -- difficulty processing recombined news -- which translates into substantial return overreaction. Interestingly, our results contrast with the more positive role that information recombination plays in other domains. For example, Hirshleifer et al. (2018) find that patents citing prior work across broader technology classes predict sustained increases in firm productivity and higher abnormal returns. Our findings suggest that, in the context of financial news, the erroneous overreaction to recombined news due to correlation neglect outweighs potential positive effects, such as recombined news providing novel insights by synthesizing individual pieces of news.
我们在几个方向上扩展了我们的实证分析。
首先，为了直接探讨有限注意力的作用，我们根据投资者的注意力来划分样本。我们使用 Ben-Rephael 等人在彭博终端上衡量投资者注意力的指标。 (2017)，可在安全日期级别获取。与关注度有限相一致，当投资者关注度较高时，市场对旧消息的反应较小。但这主要是通过更好地筛选简单转载来推动的，即使在投资者高度关注的情况下，重组效应仍然存在。 其次，受实验中散户投资者对陈旧新闻更敏感的启发，我们研究了机构与散户订单不平衡对旧新闻和重组新闻的反应。我们提供的证据表明，零售交易（比机构交易）相对较大，以应对一般旧消息，但对于重组消息则较少。 第三，我们探讨新闻情绪和模糊性（硬定量与软主观信息）如何影响我们的结果。我们发现我们的实证结果对于正面和负面新闻都非常相似，并且对于控制硬定量信息量来说是稳健的。
We extend our empirical analysis in several directions.
First, to directly examine the role of limited attention, we partition the sample by investor attention. We use the Bloomberg terminal-based measure of investor attention from Ben-Rephael et al. (2017), available at the security-date level. Consistent with limited attention, the market reaction to old news is smaller when investor attention is higher. However, this is primarily driven by better screening of simple reprints; the recombination effect persists even under high investor attention. Second, motivated by our experimental finding that retail investors are more sensitive to stale news, we examine institutional versus retail order imbalance responses to old and recombined news. We provide evidence that retail trading is relatively larger (compared to institutional trading) in response to general old news, but less so for recombined news. Third, we explore how news sentiment and ambiguity (hard quantitative vs. soft subjective information) affect our results. We find that our empirical results are very similar for positive and negative news, and robust to controlling for the amount of hard quantitative information.
最后，我们利用大型数据集的时间序列来研究记录的效果如何随时间变化。我们对样本中的每个全年（从 2001 年到 2014 年）分别进行了测试。系数的时间趋势表明，对于（总体）旧新闻水平较高的公司，异常日回报逐渐变小。然而，随着时间的推移，对重组消息的不同反应不断增加，公司额外 10% 的消息是重组（而不是重印）的系数从 2001 年的正 5 个基点效应增加到正的 24 个基点效应。 2014 年的点效应。这些结果表明投资者在筛选简单转载方面的成熟度有所提高，但仍然对重新组合先前可用信息的文章敏感。
Finally, we exploit the time series of our large dataset to study how the documented effects evolve over time. We run the tests separately for each full year in the sample (from 2001 to 2014). The time trend in coefficients shows that abnormal daily returns become progressively smaller for firms with higher (overall) old news levels. However, over time, the differential reaction to recombined news is increasing, with the coefficient on an additional 10% of a firm's news being recombinations (rather than reprints) rising from a positive 5 basis point effect in 2001 to a positive 24 basis point effect in 2014. These results suggest that investors have become more sophisticated at screening simple reprints, but remain sensitive to articles that recombine previously available information.
本文提出了市场未能识别旧消息的渠道，并通过实验和实证资产价格为该机制提供了证据，为有关金融市场认知偏差的文献做出了贡献。 6 我们的论文涉及越来越多的研究，这些研究检验了投资者在处理金融信息时注意力有限对资产价格的影响。 7 特别是，受相关忽略理论工作的推动（DeMarzo et al. (2003)；Ortoleva and Snowberg (2015)），我们推测一种特定类型的有限注意力，即市场参与者不完全关注将多个来源的旧信息重新组合为旧新闻。这是基于实验经济学的证据，即人类受试者在分析代表相同潜在信号的不同组合的一系列报告时往往会忽略相关性（Enke 和 Zimmermann（2019）），以及最近在可比定价中过度重视重复信息的证据（Murfin）和普拉特（2019））。我们的研究的独特之处在于，我们提供了金融新闻中相关性忽略的直接实验证据，并表明即使是经验丰富的投资者也很难处理旧新闻的重组。除了通过实验记录这种认知局限性之外，我们还通过实证确认其对资产价格的影响并证明其随时间的持续相关性来展示其相关性。
This paper identifies a channel through which the market fails to recognize old news and provides evidence for this mechanism through both experiments and empirical asset prices, contributing to the literature on cognitive biases in financial markets. Our paper relates to the growing body of research examining the effects of limited investor attention on asset prices when processing financial information. In particular, motivated by the theoretical work on correlation neglect (DeMarzo et al. (2003); Ortoleva and Snowberg (2015)), we conjecture a specific type of limited attention: market participants' incomplete attention to the recombination of old information from multiple sources into old news. This builds on evidence from experimental economics that human subjects tend to neglect correlations when analyzing a series of reports representing different combinations of the same underlying signals (Enke and Zimmermann (2019)), and on recent evidence of overweighting duplicated information in comparable pricing (Murfin and Pratt (2019)). Our study is unique in providing direct experimental evidence of correlation neglect in financial news, showing that even experienced investors struggle to process recombinations of old news. Beyond documenting this cognitive limitation experimentally, we demonstrate its relevance by empirically confirming its impact on asset prices and showing its persistent relevance over time.
本文的其余部分如下。第二节介绍了金融专业人士对新闻新颖性的看法的实验调查，表明市场参与者更容易受到旧信息重组的影响，而不是直接转载。第 3 节介绍了评估彭博新闻大样本文本新颖性的数据和方法。第 4 节介绍了我们评估所提出的机制对资产价格动态影响的主要实证结果。第 5 节介绍了稳健性和其他分析，第 6 节进行了总结。
The remainder of the paper is organized as follows. Section 2 presents the experimental investigation of financial professionals' perceptions of news novelty, showing that market participants are more susceptible to recombined old information than direct reprints. Section 3 describes the data and methodology for evaluating textual novelty in the large Bloomberg news sample. Section 4 presents the main empirical results assessing the impact of the proposed mechanism on asset price dynamics. Section 5 presents robustness and additional analyses, and Section 6 concludes.
Experimental evidence 我们通过哈佛商学院校友网络招募了 155 名活跃的金融专业人士，参与 2018 年底和 2019 年初进行的在线实验。参与者涵盖了金融服务行业的整个领域（他们的公司隶属关系见互联网附录表 A.1）。 1).绝大多数参与者来自高盛、摩根士丹利等大型银行和券商，富达、道富等投资管理公司，贝恩资本、Lindsay Goldberg等私募股权公司，以及Two Sigma、Point等对冲基金72、以及巴克莱和麦格理等投资银行。其余 22% 的样本包括来自金融新闻机构（例如《金融时报》）、保险公司（例如利宝互助银行）、政府机构（例如联邦储备委员会）、咨询公司（例如德勤）、私人投资者的个人，以及大学或 Facebook 等科技公司的金融员工。样本包括合伙人和董事总经理等关键决策者，以及投资组合经理和交易员等活跃的年轻员工。
We recruited 155 active financial professionals through the Harvard Business School alumni network for an online experiment conducted in late 2018 and early 2019. Participants spanned the full spectrum of the financial services industry (firm affiliations are listed in Internet Appendix Table A.1). The vast majority came from major banks and broker-dealers such as Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, investment managers such as Fidelity and State Street, private equity firms such as Bain Capital and Lindsay Goldberg, hedge funds such as Two Sigma and Point72, and investment banks such as Barclays and Macquarie. The remaining 22% of the sample included individuals from financial news organizations (e.g., Financial Times), insurance companies (e.g., Liberty Mutual), government agencies (e.g., Federal Reserve Board), consulting firms (e.g., Deloitte), private investors, and finance staff at universities or technology companies such as Facebook. The sample included key decision-makers such as partners and managing directors, as well as active junior employees such as portfolio managers and traders.
如$\color{blue}{Tetlock(2007)}$、$\color{blue}{Tetlock\ et\ al.(2008)}$ 和 $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2011)}$ 使用新闻和训练的算法成功预测股票收益，可能是因为组合新信息很复杂（$\color{blue}{Fedyk\ and\ Hodson,2023}$）。
第三，利用模型的特征重要性（$\color{blue}{Binsbergen\ et\ al.,2023}$）理解更能准确预测判断的概念； - 周期：2021年10月-2022年12月（as ChatGPT's training data stops in September 2021）【*This ensures that our $\color{red}{evaluation\ is\ based\ on\ information\ not\ present\ in\ the\ model's\ training\ data}$,
$\color{purple}{Panel A}$表明新闻标题总体上正面；$\color{purple}{Panel B}$报告了变量的相关矩阵，GPT得分与事件情绪得分之间的相关性较低，小于0.28。
As shown by $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2007)}$, $\color{blue}{Tetlock\ et\ al.(2008)}$, and $\color{blue}{Tetlock(2011)}$, news-based trained algorithms can successfully predict stock returns, possibly because combining new information is complex ($\color{blue}{Fedyk\ and\ Hodson,2023}$).
Third, leveraging the model's feature importance ($\color{blue}{Binsbergen\ et\ al.,2023}$) to understand which concepts most accurately predict judgments; - Period: October 2021 - December 2022 (as ChatGPT's training data stops in September 2021) [*This ensures that our $\color{red}{evaluation\ is\ based\ on\ information\ not\ present\ in\ the\ model's\ training\ data}$,
$\color{purple}{Panel A}$ indicates that news headlines are generally positive; $\color{purple}{Panel B}$ reports the correlation matrix of the variables, with a low correlation (less than 0.28) between GPT scores and event sentiment scores.
参考文献 / References Tetlock, P. C. (2007). Giving content to investor sentiment: The role of media in the stock market. The Journal of Finance, 62(3), 1139-1168.2.

entry.title: Software | R | Stemming and lemmatization
entry.content:
内容摘自论文《词形还原方法及实现工具比较分析》
词形还原（lemmatization），是把一个任何形式的语言词汇还原为一般形式（能表达完整语义）
词干提取（stemming）是抽取词的词干或词根形式（不一定能够表达完整语义）。
词形还原和词干提取是词形规范化的两类重要方式，都能够达到有效归并词形的目的，二者既有联系也有区别
Content excerpted from the paper "Comparative Analysis of Lemmatization Methods and Tools."
Lemmatization reduces any word form to its base form (which carries complete semantics).
Stemming extracts the stem or root form of a word (which may not carry complete semantics).
Lemmatization and stemming are two important approaches to word form normalization. Both can effectively consolidate word forms, but they have both similarities and differences.
Similarities 现将共同点和联系总结为以下4方面：
（1）目标一致。 词干提取和词形还原的目标均为将词的屈折形态或派生形态简化或归并为词干（stem）或原形的基础形式，都是一种对词的不同形态的统一归并的过程。
（2）结果部分交叉。 词干提取和词形还原不是互斥关系，其结果是有部分交叉的。一部分词利用这两类方法都能达到相同的词形转换效果。如"dogs"的词干为"dog"，其原形也为"dog"。
（3）主流实现方法类似。 目前实现词干提取和词形还原的主流实现方法均是利用语言中存在的规则或利用词典映射提取词干或获得词的原形。
（4）应用领域相似。 主要应用于信息检索和文本、自然语言处理等方面，二者均是这些应用的基本步骤。
The similarities can be summarized in four aspects:
Same objective. Both stemming and lemmatization aim to simplify or consolidate inflected or derived word forms into their stem or base form.
Partially overlapping results. Stemming and lemmatization are not mutually exclusive — some words yield the same result with both methods. For example, "dogs" has stem "dog" and lemma "dog."
Similar mainstream implementation. Both primarily rely on rule-based transformations or dictionary mappings.
Similar application domains. Both are foundational steps in information retrieval, text processing, and natural language processing.
Differences 二者的区别归纳为以下5方面：
（1）原理不同。 词干提取主要是采用"缩减"的方法，将词转换为词干，如将"cats"处理为"cat"，将"effective"处理为"effect"。而词形还原主要采用"转变"的方法，将词转变为其原形，如将"drove"处理为"drive"，将"driving"处理为"drive"。
（2）复杂性不同。 词干提取方法相对简单，词形还原则需要返回词的原形，需要对词形进行分析，不仅要进行词缀的转化，还要进行词性识别。词性标注的准确率也直接影响词形还原的准确率，因此，词形还原更为复杂。
（3）实现方法侧重不同。 词干提取的实现方法主要利用规则变化进行词缀的去除和缩减。词形还原则更依赖于词典，进行词形变化和原形的映射，生成词典中的有效词。
（4）结果不同。 词干提取的结果可能并不是完整的、具有意义的词，如"revival"词干提取的结果为"reviv"。而词形还原处理后获得的结果是完整的词，一般为词典中的有效词。
（5）应用领域侧重不同。 词干提取更多被应用于信息检索领域，如Solr、Lucene等，粒度较粗。词形还原更主要被应用于文本挖掘、自然语言处理，用于更细粒度、更为准确的文本分析。
The differences can be summarized in five aspects:
Different principles. Stemming uses a "reduction" approach (e.g., "cats" → "cat", "effective" → "effect"), while lemmatization uses a "transformation" approach (e.g., "drove" → "drive", "driving" → "drive").
Different complexity. Stemming is relatively simple. Lemmatization requires morphological analysis and part-of-speech identification, making it more complex.
Different implementation focus. Stemming relies primarily on rule-based affix removal. Lemmatization relies more on dictionary lookups and morphological mappings.
Different results. Stemming may produce incomplete word fragments (e.g., "revival" → "reviv"), while lemmatization always returns valid dictionary words.
Different application emphasis. Stemming is more commonly used in information retrieval (e.g., Solr, Lucene) at coarser granularity. Lemmatization is preferred in text mining and NLP for finer-grained, more accurate analysis.
相对而言，词干提取是简单的轻量级的词形归并方式，最后获得的结果为词干，并不一定具有实际意义。词形还原处理相对复杂，获得结果为词的原形，能够承载一定意义，与词干提取相比，更具有研究和应用价值。
In summary, stemming is a simpler, lightweight approach that may not produce meaningful words, while lemmatization is more complex but yields complete base forms with semantic meaning, making it more valuable for research and applications.

entry.title: Tool | Xiumi|How to insert small images within text in WeChat Official Account articles?
entry.content:
Keyboard Shortcut for Inserting Small Images in Text 首先，将鼠标的文字光标定位到文字段落里； 然后，按住键盘快捷键 Alt + Ctrl（MAC: Option + Cmd） 不松手的同时，用鼠标点击左侧栏的图片即可。 提示：按住快捷键不松手的同时，用鼠标去点图库里的图片。这是成功的关键！
First, place the text cursor at the desired position in the paragraph. Then, hold the keyboard shortcut Alt + Ctrl (Mac: Option + Cmd). While holding the shortcut, click on the image in the left sidebar. Tip: The key to success is holding the shortcut while clicking the image in the gallery!
Using Inline Insertion Mode 文字行内插入模式，是一种新的文字内插入内容操作，在文字工具条的格式里:
选中文字，点击工具条上的格式，点击行内插入模式; 然后，将鼠标光标放在文字间，再从左边点击图片（或贴纸），就可以插入到文字内； 接着，用鼠标点击选中图片，拖动图片四周的锚点，可以调整它的大小； 完成文字内插入小图片后，点击右边文字提示退出行内插入模式，或是鼠标点击编辑区外空白区域一次，即可退出行内插入模式。
Inline insertion mode is a way to insert content within text, accessible from the Format menu in the text toolbar:
Select the text, click Format in the toolbar, then click Inline Insertion Mode. Place the cursor between text, then click an image (or sticker) from the left panel to insert it. Click on the inserted image and drag the anchor points to resize it. When finished, click the Exit Inline Insertion Mode prompt on the right, or click outside the editing area.

entry.title: Journal | JBES | Forecasting with economic news
entry.content: 封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·ECover:ChatGPT-4 DALL·E 摘要： The goal of this article is to evaluate the informational content of sentiment extracted from news articles about the state of the economy. We propose a fine-grained aspect-based sentiment analysis that has two main characteristics: (a) we consider only the text in the article that is semantically dependent on a term of interest (aspect-based) and, (b) assign a sentiment score to each word based on a dictionary that we develop for applications in economics and finance (fine-grained). Our dataset includes six large U.S. newspapers, for a total of over 6.6 million articles and 4.2 billion words. Our findings suggest that several measures of economic sentiment track closely business cycle fluctuations and that they are relevant predictors for four major macroeconomic variables. We find that there are significant improvements in forecasting when sentiment is considered along with macroeconomic factors. In addition, we also find that sentiment matters to explains the tails of the probability distribution across several macroeconomic variables. 引用格式：Barbaglia, L., Consoli, S., & Manzan, S. (2023). Forecasting with economic news. Journal of Business & Economic Statistics, 41(3), 708-719. 文献阅读：

entry.title: DL | Deep learning for label imbalance in High-frequency trading
Literature | FRL | Can ChatGPT assist in picking stocks?
entry.content:
封面来源：ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
Cover source: ChatGPT-4 DALL·E
摘要：本文研究了可接入互联网的ChatGPT-4能否提供有价值的投资建议并及时评估财务信息。通过现场实验，发现 ChatGPT-4 评级与未来盈余公告和股票收益之间正相关。实证表明ChatGPT-4会根据盈利意外和新闻事件信息及时调整评级，"attractiveness ratings"策略可产生正收益。
Abstract: This paper investigates whether internet-enabled ChatGPT-4 can provide valuable investment advice and timely assess financial information. Through a field experiment, ChatGPT-4 ratings are found to be positively correlated with future earnings announcements and stock returns. Empirical results show that ChatGPT-4 adjusts its ratings in response to earnings surprises and news events, and the "attractiveness ratings" strategy generates positive returns.
引用：Pelster, M., & Val, J. (2024). Can ChatGPT assist in picking stocks?. Finance Research Letters, 59, 104786.
Citation: Pelster, M., & Val, J. (2024). Can ChatGPT assist in picking stocks?. Finance Research Letters, 59, 104786.
文献/Paper： Highlights
We conduct a live experiment to evaluate whether ChatGPT can pick stocks. ChatGPT’s earnings forecast significantly correlate with actual earnings. ChatGPT’s attractiveness ratings significantly correlate with future stock returns. ChatGPT updates its ratings to news information in a timely manner.
Keywords Information processing; Artificial intelligence (AI); ChatGPT; Introduction 新闻在金融市场中至关重要，其可为投资者判断资产内在价值、正确投资决策提供信息。然而，当今信息爆炸时代（甚至信息冲突）可能会让投资者无法彻底判断。相较于机构投资者，个人投资者尤其如此，其在时间和技术方面面临更多限制。
News is crucial in financial markets, providing investors with information to assess intrinsic asset values and make sound investment decisions. However, the current era of information overload (and even conflicting information) may prevent investors from making thorough judgments. This is particularly true for retail investors compared to institutional investors, as they face greater constraints in time and expertise.
作为一种先进的人工智能 (AI) 语言模型，ChatGPT已成为潜在有益于投资者的工具。通过利用其自然语言处理 (NLP) 功能，ChatGPT根据过往观察到的模式将大量信息提炼成简洁的摘要，从而帮助投资者有效筛选大量新闻等数据，并提供对特定查询的答复，达成满足投资者需求的互动和定制交流。而且该技术具有普适性，且可能降低信息成本，使金融知识普惠大众。
As an advanced artificial intelligence (AI) language model, ChatGPT has emerged as a potentially beneficial tool for investors. By leveraging its natural language processing (NLP) capabilities, ChatGPT distills vast amounts of information into concise summaries based on previously observed patterns, helping investors efficiently filter through large volumes of news and data, and providing responses to specific queries for interactive and customized communication. Moreover, the technology is universally accessible and may reduce information costs, democratizing financial knowledge.
本研究评估了ChatGPT综合财经新闻的能力，探讨其能否准确提供切实可行的投资见解。在2023年第二季度财报公布期间进行现场实验，以测试ChatGPT能否可靠评估财报意外和其他新闻事件。使用盈利公告作为测试是因为此时安排了重大企业活动，创造了信息丰富的环境，蕴含复杂性和微妙性，对投资者来而言较难判断。
This study evaluates ChatGPT's ability to synthesize financial news and examines whether it can accurately provide actionable investment insights. A field experiment was conducted during the Q2 2023 earnings announcement period to test whether ChatGPT can reliably assess earnings surprises and other news events. Earnings announcements were chosen as the test setting because they involve major scheduled corporate events, creating an information-rich environment with complexity and nuance that is difficult for investors to judge.
$\color{red}{现场实验可有效避免信息泄露}$
具体而言，不能使用历史数据分析ChatGPT的性能，因为无法保证ChatGPT不使用"未来"数据。
Specifically, historical data cannot be used to analyze ChatGPT's performance, since there is no guarantee that ChatGPT does not use "future" data.
实证结果表明控制共识预测（consensus forecasts）后，ChatGPT盈利预测与实际盈利正相关。 "吸引力评级"（attractiveness ratings）与未来股票收益正相关。此外，ChatGPT能够及时对新闻做出反应。
Empirical results show that, after controlling for consensus forecasts, ChatGPT's earnings predictions are positively correlated with actual earnings. The "attractiveness ratings" are positively correlated with future stock returns. Furthermore, ChatGPT is able to react to news in a timely manner.
通过研究盈余公告（earnings announcements）预测和股票吸引力评级，探索两个用例以支持投资者决策过程中使用ChatGPT。公告发布后几个月内，积极（消极）意外会导致积极（消极）的累积异常收益（CAR）。可要求ChatGPT在财报公布之前协助选股、判断股票吸引力评级。由于投资者处理信息存在认知、时间限制，因此考虑预选方案易于考虑全市场。
Two use cases are explored to support investors' decision-making with ChatGPT: earnings announcement forecasting and stock attractiveness ratings. In the months following announcements, positive (negative) surprises lead to positive (negative) cumulative abnormal returns (CAR). ChatGPT can be asked to assist in stock picking and judge attractiveness ratings before earnings releases. Given investors' cognitive and time constraints in processing information, considering a pre-selected set is easier than evaluating the entire market.
本文对ChatGPT在金融领域的用例文献做出重要贡献（$\color{blue}{Dowling\ and\ Lucey，2023}$；$\color{blue}{Fieberg\ et\ al.，2023}$；$\color{blue}{Niszczota\ and\ Abbas，2023}$），通过提供类似辅导的支持并帮助以对话方式解释数据，ChatGPT和类似技术很可能会影响投资者的决策过程。ChatGPT以及类似技术在各行业的快速融合表明，未来人们将更加依赖人工智能检索、总结和分析的信息。研究结果表明，该技术可能有助于金融机构帮助客户、员工提取和解释信息的实施。
This paper makes an important contribution to the literature on ChatGPT use cases in finance ($\color{blue}{Dowling\ and\ Lucey，2023}$; $\color{blue}{Fieberg\ et\ al.，2023}$; $\color{blue}{Niszczota\ and\ Abbas，2023}$). By providing tutoring-like support and helping to interpret data in a conversational manner, ChatGPT and similar technologies are likely to affect investors' decision-making processes. The rapid integration of ChatGPT and similar technologies across industries suggests that people will increasingly rely on AI-retrieved, summarized, and analyzed information. The findings indicate that this technology may help financial institutions assist clients and employees in extracting and interpreting information.
What is ChatGPT? 基于OpenAI的GPT-4架构，ChatGPT是一种最先进的NLP算法，其旨在根据给定的提示生成类似人类的文本。该模型建立在$\color{blue}{Vaswani\ et\ al.(2017)}$引入的Transformer架构之上，并利用具有多层注意力机制的深度学习方法处理、生成语言。由于其庞大的训练数据和先进的架构，ChatGPT擅长多种应用，例如内容生成、翻译，甚至是代码编写等复杂任务。
Based on OpenAI's GPT-4 architecture, ChatGPT is a state-of-the-art NLP algorithm designed to generate human-like text based on given prompts. The model is built upon the Transformer architecture introduced by $\color{blue}{Vaswani\ et\ al.(2017)}$ and leverages a deep learning approach with multiple layers of attention mechanisms to process and generate language. Due to its vast training data and advanced architecture, ChatGPT excels at multiple applications such as content generation, translation, and even complex tasks like code writing.
LLMs广泛应用：在金融领域中，大语言模型 (large language models, LLMs) 可用于自动化客户支持、财务分析、欺诈检测、算法交易、个人财务管理、法规遵从、教育或咨询($\color{blue}{Cucchiara, 2023}$)。鉴于应用范围广泛，摩根士丹利已经在其财富管理业务中采用 ChatGPT 也就不足为奇了。在投资方面，$\color{blue}{Fieberg\ et\ al.(2023)}$认为"公开信息是可预测资产价格变动的关键驱动因素，意味着GPT-4等人工智能应用程序可能特别适合根据新信息调整投资组合配置建议"。OpenAI集成浏览功能可能会显著改变ChatGPT在该领域的能力，因为来自网络的当前信息绕过了ChatGPT的知识截点（2021年9月）。
Broad LLM applications: In finance, large language models (LLMs) can be used for automated customer support, financial analysis, fraud detection, algorithmic trading, personal finance management, regulatory compliance, education, or consulting ($\color{blue}{Cucchiara, 2023}$). Given the wide range of applications, it is not surprising that Morgan Stanley has already adopted ChatGPT in its wealth management business. Regarding investments, $\color{blue}{Fieberg\ et\ al.(2023)}$ argue that "publicly available information is the key driver of predictable asset price movements, implying that AI applications such as GPT-4 may be particularly well-equipped to adjust portfolio allocation advice to new information." OpenAI's integration of browsing capabilities may significantly change ChatGPT's abilities in this field, as current web information bypasses ChatGPT's knowledge cutoff (September 2021).
考虑到海量信息和投资者的注意力有限（$\color{blue}{Hirshleifer\ and\ Teoh, 2023}$），利用LLMs协助处理信息可能会改善投资管理。然而，ChatGPT根据在其训练数据中观察到的模式生成响应，如果出现模棱两可或误导性的提示，则很容易产生错误信息（$\color{blue}{Lipton\ and\ Steinhardt,\ 2019}$）；其并不"理解"人类意义上的信息，而是重复所学到的模式。这有时会导致输出看起来合理但实际上不正确2。此外，虽然该模型可以生成广泛主题的文本，但其在特定、细致入微的主题上的专业知识深度可能会有所不同，这通常反映了其训练数据的分布和质量。总而言之，该模型评估财务信息的潜在优势来自于其观察过去数据（训练数据）模式并将这些模式应用于当前信息的能力。如果相同或非常相似的模式导致不同的结果，那么评估就会导致误导性的建议。
2. Compared to previous versions, GPT-4 reduces the probability of hallucination, i.e., that the language model generates random answers not in line with reality. ↩
Considering the vast amount of information and investors' limited attention ($\color{blue}{Hirshleifer\ and\ Teoh, 2023}$), leveraging LLMs to assist in information processing could improve investment management. However, ChatGPT generates responses based on patterns observed in its training data, making it prone to producing misinformation when presented with ambiguous or misleading prompts ($\color{blue}{Lipton\ and\ Steinhardt,\ 2019}$); it does not "understand" information in the human sense but rather replicates learned patterns. This can sometimes lead to outputs that appear plausible but are factually incorrect2. Moreover, while the model can generate text on a broad range of topics, its depth of expertise on specific, nuanced subjects may vary, typically reflecting the distribution and quality of its training data. In summary, the model's potential advantage in evaluating financial information stems from its ability to observe patterns in past data (training data) and apply those patterns to current information. If the same or very similar patterns lead to different outcomes, the evaluation may produce misleading recommendations.
Related literature ChatGPT在金融领域的研究仍处于起步阶段，一些研究调查了ChatGPT是否有可能帮助投资组合构建。Ko 和 Lee（2023）专注于资产类别，发现ChatGPT的选择更加多元化，并且优于随机投资组合。Lopez-Lira 和 Tang (2023) 使用 ChatGPT 评估单一股票新闻头条的情绪，发现基于 ChatGPT 情绪评分的投资组合优于基于其他 LLMs（例如 BERT）的投资组合。Smales (2023) 研究了 ChatGPT 是否能够对澳大利亚储备银行做出的货币政策决策进行分类。
Research on ChatGPT in financial contexts is still in its infancy. Several studies investigate whether ChatGPT can potentially assist portfolio construction. Ko and Lee (2023) focus on asset classes and find that ChatGPT's selections are better diversified and outperform random portfolios. Lopez-Lira and Tang (2023) use ChatGPT to assess the sentiment of individual stock news headlines and find that portfolios based on ChatGPT sentiment scores outperform those based on other LLMs such as BERT. Smales (2023) studies whether ChatGPT can classify monetary policy decisions made by the Reserve Bank of Australia.
Niszczota 和 Abbas (2023) 研究了 GPT 是否可以充当金融机器人顾问。作者表明，基于 GPT-4 的 ChatGPT 在金融素养测试中取得了近乎完美的 99% 分数，这比之前的版本有了显著的改进。Fieberg et al. (2023) 设计假设的投资者概况，其风险承受能力、年龄和投资期限各不相同。对于这些投资者，作者随后要求 ChatGPT 推荐投资组合。他们的结论是，GPT 的建议反映了投资者的个人情况，例如风险承受能力、风险能力和可持续性偏好，但对投资期限不敏感。尽管如此，他们得出的结论是，历史风险调整后的表现与专业管理的基准投资组合相当。
Niszczota and Abbas (2023) investigate whether GPT can serve as a financial robo-advisor. The authors show that ChatGPT based on GPT-4 achieves a near-perfect 99% score in a financial literacy test, a significant improvement over previous versions. Fieberg et al. (2023) design hypothetical investor profiles varying in risk tolerance, age, and investment horizon. For these investors, the authors then ask ChatGPT to recommend portfolio compositions. They conclude that GPT's suggestions reflect individual circumstances such as risk tolerance, risk capacity, and sustainability preferences, but are insensitive to investment horizon. Nevertheless, they find that historical risk-adjusted performance is on par with professionally managed benchmark portfolios.
与现有文献不同，我们进行了一项实时实验来检查 ChatGPT 的一个版本，该版本可以自主浏览互联网并权衡所获得的信息与其响应的相关性。我们探讨了 ChatGPT 在处理时间敏感信息和对股票进行排名方面的表现，这是投资者在众多投资选择中导航的常见做法。
Unlike the existing literature, we run a live experiment to examine a version of ChatGPT that autonomously browses the internet and weighs the relevance of obtained information for its responses. We explore ChatGPT's performance in processing time-sensitive information and ranking stocks, a common practice for investors navigating the vast array of investment options.
$\color{purple}{Panel\ A}$分析普通股全样本，$\color{purple}{Panel\ B}$分析纽约证券交易所小盘股，$\color{purple}{Panel\ C}$分析剩余非小型股； $\color{green}{更复杂的模型具有更高的夏普比}$；
参考文献 / References Dowling, M., & Lucey, B. (2023). ChatGPT for (finance) research: The Bananarama conjecture. Finance Research Letters, 53, 103662. Fieberg, C., Hornuf, L., & Streich, D. (2023). Using GPT-4 for financial advice. Available at SSRN 4488891.已下架。 Niszczota, P., & Abbas, S. (2023). GPT has become financially literate: Insights from financial literacy tests of GPT and a preliminary test of how people use it as a source of advice. Finance Research Letters, 58, 104333. Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A. N., … & Polosukhin, I. (2017). Attention is all you need. Advances in neural information processing systems, 30. Cucchiara, R. (2023). What large language models like GPT can do for finance. https://www.nature.com/articles/d43978-023-00095-8. Hirshleifer, D., & Teoh, S. H. (2003). Limited attention, information disclosure, and financial reporting. Journal of Accounting and Economics, 36(1-3), 337-386. Lipton, Z. C., & Steinhardt, J. (2019). Troubling Trends in Machine Learning Scholarship: Some ML papers suffer from flaws that could mislead the public and stymie future research. Queue, 17(1), 45-77.

entry.title: Tool | OpenClaw: The Open-Source AI Agent That Broke GitHub Records
entry.content:
Why OpenClaw Matters 2026 年初，一个名为 OpenClaw 的开源项目以前所未有的速度席卷了 GitHub。60 天内，Stars 从 9,000 飙升到 282,000——超越了 Ollama（162K）、Dify（130K）等老牌项目，成为 GitHub 历史上增长最快的仓库。
这不仅仅是数字上的胜利。OpenClaw 代表了 AI 工具的一个范式转移：从"你问我答"的聊天机器人，到"你设定目标，我持续执行"的自主智能体。
传统的 AI 助手——无论是 ChatGPT、Claude 还是 Gemini——都是被动反应式的：你发一条消息，它回一条消息，对话结束后一切归零。OpenClaw 不同，它具备三个关键能力：
跨会话记忆：记住上下文，不会"失忆" 主动执行：可以定时触发任务，不需要你每次手动提问 多平台集成：连接你已经在用的所有消息渠道
这意味着你可以让 OpenClaw 每天早上 8 点整理你的邮件摘要发到 Telegram，监控某个 GitHub 仓库的新 Issue 并自动回复，或者在你的 Slack 频道里充当一个 24 小时在线的技术助手——而这一切都运行在你自己的设备上。
In early 2026, an open-source project called OpenClaw swept through GitHub at an unprecedented pace. In 60 days, its stars surged from 9,000 to 282,000 — surpassing established projects like Ollama (162K) and Dify (130K) to become the fastest-growing repository in GitHub history.
This isn't just a numbers game. OpenClaw represents a paradigm shift in AI tooling: from "you ask, I answer" chatbots to "you set the goal, I keep executing" autonomous agents.
Traditional AI assistants — whether ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini — are reactively conversational: you send a message, it replies, and everything resets when the conversation ends. OpenClaw is different, possessing three critical capabilities:
Cross-session memory: Retains context without "amnesia" Proactive execution: Can trigger tasks on schedule without manual prompting Multi-platform integration: Connects to all the messaging channels you already use
This means you can have OpenClaw compile your email digest every morning at 8 AM and send it to Telegram, monitor a GitHub repository for new issues and auto-reply, or serve as a 24/7 technical assistant in your Slack channel — all running on your own hardware.
Architecture: The Local-First Design OpenClaw 的技术架构围绕一个核心理念构建：本地优先。
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ 你的设备 (macOS/Linux) ││ ││ ┌─────────┐ WebSocket ┌───────────┐ ││ │ OpenClaw│◄───────────────►│ Gateway │ ││ │ Agent │ :18789 │ Server │ ││ └────┬────┘ └─────┬─────┘ ││ │ │ ││ ┌────▼────┐ ┌──────▼──────┐ ││ │ LLM API │ │ Channels │ ││ │ or Local│ │ WhatsApp │ ││ │ (Ollama)│ │ Telegram │ ││ └─────────┘ │ Slack ... │ ││ └─────────────┘ │└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Gateway 层是整个系统的控制中枢，通过 WebSocket 连接所有组件：Agent 运行时、CLI 客户端、WebChat UI、macOS/iOS/Android 节点。每个消息渠道都是一个独立的连接器，支持群组隔离和消息路由。
模型选择极为灵活：
使用 OpenAI、Anthropic、Google 等云端 API 通过 Ollama 运行本地模型（完全离线） 支持模型自动回退：当主模型不可用时自动切换到备用模型
Skills 生态系统是 OpenClaw 的另一大亮点。ClawHub 技能注册中心已经有超过 5,700 个社区构建的技能，覆盖日历管理、代码审查、研究自动化等各种场景。你可以把 Skills 理解为 OpenClaw 版本的"App Store"。
OpenClaw's technical architecture is built around one core principle: local-first.
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ Your Device (macOS/Linux) ││ ││ ┌─────────┐ WebSocket ┌───────────┐ ││ │ OpenClaw│◄───────────────►│ Gateway │ ││ │ Agent │ :18789 │ Server │ ││ └────┬────┘ └─────┬─────┘ ││ │ │ ││ ┌────▼────┐ ┌──────▼──────┐ ││ │ LLM API │ │ Channels │ ││ │ or Local│ │ WhatsApp │ ││ │ (Ollama)│ │ Telegram │ ││ └─────────┘ │ Slack ... │ ││ └─────────────┘ │└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
The Gateway layer is the control hub, connecting all components via WebSocket: agent runtime, CLI clients, WebChat UI, macOS/iOS/Android nodes. Each messaging channel is an independent connector with group isolation and message routing.
Model selection is highly flexible:
Use cloud APIs from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc. Run local models via Ollama (fully offline) Automatic model failover: switches to backup models when the primary is unavailable
The Skills ecosystem is another highlight. The ClawHub skill registry has over 5,700 community-built skills covering calendar management, code review, research automation, and more. Think of Skills as OpenClaw's version of an "App Store."
Getting Started in 5 Minutes OpenClaw 的安装非常简单，但前提是你熟悉终端操作——这不是一个面向非技术用户的产品。
第一步：安装
npm install -g openclaw@latestopenclaw onboard --install-daemon
这会启动一个交互式引导流程，帮你配置 API 密钥和第一个消息渠道。
第二步：连接你的第一个渠道
以 Telegram 为例，你只需要在引导过程中输入 Bot Token（通过 @BotFather 获取），OpenClaw 就会自动连接。
第三步：开始对话
# 在命令行中直接对话openclaw agent --message "帮我总结今天 GitHub Trending 上的热门项目" --thinking high# 或者通过已连接的消息渠道发送消息openclaw message send --to your-telegram-chat --message "你好！"
系统要求：Node.js ≥ 22，macOS / Linux / Windows (WSL2)
费用：软件免费（MIT 许可证），费用来自 LLM API 调用。轻度使用每月几美元，重度使用取决于你选择的模型。使用 Ollama 本地模型则完全免费。
OpenClaw installation is straightforward, but it requires comfort with the terminal — this is not a product for non-technical users.
Step 1: Install
npm install -g openclaw@latestopenclaw onboard --install-daemon
This launches an interactive wizard that guides you through API key configuration and your first channel connection.
Step 2: Connect your first channel
For Telegram, simply enter your Bot Token (obtained via @BotFather) during onboarding, and OpenClaw connects automatically.
Step 3: Start chatting
# Chat directly from command lineopenclaw agent --message "Summarize today's trending GitHub projects" --thinking high# Or send via a connected channelopenclaw message send --to your-telegram-chat --message "Hello!"
Requirements: Node.js ≥ 22, macOS / Linux / Windows (WSL2)
Cost: Software is free (MIT license). Costs come from LLM API calls. Light usage runs a few dollars monthly; heavy usage depends on your chosen model. Using Ollama local models is completely free.
20+ Messaging Channels OpenClaw 支持的消息渠道之广令人印象深刻——这也是它快速走红的关键原因之一：
类别平台即时通讯WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, iMessage (BlueBubbles), LINE, Zalo团队协作Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Feishu (飞书), Mattermost开源/去中心化Matrix, IRC, Nostr, Nextcloud Talk其他Twitch, Synology Chat, Tlon, WebChat每个渠道都是独立的连接器模块，支持：
群组隔离：不同群组可以路由到不同的 Agent 配置 多 Agent 路由：一个 OpenClaw 实例可以运行多个 Agent，按渠道分配 激活模式：可以设置在群组中只响应 @ 提及，而在私聊中始终响应
对于中国用户来说，飞书的支持尤其值得关注——这意味着你可以在企业内部部署一个完全自主可控的 AI 助手。
The breadth of OpenClaw's messaging channel support is impressive — and a key reason for its rapid adoption:
CategoryPlatformsInstant MessagingWhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, iMessage (BlueBubbles), LINE, ZaloTeam CollaborationSlack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Feishu (Lark), MattermostOpen/DecentralizedMatrix, IRC, Nostr, Nextcloud TalkOtherTwitch, Synology Chat, Tlon, WebChatEach channel is an independent connector module supporting:
Group isolation: Different groups can route to different Agent configurations Multi-agent routing: A single OpenClaw instance can run multiple agents, assigned by channel Activation modes: Respond only to @ mentions in groups, always respond in DMs
For Chinese users, Feishu (Lark) support is particularly noteworthy — this means you can deploy a fully self-controlled AI assistant within your organization.
OpenClaw vs. Cloud AI Assistants OpenClaw 与传统云端 AI 助手（ChatGPT、Claude、Gemini）之间的区别，不是"谁更聪明"——它们可以使用完全相同的底层模型。根本区别在于交互范式：
云端 AI 助手OpenClaw交互模式你发消息 → AI 回复AI 持续运行，主动+被动执行记忆对话结束即遗忘跨会话持久记忆触发方式手动输入手动 + 定时 + 事件驱动渠道专用网页/App你已有的所有消息平台数据位置云端服务器你的本地设备可扩展性有限（插件/GPTs）5,700+ 社区技能成本控制订阅制按 API 调用付费，或本地模型免费换句话说，ChatGPT 是一个你去访问的网站，而 OpenClaw 是一个住在你设备上的智能体——它不等你来找它，而是主动为你工作。
The difference between OpenClaw and traditional cloud AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) isn't "who's smarter" — they can use the exact same underlying models. The fundamental difference lies in the interaction paradigm:
Cloud AI AssistantsOpenClawInteractionYou message → AI repliesAI runs continuously, proactive + reactiveMemoryForgets after conversationPersistent cross-session memoryTriggersManual input onlyManual + scheduled + event-drivenChannelsDedicated web/appAll your existing messaging platformsData locationCloud serversYour local deviceExtensibilityLimited (plugins/GPTs)5,700+ community skillsCost controlSubscriptionPay-per-API-call, or free with local modelsIn other words, ChatGPT is a website you visit, while OpenClaw is an agent living on your device — it doesn't wait for you to come to it, it proactively works for you.
The Backstory OpenClaw 的故事本身就是一段传奇。
项目由 Peter Steinberger 创建，早期经历了多次更名——从 Moltbot 到 Clawdbot，最终定名为 OpenClaw。社区至今仍亲切地称这个 AI 为"Molty"。
2026 年 1 月底，OpenClaw 因 Moltbook 项目的病毒式传播而爆红，72 小时内就获得了 68,000 个 GitHub Stars。此后增长从未停止。
2026 年 2 月 14 日，Steinberger 宣布加入 OpenAI，并承诺将项目移交给一个开源基金会管理。这个决定引发了社区的热烈讨论——有人担心 OpenAI 的介入会影响项目的独立性，也有人认为这恰恰证明了 OpenClaw 的技术价值。
无论如何，OpenClaw 已经是 MIT 许可证下的开源项目。即使未来的方向发生变化，社区随时可以 fork 并继续发展。这正是开源的力量所在。
OpenClaw's story is a legend in itself.
Created by Peter Steinberger, the project went through multiple name changes — from Moltbot to Clawdbot, finally settling on OpenClaw. The community still affectionately calls the AI persona "Molty."
In late January 2026, OpenClaw went viral through the Moltbook project, gaining 68,000 GitHub stars in just 72 hours. Growth never stopped.
On February 14, 2026, Steinberger announced he would be joining OpenAI and committed to transferring the project to an open-source foundation. This decision sparked lively community debate — some worried about OpenAI's influence on the project's independence, while others saw it as validation of OpenClaw's technical value.
Regardless, OpenClaw is an MIT-licensed open-source project. Even if future direction shifts, the community can always fork and continue development. That's the power of open source.
Who Should Use OpenClaw? OpenClaw 不是给所有人的。它最适合以下场景：
适合你，如果你： 需要一个 7×24 小时运行 的 AI 助手，而不是按需对话 想在 多个消息平台 上统一使用 AI，而不是切换不同的应用 关心 数据隐私，希望一切运行在自己的设备上 会使用终端，愿意花 30 分钟做初始配置 想要 自动化工作流（定时任务、事件监控、邮件处理）
不适合你，如果你： 只是偶尔问 AI 几个问题——直接用 ChatGPT 更方便 不熟悉命令行操作 不愿意管理自己的基础设施（API 密钥、服务器运行等）
OpenClaw 的核心价值不是"更好的对话"，而是"持续运行的自动化"。如果你把 AI 仅仅当作一个问答工具，那么 OpenClaw 对你来说是过度设计。但如果你想让 AI 真正成为一个不知疲倦的数字助手，OpenClaw 目前是开源领域最成熟的选择。
项目地址：
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw
OpenClaw isn't for everyone. It's best suited for these scenarios:
Good fit if you: Need a 24/7 running AI assistant, not just on-demand conversations Want to use AI across multiple messaging platforms without switching apps Care about data privacy and want everything running on your own hardware Are comfortable with the terminal and willing to spend 30 minutes on initial setup Want workflow automation (scheduled tasks, event monitoring, email processing)
Not for you if you: Only occasionally ask AI a few questions — ChatGPT is more convenient Are unfamiliar with command-line operations Don't want to manage your own infrastructure (API keys, running services, etc.)
OpenClaw's core value isn't "better conversations" but "continuous automation." If you treat AI merely as a Q&A tool, OpenClaw is overengineered for your needs. But if you want AI to truly become a tireless digital assistant, OpenClaw is currently the most mature choice in the open-source space.
Repository:
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw
